2.B.3 Records and Reporting 
 
2.B.3.a  The MCMC shall input all Maintenance and Maintenance Management data into AIS with a minimum accuracy rate of 95%. The data must conform to the Marine Corps' established standards for formatting and quality to maintain consistent, reliable, and compliant records. The accuracy of data entered will be evaluated and calculated through 
the following process: 
 
(i)  The Maintenance Management Administrative Records retrieved from the AIS will be used to conduct regular Quality Assurance (QA) inspections.  These inspections will focus on verifying the completeness, correctness, and reliability of the data entered by the MCMC.  (ii) The data accuracy rate will be calculated based on a statistically representative sample of records reviewed.  For example, in a sample of 100 records, if 5 different records contain errors, the accuracy rate with be calculated as follows:  100 records reviewed – 5 records with errors = 95 accurate records.  
 
2.B.3.b  The MCMC shall conduct weekly validation/reconciliations between maintenance management , supply, and maintenance  IAW MCO 4790.2 (Chapter 1 4.b; Appendix A 3.t) and CmdO 4790.1B.  During these weekly validations and reconciliations, all open SRs, along with their associated tasks and parts, shall be reviewed.  The MCMC shall submit a Reconciliation Schedule (CDRL 1011) to the Government NLT 21 business days after contract award and thereafter no later than the fifth business day in July.     MCPP -N Specific (2.B.3.b).  The MCMC Norway section shall participate in 
weekly validations and reconciliations via Teams or Teleconference with the NDLO/MEB and the Norway Branch.  The MCMC shall maintain detailed records of all reconciliations to includ e actions required to be taken.

SECTION 2.C  BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE & KEY PERFORMANCE 
INDICATORS  (KPIs)   
 
This section identifies the business systems and deliverables by which much of the contract 
performance rating for Program Management Effectiveness (PME) is derived.  It is through these systems that the Command will gather the Key Performance Indicators ( KPIs) that will inform 
the Command on the state of the MCMC’s program management.  The basis of these KPIs are values associated with cost (Cost CLINs), schedule, and product/service conformance.  It is these KPIs that are applied to the three missions:  P roduction (MMC), Sustainment Operations, and 
OCONUS Operations that comprise an overall empirical MCMC PME rating.     It is important to understand the distinction between conformance and performance.  Where “conformance” values whether a product or service meets specific tolerances at the component/service level (a.k.a. acceptable quality level ), “performance” values how well all parts are functioning as an integrated whole so that they are performing in the manner in which the entire system was intended to perform.  When applying this concept to program management effectiveness this means affirming that the services the  government receives are being effectively 
managed such that the highest possible conformance/performance is obtained at the lowest risk and lowest cost in three mission areas (Production (MMC), Sustainment Operations, and OCONUS Operations).     
Additionally, the MCMC is responsible for developing and implementing local procedures (IAW 
International Standards Organization 9001, CmdO P5000.11, Management Procedures, andWork Instructions (ISOs)) that delineate those processes used to provide timely and accurate data within the defined systems of this section.  Using the full spectrum of business systems identified and referenced herein, the MCMC is responsible for fully and accurately entering data into those systems thereby enabling cyclical assessment of program management performance in as near real -time as p racticable.

 (v)  The management effectiveness (ME) of the MCMC shall be valued in three mission areas:  Production Operations (MMC), Sustainment Operations, and OCONUS Operations; and, in the three KPIs, Cost (Cost CLINs), Schedule, Conformance.  The value of the ME performance is based on the ME scale at SOW Attachments C.2.C -3a through C.2.C -3b where - 18 is the lowest possible 
score (Unacceptable) and +18 is the highest possible score (Exceptional).  Details of this rating can be found in CmdO 5201.1.  Variance in ME  KPIs is evaluated 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 99 of 425 
 
 against those agreed to in the ships’ kick-off meeting.  Variance in ME KPIs for 
Sustainment Operations is evaluated against KPIs established by contract quality and schedule requirements.  The MCMC is not required to be Earned Value Management (EVM) certified, however, EVM concepts and principles must be included in the methodology and techniques used to manage associated cost(s) (Cost CLINs) and schedule(s).
     
 
2.C.1.c  Global Combat Support System - Marine Corps (GCSS -MC) .  The Government 
will provide software and systems update training for program wide use of this software application.  The MCMC shall use GCSS- MC as the Enterprise Resource Planning 
system for all retail supply and field -level maintenance requirements in the execution of 
logistics functions in support of the MCPP.  Similar to Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS), a widely used ERP system in commercial industries for supply chain management, maintenance management, and human resource functions, GCSS- MC integrates various 
logistics, financial, and maintenance functions into a unified platform specifically designed for the Marine Corps.  The MCMC shall utilize GCSS-MC to record and report all maintenance and supply efforts IAW MCO 4790.2 and CmdO 4790.1.  The eight (8) functional areas for recording and reporting are: maintenance management, maintenance administration, training, records and reports, PMCS/CM, equipment availability, publications control, supply support and maintenance programs.  The Marine Corps Users Manual 4000-125 contain all the technical requirements associated with GCSS-
MC.  The Objective of these Marine Corps User’s Manuals is to provide user 
documentation on functional procedures for executing supply and maintenance in the 
logistics continuum in both garrison and contingency/combat environments. This manual outlines those tasks required in utilizing GCSS-MC for retail supply accounts and maintenance activities at all levels.  This manual is instructive to all personnel who must make reference to the GCSS -MC ERP tool; and this manual is to serve as a guide for 
training and educating personnel thereby reducing the impact during personnel turnover as it establishes a standard, repeatable, and auditable process common to all Marine Corps activities.  
 
2.C.1.d  Quality Management Support System (QMSS).  The MCMC shall utilize the 
most current and approved Quality Management Support System (QMSS) software provided by the Government (currently MCPIC IPRIME Inspection Web and eQMS) for recording and responding to all quality related non- conformities intro duced into the 
system IAW CmdO P5000.11. The Government will provide software licensing and initial as well as systems update training for program wide use of this software application.  Specific policies go verning the operation and administration of the BICmd 
use of the QMSS as a program wide KPI monitoring and measuring software can be found in CmdO P5000.11. 
 
(i)  QMSS KPI reporting is used by Blount Island Command (BICmd) and the MCMC in order to increase the efficiency, effectiveness, and repeatability of regular status reporting and performance reviews for each Maritime Prepositioning Ship (MPS) Maintenance Cycle (MMC) in accordance with CmdO 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP

 5201.1 andCmdO 5202.1.  This establishes regular/frequent group 
communications and reviews that both BICmd and MCMC personnel can rely on to provide an opportunity to address issues, solve problems, and maintain awareness of the status of MMC production.  The QMSS KPI dashboard has six parts that are intended to provide information on three specific Program Management areas – cost (Cost CLINs), schedule, and conformance.  The six parts are as follows:  
 
(a)  Quality Control Plan (QCP).  The QCP is as described in Section C.2.D. and contains the KPIs that describe the state of the MCMC’s quality control program.   
 
(b) Risk Control Plan (RCP).  The RCP is as described in Section C.2.D. and contains the KPIs that describe the state of the MCMC’s risk control program. 
 
(c) Nonconformance Report (NCR).  The MCMC’s requirement to respond to NCRs is as described in Section C.2.D and CmdO P5000.11.  (d) Nonconformance Trend (NCT).  The MCMC’s requirement to respond to NCTss is as described in Section C.2.D and CmdO P5000.11.   
 
(e)  End -of Ship MCMC Performance Review.  The MCMC’s requirement 
to respond to and participate in these End- of Ship MCMC Performance 
Reviews is as described in Section C.2.D and CmdO P5000.11,.  (f) Quarterly Sustainment MCMC Performance Review.  The MCMC’s requirement to respond to and participate in the Quarterly Sustainment MCMC Performance Review is as described in Section C.2.D and CmdO P5000.11, Risk and Performance.

system. It is the Accountable Property System of Record (APSR).  DPAS contains three

Defense Medical Logistics Support System (DMLSS).  The Government will

 DML -ES portfolio provides a continuum of medical logistics support for the Defense 
Health Agency. DMLSS supports all medical logistics functions in the Military Health 
System.  DMLSS delivers an automated and integrated information system with a comprehensive range of medical logistics management functions. It is a local server-based application that supports medical logistics functions internal to a military treatment facility a nd War Reserve Management sites. DMLSS supports all local medical logistics 
business practices including catalog research and purchase decisions, customer inventory management, medical inventory management, biomedical equipment maintenance, property management, and assemblage management.  DMLSS is currently undergoing a technical ref resh.

command and control support information system, used for Navy maintenance and logistics management.  NTCSS is the Navy’s program of record for management of ships, submarines, aviation squadrons, and intermediate maintenance activiti es (afloat 
and ashore). NTCSS was developed to merge 3 legacy programs:  Shipboard Non-Tactical Automated Data Processing Program (SNAP), Naval Aviation Logistics Command Management Information System (NALCOMIS), and Maintenance Resource Management System (MRMS).  
  
(i)  NTCSS provides ship and aviation maintenance management, inventory management, supply and financial business management, administration, and personnel management to Navy and Marine Corps operational forces, afloat and ashore.

OMMS-NG Tracking assists personnel in the planning, scheduling, and reporting of maintenance and related logistics support activities.  This application interfaces with other applications in the NTCSS suite including RSupply.  MCMC shall utilize this application in accordance with the direction provided in the Navy Section C.6.  There is a comprehensive imbedded User Guide within the application. SECTION 2.D RISK AND PERFORMANCE  
 
This requirement sets forth the responsibilities of the MCMC for: a) establishment and management of a Quality Control (QC)/Risk Control (RC) program that meets requirements for continued International Organization of Standards (ISO) 9001 certification (currently 9001:2015) with the MCMC included in the scope of the certification; b) use of the government provided quality management support system (QMSS) software, (currently MCPIC IPrime Inspection Web and eQMS modules), used for conducting and capturing quality control inspections, tracking, tracing, trending, and resolution of non- conforming products and services as well as 
managing audits and corrective; c) consideration and compliance with  the Command’s Quality Manual (CmdO P5000.11).  These requirements are program wide and cover home station operations as well as operations overseas and afloat on the prepositioning ships.  The MCMC shall adhere to all requirements identified within the SOW to the standards and quality levels identifie d, however, critic al services are described in the Performance Requirement Summary 
(SOW Attachment 2.D -1). 
 The MCMC shall create internal quality and risk control policies and procedures to provide effective management and cost control (Cost CLINs) of the processes used to realize the products and services of the MCPP contract. These policies shall be compliant  with, and 
supportive of, the  Command’s ISO 9001:2015 certification.  
 The MCMC shall dedicate sufficient QC/RC Program Quality Inspectors (QI) and Process Control Analysts (PCA) with appropriate knowledge of the specific equipment, supply and maintenance administration required to effectively conduct quality control of MCPP military equipment.  The MCMC shall include these personnel in the PPSP identified in paragraph 1.H.2.b of this document.  The QIs and PCAs shall report directly to an independent Quality Manager (QM) and be independent of the maintenance and production ef forts.  QC/RC Program 
QI, PCA and QM, as agreed upon during the semi -annual PPSP, will be designated by name to 
support MCPP quality requirements as these individuals will be the primary focal points for government COR, COTR and QAR interactions.  
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 105 of 425 
 
  
The Command’s ISO certification requirement is a joining of the two aspects of quality; quality control and quality assurance; where the MCMC has quality control responsibilities, and the government has quality assurance responsibilities.  By extension this concept also applies to program risk identification and mitigation.  Because proper program management oversight and control of quality and risk requires a holistic symbiotic relationship between quality control/risk control and quality assurance/risk assurance a comprehensive joined program must exist.  It is for this reason the requirement for ISO 9001 exists; and, moreover, the requirement to be  certified in ISO 9001:2015 compliance with the MCMC included in the certification scope.   The QMS utilizes the MCPIC IPrime Inspection Web and eQMS modules to record the sum of the QMS activities.  Within 60 calendar days of contract award a three -day government-provided 
training on the systems use/utility for the MCMC’s quality control team will take place.  
 
2. D.1 QUALITY CONTROL REQUIREMENTS  
 
2. D.1.a The MCMC shall provide Quality Control of products and services IAW FAR 52.246-11, ISO 9001:2015, and CmdO P5000.11. 
 
(i) IAW CmdO P5000.11, the MCMC shall use the Government provided QMSS to conduct, capture and record all quality control inspections, internally track, trend, trace, and document resolution of quality control/assurance non-conforming product(s)/service(s) as well as responding to non- conformance findings 
discovered by external agents. 
 
(ii) The MCMC shall respond to Government QA non-conformances initiated in the QMSS; i.e., Nonconformance Reports (NCRs) and Nonconformance Trends (NCTs).  Official communications in response to non-conformances initiated by the Government QAs shall be entered into the QMSS.  The MCMC shall acknowledge non-conformance findings within 1 work day by either accepting defects and assigning tasks or by disputing t he defect..  The MCMC shall resolve 
non-conformance by the due date on the NCR or NCT or request and be approved for due date extension.   
 
2. D.1.b  The MCMC shall develop and submit a draft Risk and Performance Policy in alignment with CmdO P5000.11 within 30 calendar days of contract award and submit a final review for Government approval within 60 calendar days of contract award in order to maintain continuity of risk and quality operations between Government and MCMC programs in support of the Command’s ISO 9001 certification. The approved policy is subject to validation by an accredited (American National Accreditation Board (ANAB) or Reg istrar Accreditation Board (RAB) third party auditor for ISO 9001:2015 
certification and/or surveillance every six months (semi-annually) thereafter to remain ISO 9001:2015 certified. The MCMC shall be included in the scope of the Command’s ISO 9001 certification.   
 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 106 of 425 
 
 (i)  The MCMC shall be subject to a government analysis and assessment of the 
Quality and Risk Control Plan (Q&RCP) draft for government review within 30 calendar days of contract award and for final review within 60 calendar days of contract award.  The Q &RCP shall comply with ISO 9001:2015, CmdO 
P5000.11, and shall contain the following:  quality control and risk performance levels for each government defined contract deliverable as well as internal Key Performance Indicators for the Q&RCP.  The MCMC shal l update the Q&RCP 
such that it passes the semi- annual 3
rd Party Audits and recertification; all 
Q&RCP revisions shall  be submitted to the Government for review and approval. 
 
(ii)  The MCMC shall be subject to a government analysis and assessment of the Quality Key Performance Indicator (KPI) control charts on a quarterly basis for all internal quality control elements used to support the quality control plan portion of the Q&R CP.  The Quality KPI Charts shall be provided IAW CmdO 
P5000.11 and CmdO 5201.1.     
2.D.1.c  The MCMC shall participate in the Ship’s Kick -Off briefing IAW CmdO 
P5000.11, and CmdO 5201.1.  Participation is required to present the projected cost, schedule, conformance, and risk baseline for each ship’s MMC.   
 
2.D.1.d  The MCMC shall participate in End- of-Ship (EOS)/ Period of Performance 
MCMC performance review IAW CmdO P5000.11.  Participation is required to review cost, schedule, conformance and risk variations from those agreed to at the Ship’s Kick-Off brie fing as well as review of lessons learned that may be executed as CPI initiatives 
moving forward.   The MCMC shall provide specific details for why KPIs covering cost, schedule, conformance and risk were different from those agreed to at the Ship’s Kick-Off Briefing.  
 
2.D.1.e  The MCMC shall maintain an annual ISO 9001:2015 Internal Audit Program IAW  9001:2015 and CmdO P5000.11..   The MCMC shall submit, for government 
approval, a rolling three -year audit schedule NLT the 2
nd week in September for the next 
fiscal year.  The audit schedule shall be designed in such a fashion that all applicable 
areas of the ISO 9001:2015 are audited at least once every three years and internal MCMC processes are audited as well.  Upon approval by the government, the internal audit schedule shall be added by the MCMC Quality Manager to the MCPIC IPRIME eQMS Audit Module. Internal audits shall be conducted according to the annually approved Audit Schedule.  The MCMC shall provide updates regarding the internal audit schedule at each Quality  Meeting.  
 
2.D.1.f The MCMC shall report on Continuous Process Improvement (CPI) of the Quality Management System as well areas of quality performance gaps/trends IAW CmdO P5000.11.  The MCMC is expected to use industry best practices for data collection, analysis and seek Government approval for implementation (when required) for CPI.  The MCMC shall provide a CPI summary brief at each ISO 9001 Quality Management Review occurring ever y six months.  
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 107 of 425 
 
  
2.D.1.g  The MCMC shall provide a Quality Control Plan (QCP) no later than 01 November on an annual basis.  The QCP details how the MCMC intends to provide surveillance and verification of the performance requirements contained within the SOW. The QCP shall include, but not be limited to, the following:  
(i) Contain procedures for written and verbal communication with the Government regarding performance of the contract  
(ii) Contain clear, measurable, and acceptable levels of performance  
(iii) Contain procedures for preventing defects and deficiencies, early detection of problems, and handling corrective actions without dependence upon Government intervention 
(iv) Contain surveillance procedures for each service to be monitored under this SOW. These procedures shall identify the list of items under surveillance, who will perform the surveillance, the frequency and method of surveillance, and procedures for correctio n of deficiencies.  
(v) Include a system to investigate and resolve customer complaints forwarded by the Government. The investigation results shall be documented and forwarded to the Government to include immediate corrective actions. 
(vi)   Files shall be maintained and be available to the COR upon request.  
 
 
 
SECTION C.3 – MAINTENANCE ACTIVITIES  
3.A INTRODUCTION  
This section delineates the requirements for equipment maintenance services to be accomplished by the MCMC during the MPF Maintenance Cycle (MMC) in support of Marine Corps ground Military Equipment (ME) and Navy Civil Engineer Support Equipment (CESE) required for Maritime Prepositioning Ships (MPS), MCPP -N, MCPP -PHIL, and future ashore site(s) per 
MCO 3000.17 and TM 4790- 14/2.  The primary mission of the overall maintenance effort  is to 
identify, plan, perform, and provide effective maintenance solutions for MPS, MCPP -N, MCPP -
PHIL and future ashore site military equipment, in accordance with applicable Marine Corps orders and applicable technical publications for the respective equipment. The secondary mission is to identify and report negative trends found with MCPP ME and CESE assets. The MCMC shall submit official correspondence to the Government identifying the negative trend(s) and the recommended corrective actions necessary t o prevent the negative trend from re -occurring in the 
future.  The Government will review and provide approval before the MCMC implements any recommended corrective action.  
 The diversity and quantity of Navy and Marine Corps ground military equipment covered under the scope of this section is extensive. Consequently, it is impractical to provide a complete listing of all required publications. Equipment associated technical publications required to support the scope of work identified in this section shall be determined by the equipment’s unique 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 108 of 425 
 
 Identification Number (ID) and levels of authorized maintenance identified herein.  To obtain 
equipment ID numbers, the MCMC shall cross- reference the National Stock Number (NSN) 
found on the equipment’s data plate with the Marine Corps Stock list of Appli cations Lists (SL 
6-1/6-2) located in the electronic Federal Logistics Data Catalog (FEDLOG).   Equipment TAMCN and ID numbers are used on the Marine Corps Technical Publications website (TDM-Publications) to obtain a current listing of authorized technical publications (TIs, SIs, TMs, MAMs, SOUMs, SL -3s, ECPs, and DPs) that support each unique item of equipment.  
 Maintenance services shall be performed in accordance with MCMC -developed and 
Government-approved production and delivery schedules.  Maintenance services (preventative and corrective maintenance, modifications, calibration, special function tests, certification of tactical fuel system, battery services, load test certification, fuel filtration service, corrosion control, SL-3 inventory, shelflife and service- life management, limited technical inspection, 
equipment configuration, and care of supplies in storage [COSIS]) shall conform to the specifications described within the SOW.  MCMC Quality Control (QC) and Government Quality Assurance (QA) measures will be governed by the performance requirements and standards associated with applicable sections of the SOW and MMSOP.  
3.A.1  MAINTENANCE REQUIREMENTS   
The maintenance requirements listed in this section apply to all MCPP ME and Navy CESE assets, to include equipment received as a result of equipment modernization programs, stock rotation programs, and any additions to the Prepositioning Objective and/or equipment set(s).   Refer to Section C.6 for Navy CESE specific maintenance requirements.  The term "equipment” used herein refers to Class II and Class VII ME and CESE assets in support of each individual MPS, MCPP- N, MCPP -PHIL, and future ashore site(s).  To ensure an effective maintenance 
effort and appropriate level of equipment readiness, the MCMC shall ensure that requirements outlined in other appropriate sections within the Statement of Work (SOW) are met. Maintenance requirements listed in this sec tion shall be identified and captured as individual 
tasks on an appropriate GCSS-MC Service Request IAW CmdO 4790.1_ (MMSOP). Maintenance activities may be required at other locations/off -load sites and/or during other 
periods/occasions and will be identified and negotiated by the Government when required.  
3.A.1.a The MCMC shall perform Limited Technical Inspections (LTIs) on ME and CESE assets IAW  TM 4700-15/1, applicable equipment TMs, TIs, MCO 4400.201 and 
UM 4000-125.  LTIs shall be completed to determine equipment overall condition during the equipment maintenance acceptance phase, or when requesting disposition instructions and equipment investigation actions for ME and CESE assets.  Completed LTIs shall contain an assigned condition code in accordance TM 4700-15/1. LTIs associated with investigations and beyond economic repair, shall contain a repair cost estimate (labor and materials) and assign a condition code in accordance with TM 4700-15/1_ and MCO 4400.201.  The MCMC shall perform an equipment road/operational test during the LTI effort associated with wheeled (including medium and heavy trailers) and tracked vehicles, unless restricted by local base operation regulations.

 3.A.1.b The MCMC shall perform special function tests on ME and CESE assets.  In 
order to mitigate the need for equipment disassembly or unpackaging to support the Governments Quality Assurance (QA) functions,

 3.A.18  MCPP -PHIL Specific Maintenance Requirements:  
 
3.A.18.a  PMCS on PO assets. Throughout all stages of the MCPP -PHIL effort, MCMC 
shall follow proper PMCS procedures to monitor the PO assets’ condition and maintain the items in FMC condition.  Government approval shall be sought prior to storing PO asset s outdoors due to space constraints.  Annually, one -third of equipment stored indoors 
shall have an Annual PMCS performed, to include all services leading up to and including the Annual PMCS IAW applicable Technical Manuals.  The remaining two-thirds of equipment stored indoors shall receive a Monthly PMCS IAW applicable Technical Manuals on an Annual basis.  All equipment stored indoors shall receive 90-day COSIS to assess the equipment’s condition.  If defects are found during COSIS, the MCMC shall schedu le and complete corrective actions IAW Orders and Directives.  Any 
PO asset stored outdoors shall receive 30- day COSIS to ensure that the equipment is 
functional.  Special PMCS procedures are necessary when equipment has been exposed to harsh environments (e.g., salt water, fresh water, operations conducted in loose sand or mud).  This section sets forth the requirements and responsibilities of the MCMC for supply, container, and distribution management in support of the Marine Corps Prepositioning Program (MCPP) contract.  The MCMC shall conduct efficient, effective, and policy- compliant supply, container, 
and distribution operations necessary to provide combat-ready assets, equipment sets, supplies, and sustainment blocks to Operating Forces in support of worldwide Marine Corps Prepositioning Program operations and exercises, while maintaining maximum materiel accountability.  The MCMC shall execute supply chain management, inventory management, container management, and distribution management in accordance with standards defined in this section and all applicable Marine Corps Orders and directives, Department of Defense guidance and directives, federal, state and local laws and regulations, industry standards, and other technical documentation.  Department of Defense, Department of the Navy, and Marine Corps orders, manuals, instructions, and technical directives provide policy, procedures, and guidance, except where modified by this contract to meet unique prepositioning program requirements.  The comprehensive responsibilities of the MCMC outlined in section C.4 include demand planning and forecasting;  monitoring and identification of materiel requirements (relative to Government-established equipment and supply allowances);  identification an d sourcing of 
materiel shortfalls required to accomplish the MCPP mission (with Government approval);  identification and disposal of excess, obsolete, and unserviceable materiel (with Government approval);  data management and file retention;  document ma nagement;  inventory 
management, to include accountability, sub-custody procedures, physical inventories, property record adjustments (with Government approval), warehousing, stock rotation, care of supplies in storage (COSIS), and shelf life management;  container management and accountability, to 
include updates to property records within an AIS, inspection, repairs, and re-
certification;  preservation, packing, packaging and marking (P3&M) of materiel;  distribution and transportation management operations;  and implementation and execution of a robust quality control program.  
The MCMC shall manage the following supply accounts:  intermediate level accounts, to include a General Account (GA), Maintenance Floats/Reparable Issue Points (RIP) and associated sub -
floats;  multiple retail/consumer level accounts and sub -custody accounts associated with 
on-
island MMC operations, Maritime Prepositioning Ships (MPS), current ashore prepositioning sites , (MCPP -N 
and MCPP-PHIL), and future ashore sites;  and on- island accounts associated with General 
Equipment (Garrison Property and non- tactical Non -Standard Equipment [N- SE]) and 
Government Furnished Equipment (GFE).  The list of supply accounts, associated DODAACs, descriptions of materiel within the respective accounts, and purpose for the accounts can be found at SOW Attachment C.4 -1.

4.A.  SUPPLY DEMAND PLANNING AND MONITORING OF MATERIEL

 4.A.1 The MCMC shall monitor and track Military Equipment (ME) excesses and deficiencies.  The Table of Equipment (T/E) within TFSMS provides established allowances for the organic Port T/E account (M92502).  While the Prepositioning Objective (PO) contained in the NAVMC 2907 provides established allowances for the MPF (MMV333), Future Ashore (MMV222), MCPP -N (MMV420), and MCPP-PHIL (MMV777) accounts, the MCMC shall use the 
“spreads,” which are based on the PO and identified in the detailed plans within MCP IC, as the 
basis for equipment requirements across each of the ships/ashore sites.  The exception is MCPP-N, which does not have a “spread” identified in MCPIC but does have an established PO within the NAVMC 2907.  SOW Attachment C.4 -1 provides additional context regarding where the 
respective equipment requirements for each account are defined for the MCMC.    
4.A.1.a The MCMC shall provide a Monthly Excess/Deficiency Report (CDRL 4001) for the Non-Prepositioning / BICmd organic account (M92502), MCPP- PHIL account 
(MMV777), and MCPP-N account (MMV420), identifying excesses and deficiencies of ME relative to authorized allowances of each account NLT the 5
th business day of every 
month (Attention to: Supply Branch, MCPP-PHIL, and MCPP-N respectively).  The Maritime Prepositioning Force (MMV333) and Future Ashore (MMV222) accounts are exempt from this requirement due to requisitions not being submitted at the beginning of the ~42-month MMC, but rather prior to each respective production period within the MMC based on the equipment requirements specific for that production period.  The Monthly Excess/Deficiency Reports shall be a ccurate IAW the latest/current versions of 
MCO 4400.201 (vol 3, Chap 2), UM 4000-125 (Vol 2 & Vol 4), and the applicable account T/E or spread, as well as the Accountable Property Systems of Record (APSR) O/H and physical O/H quantities.   Reporting submission shall include data elements for 
TAMCN, NSN, nomenclature, Stores Account Code (SAC), O/H Quantity, Allowance / Authorized Quantity, Quantity Excess / Deficient, Unit Price, Extended Price of Excess / Deficiency, and Document Number with latest requisition status for deficiencies OR Service Request (SR) number associ ated with disposition requests for excesses, where 
applicable. In situations where excesses or deficiencies exist without corresponding requisitions or requests for disposition, the MCMC shall provide justification of excesses and deficiencies for each TAM CN to the Government.   
 
MCPP -N and MCPP -PHIL specific:  MCPP -N/MCPP -Phil Weekly Class of 
Supply report  (CDRL 4002 ( shall be provided weekly and shall extend beyond ME, 
to also include all classes of supply which possess a PO.  This report shall be

 4.A.2  The MCMC shall conduct demand based stock computations for consumable materials to be maintained in the (MMV200) General Account (GA) and provide recommended stocking levels to the Government on a quarterly basis via a Quarterly GA Recommended Stock Levels Report  (CDRL 5005)  IAW MCO P4400.151 (introduction and Chap 1), MCO 4400.201, and 
(Vol 3, Chap 2).  The report shall be provided to the Government for approval prior to the MCMC conducting GA replenishment buys.

Intermediate Storage Workbench (ISW) in the APSR.  GA inventory identified as “not stocked items” shall have required justifications for stocking in GA.

(shall only include "inventory type" for GA stocks), Acquisition Advice Code, TAMCN (if applicable), CEC, SAC, MOQ, Requirement Code, OP Code, Source of Supply, Qty of Hits in last 24 months (i.e., HITS 24), Qty of Demands in last 24 months (i.e, DMDS 24), as provided in the Intermediate Supply Workbench in the APSR.

4.B PROCUREMENT AND SOURCING  
 
4.B.1 Utilizing Government-provided funding, the MCMC shall procure and source SAC 1 and 3 Military Equipment IAW MCO 4400.201 (Vol 3 & Vol 6), UM 4000-125, TM 4790-14/2, and MCO 4400.16.  Prior to requisitioning from LOGCOM or DoD supply chain sources, the MCMC shall first utilize excess inventory from across the Marine Corps Prepositioning Program, processing equipment redistributions between respective BICmd DoDAACs to eliminate ME deficiencies.   Equipment redistributions between the respective DoDAACs s hall 
only occur with prior Government approval, as the Government remains the decision authority for prioritization of equipment sourcing between DoDAACs.  Additionally, while the MCMC creates the requisitions within GCSS -MC, the Government retains the “ap prover” role within 
GCSS -MC, avoiding the MCMC conduct of the Inherently Governmental Function (IGF) of 
obligating Government funds.    
4.B.1.a Specific to the MMV222 and MMV333 accounts, the MCMC shall submit 
requisitions for Military Equipment needed to meet MMC production requirements NLT 60 calendar days prior to start of the respective production periods, or 10 calendar days after receiving Government approval of “P-75 Attainment Deficiency Report,” whichever is later.  Requisitions shall be submitted IAW MCO 4400.201 (Vol 3, Chap 2) and be based on requirements identified in the respective Detailed Plans “spreads” for the production period.  
  4.B.1.b Specific to the Non-Prepositioning/BICmd organic (M92502), MCPP- PHIL 
(MMV777), and MCPP-N (MMV420) accounts, all ME T/E and PO allowances shall either be 
on hand, on order, or recorded as an unfunded deficiency (the latter applicable to SAC 1 only).  The MCMC shall submit requisitions within the APSR for all SAC 3 ME shortfalls within 30 calendar days of the shortfall emerging for Government approval.  For SAC 1 ME shortfalls, the 
MCMC shall either submit requisitions within 30 calendar days of the shortfall emerging or 
identify the shortfall on the applicable account’s Unfunded D eficiency L ist.  Requisitions shall 
be submitted IAW MCO 4400.201 (Vol 3, Chap 2) to the Government for review and approval. 
 
 4.B.2 The MCMC shall execute procurement and sourcing of SAC 1 and 3 SL- 3 assets IAW 
MCO 4400.201, MCO 4400.16, MCO 4790.2, UM 4000-125, CmdO 4790.1, equipment TMs, and SL-3 Components Lists.  Requisitioning of SL-3 shall be completed by the MCMC as deficiencies emerge and/or are identified.  The only exception to this requirement applies in cases where either newly fielded assets or legacy assets are received from wholesale level with SAC 1 SSR I deficiencies.  In the exception situation, upon MCMC notification to the 
Government, the Government will coordinate with LOGCOM Ground Equipment Asset Managers for sourcing of SSRI deficiencies.     4.B.3 The MCMC shall execute procurement and sourcing of demand-based consumables for the General Account (GA) IAW MCO P4400.151 (Chap 1), MCO 4400.201, MCO 4400.16 and UM 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 125 of 425 
 
 4000- 125.  Replenishment buys for the GA (MMV200) shall be conducted in batch on a 
quarterly basis following Government approval of the MCMC -proposed "Quarterly GA 
Recommended Stock Levels Report” (ref 4.A.3).  Individual replenishment purchases may occur 
on an as -needed basis between quarterly batch purchases with prior Government approval.  
 
4.B.4 The MCMC shall manage the sourcing of approved SECREPs for the main float (MMFAU1) account to the established allowance levels.  The sourcing solution shall be guided by enterprise -wide availability of stocks and associated opportunity for lateral support, as well as 
cost to repair relative to cost to requisition.  The criteria for selecting sourcing method shall be based on cost avoidance, with the least costly method being the first choice and the costliest method being the least desirable.  Typical ly, lateral support from Marine Corps -wide excess 
stocks at other RIPs is the least expensive, followed by SECREP “repair and return” via a Government -established contract, and finally requisition of a new SECREP from wholesale level 
stock or manufacturer with a potential exchange credit for the corresponding unserviceable 
(condition code F) SECREP being turned in.  
4.B.4.a The MCMC shall process SECREP repair and returns from the MMFAU1 or MMFAQ9 accounts IAW MCO 4400.200A, MCO P4400.151, MCO 4790.2, MCO 4400.201.  Repair and return requirements for subfloats shall be transferred to MMFAU1 for processing from the mai n RIP.  Prior to evacuating SECREPs to external or 
commercial maintenance facilities for repair/rebuild, the MCMC shall request and obtain Government approval.  Upon receiving Government approval, SECREPs within the MMFAU1 account that require testing and/ or repair shall be inducted into GCSS -MC by 
the MCMC for evacuation/shipment to a Government -designated third- party repair 
MCMC and facility.  The Government establishes, manages, and funds the third- party 
contract responsible for completing testing and re pairs of SECREPs. The MCMC shall 
request for repair and return approval from Government within 5 business days of physical receipt of unserviceable SECREP from supported commodity or sub- float.  The 
MCMC shall process shipment of unserviceable SECREP to the Government -designated 
repair facility within 10 calendar days of receiving Government approval to execute repair and return.  
 
4.B.4.b The MCMC shall utilize the Logistics Integrated Support (LIS) Triangle Project for “F” condition SECREP (s) managed by MPB.  The MCMC shall submit a request for an FTA Buy to the Government for approval, and upon approval, the MCMC shall ship the “F” condition SECREP(s) to MPB.  If in stock, MPB will ship an “A” condition back to the RIP.  If not in stock, it will be placed on backorder to be filled when stock is available.  
 4.B.4.c The MCMC shall coordinate and conduct SECREP lateral support with other Marine Corps RIPs and LOGCOM Centralized SECREP Management (CSM) office IAW MCO 4400.200A and UM 4000- 125 to eliminate MMFAU1 or MMFAQ9 
inventory deficiencies where correspondi ng enterprise excesses exist.  Review of the 
Global CAL will highlight available excesses at other RIPs to fill MMFAU1 deficiencies.  The MCMC shall regularly coordinate with other RIPS and the LOGCOM CSM to seek 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 126 of 425 
 
 opportunities for lateral support.  However, the MCMC shall request and obtain 
Government approval prior to completing lateral support transfers to/from MMFAU1, MMFAQ9, or future main RIPs.    The MCMC shall request lateral support approval from Government within 5 business days of identifying an inventory deficiency where excess enterprise inventory exists.  The MCMC shall process RIP lateral support transfers within 5 calendar days of receiving Government approval.  
 4.B.4.d The MCMC shall requisition Government -approved SECREPs IAW MCO 
P4400.151, MCO 4790.2, UM 4000- 125, and CAL allowances.  Requisitioning of 
SECREPs is the last option in cases where lateral support is not available and repair and return is uneconomical.  Government approval shall be obtained prior to obligation of funds to support SECREP requisitions.  Request(s) for requisit ion shall be provided to the 
Government within 5 business days of identification of deficiency when "repair and return" or later al sourcing from enterprise excesses are not available.  Subsequently, the 
MCMC shall submit the requisition within 5 business days of receiving Government approval.  
 
4.B.5 The MCMC shall conduct requisition and document management procedures for all MPF, ashore -based prepositioning, and organic accounts.  Requisition and document management 
includes all requisition, report management, required follow -on transactions, receipt, and issue 
procedures until ultimate issue to the customer or execution of related disposition actions.   
4.B.5.a The MCMC shall conduct requisition management IAW MCO 4400.201, MCO 
P4400.151, MCO 4400.16, MCO 4790.2, CmdO 4790.1 and UM 4000- 125.  Requisition 
management includes appropriate/adequate Requisition Submission Time for priority and Required Delivery Date (RDD); appropriate use of Urgency of Need Designator (UND), priority designators, RDD, Non- Mission Capable Supply (NMCS) indicator, Job Order 
Number (JON), advice code, and signal code.  Repair parts and maintenance related supplies shall be requisi tioned IAW time standards outlined in MCO 4400.16. 
 
(i) The MCMC shall submit GFE  Procurement  Requests  (CDRL 5016)  for non-
T/E allowance items IAW APSR property records, vendor/source of supply data, and appropriate market research.  GFE Requests (for non- consumable non- T/E 
allowance items) shall be submitted to Government as requirements emerge, and Government approval must be obtained prior to procurement.  Requests shall include, where applicable:  identification as either a replacement or new procurement item, nomenclature/description, procurement cost, proposed vendor, requesting commodity, SUC that property will be loaded to, and comprehensive justification.  Additional information may be required by the Government based off the unique nature of the item(s) requested in the GFE request.

4.B.8 The MCMC shall prepare and maintain appropriate supply administrative documentation for personnel conducting requisitioning and receiving functions IAW MCO 4400.201.  4.B.9 The MCMC shall execute the procurement of fuel from the DLA -E capitalized fuel facility 
IAW MCO 4400.201, and CMC msg 051307Z Apr 18.  MCPP -PHIL currently does not have a 
supporting DLA fuel facility; therefore, MCMC shall use locally approved DLA fuel vendor under the ODCCLIN (X006) to attain fuel to support prepositioning operations.  
 
4.B.9.a The MCMC shall maintain a Fuel Logbook for the purposes of issuing QR Codes (Quick Response Code) and utilize DD 1898- e for each fuel transaction.  MCMC shall 
ensure VIL keys are secured and inventoried daily.  MCMC shall reconcile the Fuel 
Logbook and completed DD 1898- e forms daily with the Organic Supply Officer or 
Delegated Individuals IAW CMC Msg 051307Z Apr 18 and MCO 4400.201 vol 5.  The MCMC will maintain DLA 1898 -e forms as KSDs (key supporting documents); retention 
period IAW MCO 4400.201.  4.B.9.b  The MCMC shall ensure the issuing and recovery of QR CodesVIL keys are conducted via a logbook. At a minimum, the logbook will contain the following:  
 (i)  Printed name and signature of the individual issued the card or key.  
 
(ii)  The QR card number issued.  (iii)  The date and time issued and returned.

4.C INVENTORY MANAGEMENT  
 4.C.1 The MCMC shall submit an annual Physical Inventory Control Plan (PICP) (CDRL 5018) IAW DLM 4000.25 (Chap 6), MCO 4400.201(Vol 4), and MARCORLOGCOMO 4440.1 PICP for all DoDAACs within the program.    The MCMC shall submit the PICP for Government approval NLT 30 calendar  days after the 
contract commences and shall update the plan annually NLT August 15 for follow-on annual/FY PICPs. The Physical Inventory Control Plan shall be comprehensive, providing inventory timelines and planned percentages to physically inventory for all supply accounts/AACs, to include intermediate level and consumer level inventories.  The planned frequency of physical inventories and percentage of stocks to be physically inventoried by category of property shall be IAW MCO 4400.201 and MARCORLOGCOMO 4440.1 PICP.  PICP results will be reported monthly to the Government.

  4.C.1.a The MCMC shall support Independent Public Accountant (IPA), Internal 
Controls and Audit Readiness Team (ICART), Logistics Command (LOGCOM), Field Supply and Maintenance Analysis Office (FSMAO), and other external agency requirements for existence and completeness (E&C) inventory testing, as required.  Inventory sample sizes and assigned dates are not known in advance, and they are directed from Higher Headquarters (HHQ) on a periodic basis to validate the accuracy of inventory and records.  Sample responses are generally required within a 5 -day timeframe and include, at a minimum, providing KSDs and 
pictures to validate possession and accuracy within the APSR.  Templates and timelines will be provided via separate correspondence as sample are reques ted by HHQ.   
 
4.C.2 The MCMC shall maintain accurate materiel accountability of all Government-owned ME and Government Furnished Property, utilizing the appropri ate APSR IAW  DoDI 5000.64, 
SECNAVINST 7320.10, MCO 4400.201, and UM 4000-125.  Accountability includes induction of proper supply transactions to document O/H inventory and provide an audit trail of supply actions taken from the time of requisition and receipt to disposition, while ensuring assets are transitioned without a lapse in accountability.  APSRs include GCSS -MC and DPAS.  DPAS 
shall be utilized for all Non -tactical vehicles (NTV) and Non -Standard Military Equipment.  
Examples of NTVs include all non- tactical, commercially available assets such as passenger 
vehicles, cargo vehicles, and non- tactical mater ial handling equipment. Additionally, DPAS is 
the designated APSR for SAC 1 CBRN equipment. The physical O/H inventory shall match the O/H inventory reflected in APSR and vice versa (described as “Existence and Completeness”).  Change of custody APSR trans actions shall be completed within one two (2) business day of 
physical custody change in the case of receipts, issues, redistributions, and disposals IAW MCO 4400.210 (vol 6) and UM 4400-125.  The Responsible Officer (for all APSRs) and Accountable Officer (only for GCSS -MC) shall be accurately reflected in APSR.  
 
4.C.2.a The MCMC shall submit draft GCSS -MC Item Master Organization (IMO)  Load 
Requests (CDRL 5019 ) IAW MCO 4400.201, CmdO 4790.1, and UM 4000-125 NLT 10 
business days of identification by either the Government or the MCMC.  MCMC shall submit draft requests, and upon concurrence, Government will forward to the appropriate approving authority.  IMO load requests are submitted in situations where assets are possessed by or introduced to the command that cannot be loaded to the G CSS-MC 
Install Base (IB) becau se they are not yet catalogued.   
 4.C.2.b The MCMC shall nominate, in writing, supply sub-custody Responsible Officers (ROs) within 30 days of contract commencing, and subsequently, NLT 5 business days after identification of a forthcoming RO change (RO departure or change of billet) for follow-on formal appointment by the Government RO.  Nomination Packages  
(CDRL5020) routed to the Government for approval and formal appointment shall include appointment letters, completed DD Form 577 (when applicable) and NAVMC 11869 documents (IAW MCO 4400.201). RO Nomination Letters shall be in accordance with Government provided format found at SOW Attachment C.4 -3.

 (i)  Ship Supervisors shall serve as the ROs for all respective shipboard SUCs 
within both the MPF (MMV333) and Non-Prepositioning/BICmd Organic (M92502) accounts.    (ii)  Specific to the non -shipboard SUCs within MMV222, MMV333, M92502, 
and MMV777, ROs shall be the (management- level) MCMC representatives who 
maintains physical accountability, custody and control of the asset.  In some situations, primarily associated with on- island SUCs, Government representatives 
maintain sub -custody of the assets, thereby requiring Government appointment of

In the case of Government representatives appointed as the RO, there is no requirement for a MCMC nomination, but the MCMC shall maintain copies of the CO-endorsed RO appointment letters for both MCMC and Government ROs.     (iii)  Specific to the MMV420 DoDAAC for the overseas MCPP-N account, Norwegian military representatives will serve as the RO with the exception of the YN03 (M92502) organic sub-custody account, the YKMIN (MMV420) KMI sub-custody account, and the YARM (MMV 420) Armory sub-custody account. MCMC shall serve as the RO for the SUCs associated with the on -island and

4.C.2.c The MCMC shall conduct physical inventories of ME to ensure accurate Existence and Completeness of consumer level retail accounts IAW DoDI 5000.64 and MCO 4400.201.  Consumer level accounts include M92502, MMV222, MMV333, MMV420, and MMV777.  
• At a minimum, ME shall be physically inventoried 100% on an annual basis IAW MCO 4400.201 (Vol 4). 
• MPF ME that is afloat is exempt from the 100% annual inventory, and 
instead, it shall be inventoried 100% during the MMC download of the respective MPF vessels.  This exemption is specific to prepositioning 
equipment (possesses a Prepositioning Objective) and does not include 
organic equipment that is afloat on MPS.  The exemption also negates the 
need for quarterly CMR reconciliations for MPF ME that is afloat. 
• A float organic ME (within M92502 account) shall be inventoried 100% 
annually and shall be subject to quarterly CMR reconciliations by the RO. 
• Annual physical inventory of ME within the MMV420 DoDAAC for the overseas MCPP -N account will not be a responsibility of the MCMC, but 
rather will be jointly completed by NDLO/MEB custodians and Government representatives with requested assistance from the MCMC.  
• Controlled Items, EKMS/CCI, and small arms shall be physically inventoried 
at frequencies IAW MCO 400.201 (Vol 1) and EKMS 1B (Chap 7).

 4.C.3.a The MCMC shall conduct cyclical physical inventories of perpetual inventory to 
ensure accurate Existence and Completeness of intermediate level retail accounts IAW MCO 4400.201 and MARCORLOGCOMO 4440.1 PICP .   
 
(i) The requirement to conduct physical inventories of perpetual inventory includes OM&S held at the intermediate level (GA and RIP) and OM&S at the consumer level (Demand Supported Items), whether on island or afloat/ashore -
based, but excludes prepositioned sustainment blocks aboard ship due to inaccessibility, which shall be inventoried upon download during MMC production. (ii) The MCMC shall document and report physical inventory results for intermediate level accounts IAW MCO 4400.201 and MARCORLOGCOMO 4440.1.  MCMC shall submit inventory results to the Government  through 
submission of the PICP I ntermediate L evel A ccount I nvintory R esult s Report 
(CDRL 5022)  within 10 business days following inventory completion, whether 
conducting 100% annual physical inventory, 50% semi -annually, 25% quarterly, 
or 10% monthly.  Inventory results shall include comprehensive causative research and recommended adjustments trans actions (Document Identifier Code) 
to correct the APSR property records for each disparate NIIN.  Additionally, the inventory results shall include the following data elements:  NIIN, SAC, physical OH Qty vs. APSR Qty, d ifference between the OH and APSR, U/P, T/P, overall 
inventory accuracy and variance.  
 
4.C.3.b The MCMC shall utilize perpetual sub- inventories within GCSS -MC for 
accountability of perpetual inventory prepositioned stocks IAW MCO 4400.201, UM 
4000- 125, and CmdO 4790.1.  The standard perpetual sub- inventories within GCSS -MC 
will be used IAW the direction contained in the MCO 4400.201 and UM 4000- 125, apart 
from shipboard sustainment blocks embarked on ships other than T -AKEs, which is 
detailed in the next task.   Stocks shall not be resident in 01A longer than four business 
days (with exception of 01F pending disposition and 01A sub- inventory for GA, RIP, and 
T-AKE O/H stocks).  The physical O/H inventory shall match the O/H inventory in 
APSR and vice versa (existence and completion).   
 
4.C.3.c The MCMC shall load prepositioned shipboard sustainment blocks to the corresponding GCSS -MC sub- inventories and locations IAW Detailed Ship Plans and 
physical inventory embarked aboard MPF.  Loading shall be completed NLT the end of each ship’s res pective MMC production cycle.  Consumable sustainment blocks / 
perpetual inventory loaded to the T -AKE ships shall be loaded to the standard perpetual 
sub-inventories.  However, the sustainment blocks embarked on ships other than T -AKEs 
shall be loaded to the following Government -prescribed sub- inventories specific to 
BICmd:  POL (Class III), OPP, BATT, CLIX (Class IX), FORT (Class IV), and OPSTOCK (assets previously possessing TAMCNs that require accountability tracking).  Sustainment blocks embarked aboar d T-AKE vessels will be accounted for 
under the ship DODAAC using 01A sub- inventory, while sustainment blocks embarked 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 135 of 425 
 
 on other MPF will be accounted for under respective DODAAC using aforementioned 
sub-inventories and ship as location.     
 4.C.4  The MCMC shall manage the physical inventory of all maritime prepositioned equipment and supplies (MPE/S), whether afloat or ashore, maintaining accurate accountability within the appropriate APSR IAW MCO 4400.201, MCO P4400.151, and UM 4000- 125.   
 
4.C.4.a.  The MCMC shall submit GCSS -MC produced Consolidated Memorandum 
Receipts (CMR), Consolidated Asset Listing (SECREPs), CLIX and Sustainment Block listings, signed by the Responsible Officer (RO) after each ship’s production cycle at E+5 (CDRL 5024).  IAW MCO4400.201, prepositioned ME on afloat MPS is exempt from quarterly CMR reconciliations.  This exemption does not apply to organic ME (M92502) afloat on MPS, which continues to require quarterly CMR reconciliations that result in RO-endorsed CMRs. 
 
The RO must initial each page and sign the last page of the CMR, which can be accomplished electronically. The Shipboard RO shall use all available data sources to assist with validating accountability of all equipment (e.g. MCPIC, Staging Plan, Deck Diagr ams, etc.).  As equipment completes production and is declared FMC (which 
includes data accuracy), Shipboard ROs should begin validation of equipment as early possible to meet the E+5 deadline.  The signed CMR is the official Key Supporting Document (KSD) that meets the MCO 4400.201 and Financial Improvement Audit Readiness (FIAR) requirements for MPF accountability.  While prepositioned ME on afloat MPS is exempt from the recurring quarterly CMR reconciliation, during the afloat phase, there are two occasi ons which require the submission of a signed CMR.  Validated 
CMRs will be submitted within 30 days following an RO turnover and within 30 days following the completion and backload of any exercise.  As with the post -MMC 
production period CMR, the Shipboard ROs shall use all available data sources to assist with validating accountability of all equipment (e.g. MCPIC, CAL, Staging Plan, Deck Diagrams, etc.).   
      
Due to the unique nature of the MPF program, embarkation of MPE/S requires maximizing space aboard the vessel (to include containerized assets and mobile loads). Due to the inaccessibility of some containerized and mobile loaded equipment, it is essential for the Shipboard RO to maximize the time before E+5 to validate accountability of equipment.  Once afloat, the use of data sources to certify accountability of equipment is acceptable, specifically the use of seal serial numbers as verification of contain erized and packaged assets that cannot be physically verified.  
 
The guidance above regarding CMR frequency and timeframes is specific to MPF accounts afloat and does not include M92502 equipment/assets that are afloat on ship sub-custody SUCs. The shipboard SUC resident on M92502 account remain subject to 
the quarterly  CMR reconciliation requirements.  Additionally, the conduct of monthly 
small arms serialized inventories afloat (CDRL 5025) is still required to be conducted IAW Section C.4.C.2.c and C.4.C.4.b.

4.C.4.b The MCMC shall conduct monthly physical inventories of CRANE reportable serialized small arms embarked aboard MPS and subsequently provide a Monthly Afloat Armory Report  (CDRL 5025)  for each ship with embarked small arms/weapons IAW OH 
inventory, GCSS -MC, and MCPIC records.  The Monthly Afloat Armory Report shall be 
submitted NLT the last business day of the month and must be RO - endorsed, validating 
continuous accountability of serialized small arms.  The report shall be IAW Government -provided format ( SOW Attachment C.4 -4).  
 4.C.4.c  The MCMC shall maintain positive chain of custody and accountability of all ME during exercise and contingency operations IAW MCO 4400.201, TM 4790- 14/2, 
MCO 3000.17, Government -provided Exercise Support Letters, and UM 4000- 125.  
Prepositioning Program ME (to include Navy assets), either offloaded from MPS or drawn from ashore prepositioning inventory, provided to the MAGTF in support of exercises and contingencies shall be issued on a temporary loan basis, utilizing a Consolidated Asset Listing ( CAL).  The Government provides the MCMC with an initial 
CAL, which is produced from MCPIC data for MPS and NEIMS data for MCPP -N, prior 
to an exercise based upon specific equipment that is expected  to be temp loaned to the 
MAGTF.  However, the MCMC is responsible for the final generation of an accurate CAL that matches the equipment that was actually  temp loaned for the exercise or 
operation, which shall be signed by the MAGTF RO and NSE RO as a sub- custody 
receipt for the exercise.  Subsequent to the MCMC’ s development of an accurate RO -
endorsed CAL, the MCMC shall change the assets to reflect a temporary loan status within GCSS -MC. 
 
(i) The MCMC shall place applicable ME into a temporary loan status within GCSS -MC after issuing MPF, MCPP -N, and MCPP -PHIL prepositioning 
program equipment to the MAGTF in support of exercises or contingencies IAW MCO 4400.201, CmdO 4790.1, UM 4000- 125, and TM 4790- 14/2.  Temp loan 
status shall be reflected in GCSS -MC within 5 calendar days of equipment change 
of custody (temp loan upon issuing ME, and removal of temp loan upon accepting ME back).  There will be assets resident on the CAL and physically te mp loaned 
to the MAGTF that will not be able to be placed into a GCSS -MC temp loan 
status due to not residing in GCSS -MC (i.e. Navy CESE assets are not in GCSS -
MC, assets that are not loaded to the IMO cannot be picked up in GCSS -MC and 
are accounted for w ithin DPAS, CBRN assets are accounted for within DPAS, 
Non-Standard Equipment with HQMC -established POs such as wash racks and 
floodlights).    (ii) While the standard procedure, as defined by policy, is to issue Prepositioning Program ME in support of exercises and operations on a temporary loan basis utilizing a CAL, there are occasions where HQMC (PP&O) directs the use of equipment transfers.  In these situations, the MCMC shall utilize EATO transactions to transfers the equipment identified on the CAL (where resident in GCSS -MC) to the MAGTF/Exercise Force DoDAAC.  For exercise purposes,

 weapons and CCI are not included in the transfers, avoiding the requirement for 
CRANE transactions.  
 
4.C.5 The MCMC shall conduct storage and warehousing operations for all Government -owned 
property IAW MCO 4400.201, MCO P4400.151, MCO 4450.12, MCO 10330.2, MCO 4140.5A, MCO 5530.14, MCO 4450.14, MIL -STD -129, and MIL -STD -130.  Warehousing operations 
include storage plan -o-graphs, segregation of materiel, layout, spacing, markings, location 
verifications, stock surveys, spot inventories, security, and materiel handling.  
4.C.5.a The MCMC shall maintain an updated stock location database for all retail level inventory accounts located at MCSF -BI and all MCPP -PHIL retail accounts IAW MCO 
4400.201, MCO P4400.151, and MCO 4450.14.  Accurate and updated locator decks/location information files shall be made available for Government access.  The stock location database shall possess accurate location, type/NSN, quantity, U/I, and condition code of materiel.  4.C.5.b The MCMC shall utilize the designated warehousing management system for warehouse operations of those stocks carried within the General Account (MMV200) IAW the STRATIS Administrative User's Manual, STRATIS Desktop User's Manual, and STRATIS Handheld User's Manual .  GCSS -MC is the APSR for accountability of 
perpetual inventory while the Storage Retrieval Automated Tracking Integrated System (STRATIS) currently serves as the warehouse management system to complement GCSS -MC.  The STRATIS Workload (Pi ck, Pack, Ship, Stow, and Inventory) shall be 
cleared daily.  The warehouse denial rate shall not be greater than two  percent (2%) per 
month IAW inventory accuracy standards in MCO 4400.201 (Vol 1) , and STRATIS 
inventory records shall match GCSS -MC inventory records.  
 
4.C.5.c The MCMC shall establish and maintain a surveillance program for the care of supplies in storage (COSIS) IAW MCO 4450.14, MCO 4400.201, MCO 4450.15, UM 4000- 125, applicable TMs, and the command order for COSIS .  The MCMC shall submit 
a COSIS Surveillance Plan  (CDRL 5027)  for Government review and approval within 60 
calendar days after contract commencement, and thereafter, annually NLT September 1
st 
each year.  COSIS Surveillance Plan shall include processes of in- storage 
inspection/testing to ensure that the accurate condition of materiel in storage is known and properly recorded, and that the materiel is properly provided with adequate 
packaging protection to prevent any degradation to lower condition codes.  COSIS 
actions shall include stock surveillance;  stock rotation;  quantity, location, U/I, markings, condition, and identification accuracy;  periodic inspections for serviceability, screening, 
and/or testing, with follow -on corrective action and/or PMCS completed to restore the 
assets to a ready for issue condition and/or prevent future deterioration.  COSIS Plan 
shall cover a full fiscal year with detailed status and updates provided on a monthly 
basis.  Monthly updates will include a listing of the materiel (by SN where applicable) planned for COSIS inspection, testing, or PMCS for the following month.

4.C.5.d  The MCMC shall develop and execute a Shelf Life/Service Life Management program for all shelf life assets and materiel in its possession IAW DoD 4140.27- M, 
MCO 4000.58, MCO 3000.17, MCO 4140.5A, TM 4790- 14/2, and Rations PBA Annex 
between DLA and I&L (LF) .  Standards for minimum shelf life remaining at the time of 
MMC embarkation aboard MPF, or at the time of shipment to MCPP -N, or MCPP -PHIL, 
may be waiverable on a case- by-case basis by the Government, with consideration given 
to cost to procure ne w materiel, remaining shelf life, length of afloat phase prior to next 
MMC production period, type of materiel being considered for waiver, and planned exercises/operations for the respective ship.  Waiver requests shall be submitted to the Government, as applicable, in the form of Letters of Reconsideration.

 (i) IAW MCO 4000.58, the MCMC shall ensure Type I (non- extendable) assets 
that are listed on the PO with a shelf life code of 36 months or longer are 
loaded/embarked aboard MPF with a minimum of 24 months or more remaining shelf life at the time of MMC embarka tion (E -day) aboard MPS.     
 
(ii) IAW MCO 4000.58, the MCMC shall ensure Type II (extendable) assets that are listed on the PO with a shelf life code of 24 months or longer are loaded/embarked aboard MPF with a minimum of 18 months or more remaining shelf life at the time of MMC embarkatio n aboard MPS.  
 
(iii) Exception : See Section C.3 (Maintenance), Para. 3.A.3.b for the Service Life 
Requirements designated for elastomeric components of prepositioned Tactical Fuel Systems (TFS).  
 
(iv)  IAW MCO 4000.58, the MCMC shall ensure rations/MREs possess 33 months or more until end of shelf life at the time of MMC embarkation aboard MPS.     
 
MCPP -N and MCPP -PHIL Specific (4.C.5.d):  The MCMC shall ship only 
material, products, and supplies with a 25- month or greater shelf life remaining 
IAW MCOs, TMs, and Norway and Philippines Branch direction.  In order to ensure 24 months shelf life on location in Norway/Philippines, 25 months of shelf life must remain prior to shipping. 
 
(v) The MCMC shall submit a Quarterly Shelf Life/Service Life Expiration Report within the first 10 calendar days of each FY quarter.  The report shall be 
accurate IAW MCO 4000.58, MCO 4140.5, FEDLOG, TMs, the MCMC -
generated shelf life database and O/H invento ry.  The Quarterly Shelf 
Life/Service Life Expiration Report  (CDRL 5029)  shall contain the following 
data elements (where applicable) for all O/H assets with an end of service life occurring within the following 18 months, regardless of Type I or Type II s helf 
life code (SLC):  AAC (that materiel is loaded to within GCSS -MC), SUC 
(ship, commodity, or cave), TAMCN (if applicable), NIIN, nomenclature, quantity, unit price, extended price, shelf life type (I or II), SLC, number of extensions authorized IAW SLC , number and dates of previous extensions, 
manufacturer date, last inspection date, type of inspection required to extend shelf life, next inspection date (NID), final expiration date/end of service life, and associated parent TAMCN for assets that have been configured.  
 
(vi)  The MCMC shall maintain a complete and accurate record, available to the Government for review, with all shelf life/service life data on materiel with a SLC IAW DoD 4140.27- M, MCO 4000.58, MCO 4140.5, FEDLOG, TMs, 
and physical O/H inventory.  The shelf life database shall contain the following elements (where applicable):  AAC, SUC (ship, commodity, or cave), TAMCN (if applicable), NIIN, nomenclature, quantity, unit price, extended price, shelf life type (I or II), SLC, serial number, contract/ lot/batch

 number, number of extensions authorized IAW SLC, number and dates of

Data shall be accurate IAW TM 4700 -15/1 and TI 10010-
OR for CBRN equipment.  
(vii) The MCMC shall obtain shelf life extensions on materiel when required and permissible IAW respective equipment Shelf Life Codes, Supply Bulletins (SB), DoD 4140.27- M, MCO 4000.58, MCO 4140.5, MCSF -BI Hazardous 
Management Plan, TMs and/or SL -3.  Eligible shelf life extensions shall be 
obtained in all situations where extensions are possible.  
 
(viii) The MCMC shall utilize materiel without sufficient shelf life for embarkation on MPS to fill local (on -island, MMC production, or organic account) 
requirements or redistribute to the operating forces IAW DoD 4140.27 -M,

Management Plan.  In an effort to avoid unnecessary materiel costs and maximize the efficient use of materiel, any materiel that does not meet shelf life requirements for storage/prepositioning during an afloat phase shall be used to meet other applicable requirements to the maximum extent possible.  
 
4.C.5.e The MCMC shall conduct Preservation, Packaging, Packing, and Marking (P3&M) of equipment and supplies requiring storage, embarkation, or shipment IAW MCO 4400.201 (Vol 14), MCO P4030.31, MCO 4030.40, MCO 4030.33_, MCO P4030.21, MIL -STD -129, MIL -STD -130, MIL -STD -2073- 1E, and NAVSUP P700- CNP.   
 4.C.5.f  The MCMC shall adhere to physical security and safeguarding requirements of all Government assets IAW DoD 5100.76- M and MCO 4400.201.  Physical security shall 
include segregating and restricting access to highly pilferable, sensitive, and other items requiring special storage and handling.  4.C.5.g  The MCMC shall conduct materiel/cargo handling in support of warehousing and distribution operations.  Personnel training, testing, and licensing program for MHE/CHE shall be managed IAW the latest versions of MCO P11240.106 and MCO 4450.14.  The MCMC shall use appropriate MHE and CHE to move respective materiel IAW the latest/current versions of applicable  equipment TMs.  MHE/CHE operators shall 
be licensed for the equipment they are operating.  
 
4.C.6  The MCMC shall apply Automatic Identification Technology (AIT) resources to materiel and assets to identify, track, and document materiel IAW  DoDI 8320.04, MCO 4000.51C, 
MCBul 4440, Section C.2.A of the SOW, and CmdO 4035.1.  Minimum AIT applications include passive RFID tags, Item Unique Identification (IUID), and linear/2D barcodes, and 2D data matrix bar codes.  The MCMC shall utilize the DoD IUID registry for applicable (IUID tagged) asset visibility, accountability, management and reporting of m ateriel.

4.C.7 The MCMC shall execute the redistribution, rollback, or disposition of unserviceable, obsolete, or excess materiel and assets IAW DoD 4160.21- M, MCO 4400.201, MCO P4400.151, 
MCO 4790.2, CmdO 4790.1, UM 4000- 125, UM 4400- 60, respective equipment TM/TI, 
Disposal Plans, and official messages providing disposition instructions.  Requests for disposition (WIR/GCSS -MC Return SRs) shall be entered into APSR within 20 calendar days of 
receiving Government approval to dispose of excess/obsolete/unserviceable a sset.  Assets shall 
be shipped within 25 calendar days of receiving disposition instructions or shipped in time to meet stated Required Delivery Date (RDD), whichever is earlier.  
   
4.C.7.a The MCMC shall request Government approval prior to executing redistributions, executing rollback/MRP, or submitting requests for disposition (WIR) for unserviceable, obsolete, or excess equipment or consumable supplies  (CDRL 5030) .  MCMC requests to 
the Government for redistributions, rollbacks/MRPs, or dispositions shall be accurate IAW property records (O/H and allowances / PO), GA demand history (for consumables), condition codes, and acquisition advice codes.  Government approval shall 
be obtai ned prior to executing disposition or submitting disposition request via the APSR. 
 4.C.7.b When submitting requests for disposition (WIR) for unserviceable ME, the MCMC shall upload an Uneconomical Repairs Cost Assessment Package to the Return SR within GCSS -MC IAW MCO 4400.201 (Vol 6), CmdO 4790.1, and MSG 280850Z 
Jul 21. 
 4.C.8 The MCMC shall notify the Government of missing, lost, stolen, or damaged Government -
owned assets IAW MCO 5530.14 and MCO 4400.201.  Written notification from the MCMC 
shall include, as applicable:  TAMCN, DODIC (ammunition specific), nomenclature, quantity, serial number, dollar value, name of RO and associated AAC/SUC, (issuing) local element, time of discovery, security breaches, and a brief synopsis of circumstances surrounding the incident  
(CDRL 5031) .  "Immediate notification" shall be provided for any assets identified as MLSR 
reportable items; “Immediate notification” is defined as “written with receipt confirmation via phone call provided by the end of the business day of any assets identified as MLSR reportable items.  Updates shall be provided every three business days for situations requiring "immediate notification", until the equipment is recovered, Government approval is obtained to drop the assets from property records, or Government directs ceasing notification updates.  "Urgent notification" shall be provided within 24 hours for any non- MCBul 3000 serialized assets valued 
above $800, any non -consumable unserialized property valued above $5,000, and any controlled 
item not meeting "immediate notification" standard.  "Routine notification" shall be provided within 48 hours for any serialized asset valued below $800 and not meeting criteria for "immediate" or "urgent" notification.  
 
4.C.8.a The MCMC shall prepare and route DD Form 200 for lost, damaged, or destroyed Government property IAW FLIPL MCBUL (MCBUL 4440.4) and MCO 4400.201 within five business days of discovery of the lost, damaged, or destroyed equipment or supplies  (CDRL 5032) .  DD Form 200 is used to initiate the "Financial Liability 
Investigation of Property Loss" (FLIPL) process.  The Form is to be completed when one 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 142 of 425 
 
 or more of the situations listed in the most current FLIPL MCBUL (MCBUL 4440.4) or 
FLIPL Volume of MCO 4400.201 exists, to include "Marine Corps property in the possession of the MCMC is lost, damaged, or destroyed".  DD Form 200 is not used to relieve or hold the MCMC liable for lost, damaged, or destroyed Marine Corps property, but rather to serve as documentation for inventory adjustments.  MCMC -generated 
voucher requests (reference 4.C.9.a ) are still required for Government approval of all 
inventory rec ord adjustments.  DD Form 200 is an additional requirement that will 
supplement the voucher request for situations outlined in the FLIPL MCBUL or FLIPL Volume of MCO 4400.201 related to lost, damaged, or destroyed property.  In addition to the MCMC complet ing blocks 1, 3, 4- 9, and 17a -f of DD Form 200 (as directed by 
MCBUL 4440.4), blocks 10 and 11 shall also be completed by the MCMC prior to submission to the Government.  Subsequently, the Government will complete blocks 17.e and 17.f.  The MCMC shall assi gn a document number and complete box 17.a upon 
receipt of Government approval to adjust property records.  
 4.C.8.b  The MCMC shall conduct causative research and provide a completed investigation report ( CDRL 5033) to the Government for situations requiring MLSR 
reporting and other cases of missing, lost, stolen, or damaged materiel, as directed by the Government IAW  MCO 5530.14 and MCO 4400.201.  Conduct of MCMC causative 
research  involving MLSR incidents is independent of normal adjustment procedures or 
Government command investigations.  At a minimum, the MCMC's Investigation Report format shall include:  Pre liminary Statement, Findings of Facts, Opinions, 
Recommendations, and corresponding accident LTI (in the case of tactical equipment) or repair estimate (from commercial facility in the case of GME or GSA leased vehicles) to provide dollar value of damages.  Investigation Report(s) shall be provided to the 
Government within 15 calendar days of initial notification of MLSR incident.  4.C.8.c The MCMC shall provide completed SF -91, accident LTI, and corresponding SF -
94 when applicable, to the Government when Government -owned assets are involved in 
accidents IAW TM 4700.15/1 and MCO P11240.106 ( CDRL 5034) .  Requirement 
applies to ME and Non- Tactical Vehicles/GSA leased vehicles.  In situations involving 
NTVs, SFs shall be provided to Government Fleet Manager within one business day of the accident.  In situations involving tactical assets, SFs shall be provided to the Government (Attentio n:  Director LOGDIV) within one business day of the accident.  
Accident LTI for tactical assets, or repair estimate from commercial facility in the case of NTV and GSA leased assets, shall be provided with MCMC investigation (where required) within 15 cale ndar days after accident (unless constrained by commercial 
facility's delivery of estimate).  Accident LTI shall accurately document physical damages incurred.  
 
4.C.9  The MCMC shall, upon Government approval, adjust property records to ensure on hand inventory matches APSR records IAW MCO 4400.201, MCO P4400.151, MCBUL 4440.4, and UM 4000- 125.  Inventory adjustment transactions shall be completed within five business days 
of Government approval, via Government endorsed voucher request and/or DD Form 200.
