  6.B.3  The MCMC shall update an NAV -P provided joint stakeholders' Navy Actual Cost of 
Work Performed (ACWP) (CDRL 6009)  Report on a daily basis. As authorized purchases and/or 
subcontracts for materials and/or services are executed, the MCMC shall update the applicable report (SOW Attachment C.6 -004) for the specific Navy effort (NSE, NCE, NBG, etc.) The joint 
Navy ACWP Report shall not have more than two data errors per month.  
  6.B.4  The MCMC shall provide a complete and accurate Navy End -of-Ship MPF Maintenance 
Cycle (MMC) Life Cycle Cost Report (CDRL 6010)  NLT 60 calendar days after each MPS 
embarkation using the report template provided in SOW Attachment C.6 -005. Report by serial 
number all costs incurred for the MMC and life cycle costs for Navy Watercraft, including LARC and ABLTS systems to include ship's name, maintenance cycle dates, and total costs separately for material, subcontract serv ices, shipyard basic and over and above work and supply 
system items. A summary sheet for each type of Navy Watercraft and ABLTS system shall be provided for each applicable MMC.  All NAV -P identified corrections shall be made to the 
report within one business day.   6.B.5  The MCMC shall provide a weekly Status of Funds report utilizing Government approved accounting procedures to NAV -P. The following minimum information shall be provided:  
  
(a) Posted Authorizations Posted Obligations Posted Available Balance Pending Corrections.
 
 
(b)  Revised Available Balance Allocations Received MRP Credits Received.  
  
6.B.6  The MCMC shall provide an Estimate At Completion (EAC) (CDRL 6011)  Report for 
Navy fiscal tracking and execution using the report template provided in SOW Attachment C.6 -
006. MCMC shall provide a 12- month projection initially for each Contract Line Item Number 
(CLIN) with a monthly breakdown. Subsequent months should show prior month actuals. The EAC Report is due NLT the tenth business day of the month.    
6.B.6.a  The follow -on year EAC (CDRL 6012)  will be provided to the COR NLT 1 June 
of the current year.  
  6.B.7  The MCMC shall provide a detailed Navy Invoice Report (CDRL 6013)  for accurate 
accounting purposes using the template provided at SOW Attachment C.6 -007. Detailed invoice 
shall be provided prior to submission for payment and include detailed labor, material and subcontract costs, and travel expenses. Invoice(s) shall be submitted with detailed backup documentation, delivered NLT first we ekday of each month.   
  6.B.8   The MCMC shall provide a timely and accurate electronic Contract Cost (CPFF) and Status Report (CCSR) for each Task Order (TO). The CCSR shall be submitted monthly, or as requested by the Government, concurrent with invoice submission, commencing 30 days after

6.B.10.a  Report, identify, and provide supporting documentation on accounting system 
problems to Government immediately upon discovery.   6.B.10.b  Store and maintain all source documents and accounting reports required for audits. This is to include receipt documentation for supply system and offline system requisitions.

 6.C NAVY SUPPLY CHAIN, LOGISTICS, AND WAREHOUSING  
  6.C.1 The MCMC shall establish and maintain a dedicated Navy Supply Center (NSC) Organization.   
6.C.1.a  The MCMC shall perform Supply Chain Management functions IAW NAVSUP P-485 for all Navy MCMC efforts and to include logistics support to other NAV- P 
Contracts, via Government approval and/or direction. Supply management consists of the following but not limited to: replenishment, processing Defective Material Summaries (DMS), technical data research, disposal, and upgrading condition of MPS equipment, supplies, and maintenance cycle support materiel. 
  6.C.2  The MCMC shall operate and maintain the assigned Navy Warehouses. The MCMC shall comply with facilities requirements IAW MCSF -BI Building Manager Handbook. Navy 
warehousing facilities' management includes but is not limited to indoor, outdoor, improved, and semi -improved locations with regard to cleanliness, safety violations, and discrepancies. 
  
6.C.2.a  The MCMC shall use the Operator's Daily Check List (ODCL) for all Navy owned Material Handling Equipment (MHE) IAW NAVSUP P-538.   
  6.C.3  The NSC shall execute directed redistribution and disposal of Government authorized excess and obsolete Navy materiel IAW NAVSUP P -485 and NAVFAC-P-300. Redistribution 
includes but is not limited to; receipt, disposal, and transfer to other Governme nt agencies 
external to BICmd. NAV-P will provide authorized notification of all redistribution of Navy materiel. The MCMC is required to accurately complete/submit the DD Form 1348 -1 and DD 
Form 1149 as appropriate to support Government and designated Contractors.

6.C.4.b  The MCMC shall develop and execute shelf Life/Service Life Management Program for all Navy Shelf Life Assets and Materiel resident in Navy Warehouse and Storage Areas IAW DoD 4140.27 and MCO 3000.17. The MCMC shall submit a Shelf Life Report (CDRL 6015)  on the last day of every quarter. Navy Shelf Life materiel 
prepositioned on MPF ships requires a Shelf Life of greater than 24 months. Shelf life materiel with less than 24 -months of shelf life remaining shall not be prepositioned 
unless a Letter of  Non-Conformance is approved by NAV -P. 
  
(i) The MCMC shall obtain Shelf Life extensions on materiel when required and permissible IAW respective equipment Shelf Life Codes, Supply Bulletins (SB), DoD 4140.27- M, MCSF -BI Hazardous Management Plan and 
Technical Manuals (TMs). Eligible Shelf Life ex tensions shall be obtained in 
all situations where extensions are possible.  
  6.C.5 The MCMC shall establish and maintain a Navy specific General Account (GA) by COSAL type.
  Inventory levels shall be maintained within high and low levels, using template 
provided in SOW Attachment C.6 -008.  
  
6.C.5.a  The MCMC shall, at a minimum, use the following COSAL and sub inventory structure in RSupply:  
  
(i) NSE  
(a) Reoccuring Maintenance Parts (“RMP”)  
(b) High Failure Parts (“HFP”)  
(c) Software Support Activity (“SSA”)  
(d) Navy Watercraft Afloat Maintenance Services (“WMS”)  
(e) Navy Cargo Handling Battalion (“CHB”)  
(ii) NCE  
(a) Civil Engineer Support Equipment (“CE”)  
(iii) NBG   
(a)   Expeditionary Boatswain’s Locker (“EBL”)  
  
6.C.5.b The MCMC shall, at a minimum, use the following RMP sub inventory Local Management Codes (LMC):  
  (i)     Amphibious Bulk Liquid Transfer System (“AB”)  
(ii)    Landing Craft Mechanical (“LC”)  
(iii)   Lighter, Amphibious Resupply Cargo (“LA”)  
(iv)   Ramp Module (“RM”)  
(v)    Beach Module (“BM”)  
(vi)   Combination Module (“CM”)

6.C.5.c  The MCMC shall maintain General Account Program Records and shall submit 
an accurate Quarterly Shelf Life/Service Life expiration reports (CDRL 6016)  the first 
business day of each quarter IAW DoD 4140.27- M and MCO 3000.17.  
 
6.C.5.d  The database shall be accessible to NAV -P for reviewing upon request.   
 
6.C.5.e  MCMC shall submit a RSupply Reorder Review NLT 11:59 am on the second and fourth Friday of every month for NAV -P review and guidance.  
 
 
6.C.5.f  GA contents shall be approved by NAV -P then entered into RSupply. The 
MCMC shall maintain General Program Records.  
6.C.6  The MCMC shall ensure all KSDs supporting the transfer of equipment in support of an exercise, contingency, temporary loan or transfer to another unit/contractor, are uploaded into GCSS -MC and/or RSupply, as applicable. Supporting KSDs include the Exercise Support Letter, 
Outgoing/Incoming Consolidated Asset List with proper signatures, DD Forms 1348, DD Form 1149 (if required), Navy Consolidated Me morandum Receipt (CMRs) (if required), or any other 
documents used to ensure proper chain of custody.
 
 
  6.C.6.a The MCMC shall maintain a file of all Financial Improvement and Audit                
 Readiness (FIAR) audit required KSDs. This file shall be in DoDAAC/UIC, Julian      date 
and document sequence order.   
   
6.C.6.b  The MCMC shall immediately notify NAV -P if any of the following occur:  
 
(i) RSupply, GCSS -MC or any other authorized program is not available or   
working as intended.  
(ii) A safety issue occurs.  
(iii) A work stoppage occurs for any reason.  
(iv) MCMC employees have no more tasks or work available.  
(v) Missing, lost, stolen, or damaged Government -owned assets.  
  
6.C.6.c  The MCMC shall notify the Government of missing, lost, stolen, or damaged 
Government -owned assets IAW MCO 5530.14, MCO 4400.201, and Marine Corps 
Bulletin (MCBul) 3000 series. The MCMC shall conduct investigations as directed by the Government.  
 6.C.6.d  The MCMC shall prepare and route DD Form 200 for Prepositioning Objective (PO) materiel to the Navy COR for lost, damaged, or destroyed Government property IAW NAVSUP P -485, FLIPL MCBUL (MCBUL 4440.4) and MCO 4400.201 within 
five business days of discovery of the lost, damaged, or destroyed equipment or supplies. DD Form 200 is used to initiate the "Financial Liability Investigation of Property Loss" 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 186 of 425 
 
 (FLIPL) process. The Form is to be completed when one (1) or more of the situations 
listed in the most current FLIPL MCBUL (MCBUL 4440.4) or FLIPL Volume of MCO 4400.201 exists, to include "Navy property in the possession of the MCMC is lost, damaged, or d estroyed". DD Form 200 is not used to relieve or hold the MCMC liable for 
lost, damaged, or destroyed Marine Corps property, but rather to serve as documentation for inventory adjustments. MCMC -generated voucher requests are still required for 
Government a pproval of all inventory record adjustments. DD Form 200 is an additional 
requirement that will supplement the voucher request for situations outlined in the FLIPL, MCBUL or MCO 4400.201 related to lost, damaged, or destroyed property.  In addition to the MCMC completing blocks 1, 3, 4- 9, and 17a -f of DD Form 200 (as 
directed by MCBUL 4440.4), blocks 10 and 11 shall also be completed by the MCMC prior to submission to the Government. Subsequently, the Navy COR will complete blocks 17.e and 17.f. The MCMC shall assign a document number and complete box 17.a upon receipt of Government approval to adjust property records.  
  
6.C.7  The MCMC shall requisition materiel and services for all Navy requirements IAW NAVSUP P -485. Reflect the appropriate abbreviation in the supplemental address field on the 
Military Standard Requisitioning & Issue Procedures (MILSTRIP) document.   
6.C.7.a  The MCMC shall receipt for all requisitioned and force -fed Navy materiel 
ensuring compliance with FIAR and IAW NAVSUP P -485, DoD FMR, DoD Instruction 
5000.64, SECNAVINST 4440.33A, SECNAVINST 5200.42, SECNAVINST 7320.10A.  Material shall be receipt ed for within two (2) business days of delivery. The process shall 
include:  
  
(i) Receipt and Acceptance
   
(ii) Secondary Level Review of Acquisitions  
(iii) Key Supporting Documents (KSD) to Accountable Property System of Record (APSR)  
(iv) Reconciliation of Acquisition  
(v) Physical Inventory  
(vi) Disposition Authorization 
(vii) Secondary Level Review of Disposition 
  
(viii) KSD to APSR Reconciliation of Disposition  
  
6.C.7.b  The MCMC shall stow or distribute materiel, upon receipt, to the appropriate Navy recipient ensuring compliance with FIAR IAW NAVSUP P -485, DoD FMR, DoD 
Instruction 5000.64, SECNAVINST 4440.33A, SECNAVINST 5200.42, SECNAVINST 7320.10A.     
6.C.7.c  The MCMC shall process Depot Level Repair (DLR) and returns from the 
MMV104 account IAW NAVSUP P -485 AND NAVSUP P -723. Unserviceable DLRs 
shall be turned into DLA on a one for one exchange basis. Prior to shipping a DLR carcass the MCMC shall requ est and obtain Government approval. Upon receiving 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP

 Government approval, DLRs in the MMV104 account shall be administratively 
(RSupply) and physically processed by the MCMC for evacuation/shipment to DLA.    6.C.7.d  The MCMC shall requisition Government -approved DLRs. Requisitioning of 
DLRs. Government approval shall be obtained prior to obligation of funds to support DLR requisitions.  
  6.C.8  The MCMC shall segregate, store, and maintain Navy hazardous material IAW Section 6.T. The MCMC shall have the ability to identify, declare, package, certify, and load containerized hazardous materials IAW the current International Maritime Dangerou s Goods 
(IMDG) code and Command Order (CmdO) 4035.1. 
  
6.C.9  The MCMC shall execute the redistribution, rollback, or disposition of unserviceable, obsolete, or excess materiel and assets IAW DoD 4160.21- M, NA VSUP P -723, NA VSUP P -485, 
CmdO 4790.1, respective equipment TM/TI, Disposal Plans, and official messages providing disposition instructions.  
  6.C.9.a  The MCMC shall request NAV -P approval prior to executing redistributions, 
executing rollback, or submitting requests for disposition for unserviceable, obsolete, or excess repair parts. MCMC requests to the Government for redistributions, rollbacks and/or dispositions shall be accurate IAW ASPR (O/H and allowances/RO). Government approval shall be obtained prior to executing disposition or submitting disposition via the APSR.  
  
6.C.10  The MCMC shall ensure adequate protection and physical security of all Navy assets IAW NAVSUP P -485 and NAVSUP P -723. Discrepancies shall be noted during spot check.   
  6.C.11  The MCMC shall perform Care of Supplies in Storage (COSIS) IAW NAVSUP P -485 
and NAVSUP P -723.  
  6.C.12  The MCMC shall prepare and maintain appropriate supply administrative documentation for personnel conducting requisitioning and receiving functions IAW NAVSUP P - 485 and 
NAVSUP P -723. Examples of required documentation include, but are not limited to, DD Form 
577 and Navy Marine Corps (NAVMC) 11869.  
  6.C.13  The MCMC shall track, provide follow -up and keep record of all requisitions for the 
fiscal year, follow -up on all outstanding Navy materiel requisitions including those from 
previous fiscal years and provide a Navy Weekly Status Report ( CDRL 6017)  (SOW Attachment 
C.6-009) to NAV -P IAW NAVSUP P -485. All cancelled requisitions will be identified and the 
MCMC shall provide recommendations to NAV -P on whether to reorder the National Stock 
Number (NSN), reorder an alternate NSN or not to reorder the NSN based on inputs from the requisitioner. The Navy Weekly Status Report shall be delivered each Monday by 11:59 am. The MCMC shall submit a Supply Discrepancy Report (SDR) with document identifier per “AS1” NLT 10 business days old with a value equal to or greater than $100 per line item.

485, applicable plans, and ship’s Planning Consideration Letter (PCL). Weapons are receipted for using the DD Form 1348- 1 which is included with the shipment to NAV -P. Su bmit the 
signed and dated DD Form 1348- 1 or DD Form 1149 NLT the close of business on the day 
received. This will require coordination with the MCMC. The MCMC shall identify any issues preventing completion of this task to the Government for remedy.    
  
6.C.15.a  The MCMC shall perform and submit a Navy Small Arms Monthly Inventory Report ( CDRL 6082)  (SOW Attachment C.6 -010) of all Navy weapons onboard MPF 
Ships and MCSF -BI Armory stating the actual physical inventory. Navy Small Arms 
Monthly Inventory Report shall be endorsed and validated by Responsible Individual (RI). The completed Small Arms Monthly Inventory Report is to be scanned (PDF) and submitted to the NAV -P RO, ACOR and COR NLT the tenth business day of each 
month.  
  6.C.16  The MCMC Transportation and Distribution Management Operation (TMO/DMO) shall support NSC and NAV -P shipping requirements.

contractors IAW CMdOs. Government database will be provided and used to track all pending and completed shipments, regardless of carrier, mode of shipment, or type of materiel being shipped. Data fields include: Document number, Priority, U/I, quantity,

reconciliation NLT 8:00 am daily each business day. New documents for supply system transactions that support the Navy effort shall be updated daily on the report. The DODAAC Documents Report shall not have more than two data  errors per month. For purchasing system 
buys, the MCMC shall adhere to NAV -P guidance for segregation of costs by equipment

  6.D NAVY CONTAINERIZED CAPSET PRODUCTION  
  
6.D.1  The MCMC shall manage and maintain a Navy owned International Organization for Standards (ISO) container program. Container Program management includes accountability, storage, inspection, recertification, maintenance, inventory, production, marking, and the use of containers for intermodal transport.

The MCMC shall accurately complete the Navy Container Management Report ( CDRL 6024)  (SOW Attachment C.6 -
012) NLT five business days after download.

Damaged Navy containers will be downloaded at the beginning of production to allow sufficient time for container repairs. Refer to SOW Attachment C.6 -012.

Ensuring sufficient containers quantities includes overseeing approved repairs, and container certification; and provide copies of 
the DA Form 2404 with cost estimate to NAV -P for repair approval.

packaging completion. NAV -P will provide guidance for Assembly and Class IX Block 
Packaging inventor y sheets for any requirements that are not covered by P -700-CNP and

prior to container backload. All assemblies, Class IX Blocks, shall be placard with NSN, Nomenclature, Unit of Issue, Quantity, Facility, Serial Number, Method of Packing, a nd

6.D.5   The MCMC shall pack Navy containers and associated mobile loads. 
  6.D.5.a  The MCMC shall pack containers and associated mobile loaded Principle End Items (PEIs) as required IAW with ship’s detailed plans posted on MCPIC, ship’s PCL, and as described in the MPS Production Schedule IAW DoD 4140.65M, MCO P4030.21 and MIL -STD-2073 -1. Platforms shall have proper cushioning, anchoring, bracing, and 
blocking to prevent materiel damage from deterioration during shipment, handling, storage, and wet weather conditions.   6.D.5.b  The MCMC shall place packing lists identifying contents on the inside and outside of one door for each container IAW CmDO 4035.1 prior to notice of completion. Outside packing lists shall be protected from the natural elements. The MCMC shall deliver the Navy Container Contents Packing List ( CDRL 6029)  (SOW Attachment C.6 -
014) to NAV -P NLT two business days after container completion.

(i) The MCMC shall update MCPIC data IAW CmdBul 3162. The Unit Deployment Listing (UDL) shall be updated within three business days of container completion.

(iii)  The MCMC shall weigh all Navy MPS loaded containers and associated mobile loaded platforms IAW MIL -STD 129 series and the Planning Milestones.

015) five business days after completion of the approved production period.    6.D.6.b  The MCMC shall submit complete and accurate Navy Assemblies and Class IX Shortage Report(s) ( CDRL 6031)  (SOW Attachment C.6- 016) five business days after 
completion of the approved production period.  NSE and NCE shall be separate reports.

Attachment  C.6- 017) five business days after completion of the approved production 
period.   6.D.6.d  The MCMC shall submit a complete and accurate Navy End of Production Container Report ( CDRL 6033)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 012) for all MCPIC designated 
Navy containers for the current production period within five business days after completion of the approved production period.   
  6.D.6.e The MCMC shall submit a complete and accurate Navy Consolidated Memorandum Receipt (CMR) ( CDRL 6034)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 021) for on Island 
PEIs by the fifth business day of each quarter.  
  Note: Completeness is defined as having all the necessary and/or appropriate data/information. Accuracy is defined as correct data/information.  
  6.D.7  The MCMC shall establish a Navy Calibration Control Program for all Navy CESE and containerized assets IAW the SOW and CmdO 4790.1.B.3.c. The MCMC shall deliver the Navy

 Calibration Report ( CDRL 6035)  (SOW Attachment C.6- 022) NLT E Day or seven business 
days following the end of each ship’s production cycle, whichever is sooner.    
  6.D.8  The MCMC shall perform International Organization for Standardization (ISO) Freight Container Inventory and Maintenance.  
  
6.D.8.a  The MCMC shall inspect/recertify every ISO freight container within the Navy inventory. Newly manufactured container certifications are valid for 60 months and recertifications are valid for 30 months, unless damage occurs, and repairs/recertifica tion 
is required. Inspection applies to all Navy containers (new, used, refurbished, and leased) coming into the organization's inventory, regardless of ownership. ISO freight containers shall be inspected/recertified IAW the latest/current version of ISO Handbook, 49 CFR 452, DTR 4500.9- R Part VI, Convention for Safe Containers (CSC) International Safe 
Container Act (ISCA), MIL -STD -3037. The MCMC shall complete the container 
certification (DD Form 2282) prior to packing/use, recertification, embarkation, a nd/or 
shipment. Accurate DD Form 2282 CSC decal applied to CSC data plate.   6.D.8.b  The MCMC shall retain inspection forms (DA 2404) on each container upon completion of inspection IAW MCO 4690.1, 49 CFR 452, DTR 4500.9- R Part VI, CSC 
International Safe Container Act (ISCA) and MIL -STD -3037. Records of inspections on 
containers may be destroyed/discarded after subsequent inspection generates a new record, with the exception of leased containers. All inspections for leased containers wil l 
be retained until return of the lease. This report shall be used to facilitate arbitration with the vendor regarding wear and damage while leased. The most current inspection form (DA Form 2404) for all other containers will be retained on file for all c ontainers until the 
next inspection and a copy provided to NAV -P. 
  6.D.8.c  The MCMC shall stow all Navy Containers at Navy Point with the exception of staging for MPS backload. Deviation requires NAV -P approval.   
  6.D.8.d  The MCMC shall store all Navy assigned Containers on concrete ties. NAV -P 
shall provide guidance pertaining to laydown locations as required.  
  6.D.8.e  The MCMC shall utilize proper MHE for Navy container movement operations.    6.D.8.f  The MCMC shall secure Navy freight containers prior to movement.   6.D.8.g  The MCMC shall ensure Navy freight container doors remain closed when not in use. To preclude unnecessary deterioration of wood or steel floors container doors shall not be left open except during packing/unpacking, maintenance, inspection and other necessary operations.   6.D.8.h  The MCMC shall ensure all Navy open top containers (4.25' and 6' high) are covered in a weather -tight condition when stored or staged in Condition Codes "A" or 
"F" IAW DTR 4500.9 series.

6.D.8.i  The MCMC shall obtain, stock, issue and affix placards and data plates to Navy containers IAW MIL -STD -3037, 49 CFR 452, DTR 4500.9- R Part VI and CmdO 4035.1. 
NAV- P will provide placard and data plate guidance for any situation not covered by the 
applicable publications.  
  
6.D.8.j  The MCMC shall inspect every Navy- assigned ISO freight container prior to 
embarkation or shipment IAW MIL -STD -3037 and perform necessary repairs as 
authorized by DFARS 252.217- 7028.   
  
(i) The MCMC shall establish a triage process to segregate Navy containers in categories of repair and submit an “Over and Above” Work Request IAW DFARS 252.217- 7028.  MCMC shall certify all containers with no CSC defects 
and treat any surface rust prior t o loading. Navy Containers with CSC defects or 
excessive rust shall be staged for maintenance. MCMC shall supply an acceptable itemized electronic document showing needed repairs with estimated cost(s) (CDRL 6036)  prior to being aproved by NAV -P for repair . MCMC shall not place 
any Navy owned containers in the repair cycle without approval and authorization.  
  (ii) The MCMC shall provide a schedule for Navy container maintenance and repairs IAW MIL -STD -3037. The schedule will meet production timeline 
requirements.   
  
6.D.8.k  The MCMC shall utilize the OMMS -NG module for container maintenance. 
Maintenance actions shall be entered accurately IAW maintenance requirements and actual maintenance performed. The Government will not authorize container maintenance without OMM S-NG approved work candidates. The JSN/JCN shall be clearly stated on 
the MCMC developed work request/job order routing forms. The MCMC shall close out all open container maintenance OMMS -NG work candidates within 30 calendar days of 
repair completion and associated Government acceptance.  
  
6.D.9   The MCMC shall create and complete a Service Life Data Sheet and manila tag for all natural fiber and synthetic lines in the EBL, Line Assembly, ABLTS and RRDF force module with the following information, NSN/Part #, Nomenclature, Manufacture Date or Receipt Date, In-Service Date, Assembly Number, and Name and Signature of person completing the 
information. The Manila tags shall be placed on all lines, regardless of location, and the Service Life Data Sheet shall be placed in the container when lines are put in service and a copy maintained in the warehouse line log. NSC shall maintain a copy of the Service Life Data Sheets for all lines until each individual lines are disposed of.  6.D.10
  The MCMC shall utilize the following prioritization for NSE containerized asset 
production to maximize attainment and readiness of the most critical NSE Force Modules.  
  
(a)   Expeditionary Bos’n Locker (EBL)  
(b)   High Failure Parts Container (HFP)

 6.E NAVY CIVIL ENGINEERING SUPPORT EQUIPMENT (CESE)   
  This section delineates the CESE maintenance services to be accomplished by the MCMC during MPS MMC Phases of the Maritime Prepositioning Equipment /Supplies (MPE/S) Continuum per MCO 3000.17 and TM 4790- 14/2 in support of CESE. Maintenance services execut ed under 
MCPP II shall be performed in accordance with Government approved MCMC production and delivery schedules. Maintenance Services to include but not limited to Preventative and Corrective Maintenance, Modifications, Calibration, Special Function Test s, Battery Services, 
Load Test Certification, Fuel Filtration Service, Corrosion Control, SL -3 Inventory, Shelf -Life 
and Service -Life Management, Limited Technical Inspection, Equipment Configuration, and 
Care of Supplies in Storage (COSIS) shall conform t o the specifications described within the 
SOW.  MCMC Quality Control (QC) and Government Quality Assurance (QA) measurers will be governed by the performance requirements and standards associated with applicable sections of the SOW.  
  The diversity and quantity of Navy ground military equipment covered under the scope of this section is extensive. Consequently, it is impractical to provide a complete listing of all required publications.  
  
6.E.1  The MCMC shall perform Limited Technical Inspections (LTIs) on CESE IAW TM 4700-15/1, applicable equipment TMs, TIs, MCO 4400.201 and UM 4000- 125.  LTIs shall be 
completed IAW Section C.4 disposition and investigation timelines.  LTIs are required t o 
determine equipment overall condition during equipment maintenance acceptance phase, when requesting disposition instructions and equipment investigation actions for ME and CESE assets. LTIs associated with WIR (excess & obsolete) shall contain an assign  a condition code in 
accordance MCO 4400.201. LTIs associated with investigations and beyond economic repair, shall contain repair cost estimate (labor and materials) and assign a condition code in accordance MCO 4400.201.  
  6.E.1.a  The MCMC shall perform comprehensive operational function tests on CESE, including attachments, pumping mechanisms, communications, etc. The MCMC shall 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 199 of 425 
 
 notify the Navy QAR 24 to 48 hours, via email, before performing the equipment 
operational function test outlined below. 
  
(i) The MCMC shall clean fuel containers on CESE IAW applicable equipment TMs, Defense Transportation Regulation (DTR) 4900- R, Title 49 CFR, and 
Government approved MCMC production schedules.   
6.E.2   The MCMC shall perform Preventive Maintenance Checks and Services (PMCS) for Navy CESE. This includes NCE, NSE, and as required, Expeditionary Medical Facility.    
6.E.2.a  The MCMC shall utilize the OMMS -NG module for PMCS. Maintenance actions 
shall be entered accurately IAW maintenance requirements and actual maintenance performed. The Government will not authorize PMCS without OMMS -NG approved 
work candidates. The  JSN/JCN shall be clearly stated on the MCMC developed work 
request/job order routing forms. The MCMC shall close out all open PMCS OMMS -NG 
work candidates within 30 calendar days of repair completion and associated Government acceptance.  
  6.E.3   PMCS CESE IAW applicable equipment TM, CmdO 4790.1 (MMSOP) and Government approved MCMC production schedules. Upon induction of ME into the active maintenance phase, the MCMC will perform the designated PMCS level on associated military equipment, for each occasion described in CmdO 4790.1 (MMSOP). During the PMCS actions, the MCMC will perform equipment exercise/road testing IAW the MMSOP. The service requirements established below shall be met during all levels of PMCS, regardless of level designa tion (e.g.

tests on CESE, including attachments, pumping mechanisms, communications, etc. The MCMC shall

 notify the Navy QAR 24 to 48 hours, via email, before performing the equipment

(i) The MCMC shall clean fuel containers on CESE IAW applicable equipment TMs, Defense Transportation Regulation (DTR) 4900- R, Title 49 CFR, and 
Government approved MCMC production schedules.   
6.E.2   The MCMC shall perform Preventive Maintenance Checks and Services (PMCS) for Navy CESE. This includes NCE, NSE, and as required, Expeditionary Medical Facility.    
6.E.2.a  The MCMC shall utilize the OMMS -NG module for PMCS. Maintenance actions 
shall be entered accurately IAW maintenance requirements and actual maintenance performed. The Government will not authorize PMCS without OMMS -NG approved 
work candidates. The  JSN/JCN shall be clearly stated on the MCMC developed work 
request/job order routing forms. The MCMC shall close out all open PMCS OMMS -NG 
work candidates within 30 calendar days of repair completion and associated Government acceptance.  
  6.E.3   PMCS CESE IAW applicable equipment TM, CmdO 4790.1 (MMSOP) and Government approved MCMC production schedules. Upon induction of ME into the active maintenance phase, the MCMC will perform the designated PMCS level on associated military equipment, for each occasion described in CmdO 4790.1 (MMSOP). During the PMCS actions, the MCMC will perform equipment exercise/road testing IAW the MMSOP. The service requirements established below shall be met during all levels of PMCS, regardless of level designa tion (e.g. 
“Monthly”, “Annual”) or hour/miles/rounds accumulated on equipment Elapse of Time/Distance (EOT) instrumentation. During COSIS actions, the MCMC shall only replace hydraulic and air system filters as a result of Corrective Maintenance requiremen ts. The MCMC shall test, service 
and maintain Navy equipment lead- acid batteries IAW TM 9 -6140- 200-13. 
  
6.E.3.a   The MCMC shall replace all fuel filters on CESE during PMCS functions IAW technical procedures listed in applicable equipment TMs. Change primary, secondary, and water separator fuel filters during all required MMC PMCS and Government -
approved MC MC production schedules. The MCMC mechanic that changed the fuel 
filters shall date and place his/her initials in a permanent marker on the outside of the fuel filter the date of the filter change.  
(i)  In lieu of oil and filter changing requirements contained in military equipment TM PMCS tables, the MCMC shall test the oil in engines, transmissions, transfer cases, differentials, drive axles, planetary drives, gear boxes, and hydraulic systems, and shall only replace oil and filters when contamination is detected. Additional details of this requirement are listed in CmdO 4790.1. 
  
(ii) The MCMC shall service, and replace, if necessary, the air system filter components listed below as a part of PMCS functions during every MMC Production Cycle and IAW technical procedures listed in applicable equipment 
M6700425R0007

 TMs. Services and components replacement shall be recorded as a separate task 
under the PMCS Service Request in GCSS -MC.  
  
MTVR FOV:  Dryer Air Filter & Aftercooler Filter  
 
(iii) The MCMC shall perform Government directed Service Upon Receipt (SUR) within 45 calendar days of initial receipt on all received CESE IAW technical procedures listed in applicable equipment TMs. The SUR effort will be completed IAW MCO 4790.2 and CmdO 4790.1.  
  
(iv) The MCMC shall test and service CESE engine coolant systems in 
conjunction with PMCS services IAW applicable technical procedures listed in 
TMs and Government approved MCMC production schedules. MCMC shall ensure nitrite concentration for ethylene glycol antifreeze 50/50 mixture is between 1200 and 1400 ppm. MCMC shal l record the anti -freeze type and 
protection readings as outlined in CmdO 4790.1.  
  
(v) The MCMC shall disconnect and disable all ether cylinders contained on CESE IAW applicable technical procedures listed in TMs and Government -
approved MCMC production schedules.  The ether cylinder will be capped off to prevent accidental expenditure of  the ether gas. For those cylinders that cannot 
be capped off, the MCMC shall install a new un- activated ether cylinder. The un-
activated ether cylinder shall be installed and secured in its designated bracket/holder.   
  
(vi) The MCMC shall replace lubricity fuel filters and in -line fuel strainers for 
CESE IAW technical procedures listed in applicable TMs and Government -
approved MCMC production schedules. This requirement pertains but is not limited to EC 182011 forklifts. Lubricity Fuel filters and strainers shall be replaced during MMC and every 6 months thereafter IAW equipment TM. Fuel 
filters and strainers shall be permanently marked with date changed (MM -DD-
YYYY). Service shall be recorded as a separate task under the assigned Service Request in GCSS -MC. 
  
(vii) When CESE are subject to a high- pressure wash after PMCS requirements 
have been completed, the MCMC shall apply fresh lubricant to lubrication 
service points affected by the wash, as identified in the equipment TM, 
Lubrication Order/Instruction, or data plate. For all equipment, this requirement shall be accomplished prior to placing the equipment into an “FMC” status. 
  
6.E.3.b   The MCMC shall load bank Navy owned generators and record the results IAW NAVFAC P -300 and Maintenance Requirement Card. The MCMC shall notify the NAV -
P QAR NLT 24 and 48 hours prior to load banking generators. The Navy has generator assets withi n the NSE and NCE which includes assets that are containerized. 50% of NSE 
and NCE assets will be load banked to 100% of their rated capacity and the remaining 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 201 of 425 
 
 50% will be load banked the following MMC. A Load Bank Form  (CDRL 6037)  will be 
used to record the load banking process. An example of the form is provided, see SOW 
Attachment  C.6- 023. The completed form will be filed in the equipment folder/history 
file within five business days.   
  
6.E.3.c   Portable Fire Extinguishers. When replenishing SL -3 portable fire extinguishers 
associated with Military Equipment, the MCMC shall ensure a required SL -3 
“Maintenance” SR has been established to properly reflect the SL -3 component part 
requiremen t.  For extinguishers which are an SL -3 SSRI or UURI component of parent 
ME, the MCMC shall record each portable (SL -3 component) fire extinguisher serial 
number in the “Remarks” column of the individual equipment SL -3/TM Inventory 
Extract Record for any p ortable fire extinguisher that has an assigned manufacturer’s 
serial number.  
  
(i) The MCMC shall identify, in the remarks column of the SL -3 Extract Record, 
the actual storage location of portable fire extinguishers (components of ME) that are stored away from their parent. The remarks shall reflect the actual Container Number or Ve hicle’s Navy Registration Number where the fire extinguisher is 
stowed. The MCMC shall also ensure each individual fire extinguisher that is stored away from its parent is properly captured (Log Set) in MCPIC or subsequent embarkation data AIS (e.g. IMARK, etc.).  
  6.E.3.d  Transportable fuel and water systems and their components designed with the use of ISO container fittings/couplings shall be inspected and certified IAW MCO 4690.1 (MARINE CORPS CONTAINER MANAGEMENT POLICY).  
  
 6.E.3.e  The MCMC shall perform COSIS of Class II and VII CESE assets in open 
storage IAW CmdO 4450.1. 
  
6.E.4   The MCMC shall maintain a Corrective Maintenance (CM) program IAW CmdO 4790.1, MCO 4790.2, MCO 4790.25, UM 4000- 125, SL -3s, Technical Instructions (TIs) and Technical 
Manuals (TMs). The MCMC shall identify, via a GCSS -MC service request, all CM req uired to 
return CESE assets to a Mission Capable (MC) status.    The MCMC shall perform all Corrective Maintenance for Navy CESE. This is a “Over and Above” requirement IAW DFARS 252.217- 7028 which requires prior authorization.  Corrective 
Maintenance consists of all repair actions performed, as a result of equipment fa ilure/fault, to 
restore equipment to a specified condition. The MCMC shall perform CM IAW Original Equipment Manufacture's (OEM) manuals and appropriate technical manuals to render equipment mission capable. NAV -P technical direction will be provided for N avy CESE for 
which OEM manuals and/or appropriate technical manuals are not available.   
6.E.4.a  The MCMC shall utilize the OMMS -NG module for CM. Maintenance actions 
shall be entered accurately IAW maintenance requirements and actual maintenance performed. The Government will not authorize CM without OMMS -NG approved work 
M6700425R0007

 candidates. The JSN/JCN shall be clearly stated on the MCMC developed work 
request/job order routing forms. The MCMC shall close out all open CM OMMS- NG 
work candidates within 30 calendar days of repair completion and associated Government acceptance.  
  
(i) The MCMC shall complete equipment forms and maintenance records for CESE assets prior to presenting equipment to the Government for acceptance. See 6.E.5.f for details. 
  
6.E.4.b  The MCMC shall perform fuel filtration services on CESE assets IAW Fuel Filtration TM, and Government approved MCMC production schedules. Perform filtration services on fuel contained in equipment fuel cells. Fuel cells that will not interface dir ectly with the filtration unit shall have the fuel drained, processed and 
cleaned through the local filtration unit. All fuel in newly received equipment will also be processed and cleaned through the filtration unit during the Service Upon Receipt actions .   
  
6.E.4.c  The MCMC shall wash and clean CESE assets upon completion of the production maintenance phase IAW technical procedures listed in applicable equipment TM and Government approved MCMC production schedules. Remove all mud, dirt, sand, oil and any other environmental/industrial contaminates. Operator station/cab and tool/SL -3 stowage compartments shall be clean and free of trash and debris.  
  
6.E.4.d  Lubrication Service. The Contractor shall apply fresh lubricant to all of the lubrication service points for CESE assets as shown in the equipment’s TM, lubrication order/instruction, or data plate. This requirement shall be performed after the post -
maintenance final rinse and prior to staging (rolling stock) or packing (boxed, pallet, breakbulk, or container items). 
  
6.E.4.e  The MCMC shall perform special maintenance actions for CESE assets IAW published SOUMs, Maintenance Advisory Messages (MAMs), Maintenance Information Messages (MIMs), Ground Precautionary Messages/Advisories (GPM/A), CmdO 4790.1 (MMSOP) and Government provided timelines. SOUMs, MAMs, MIMs, and GPM/As are published via Naval Message or other official communication to inform operators and maintainers of potential safety concerns and/or capability limitations (until corrected) regarding the use and employment of military equipment and supplies. If production scheduling requirements cannot meet compliance periods/timelines as published in the SOUM, MAM, MIM, GPM/A, the Government will provide alternate compliance periods/timelines via change request.    
6.E.5   Maintenance Related Programs  
  6.E.5.a  The MCMC shall execute Corrosion Control and Coating (C3) IAW CmdO 4790.1, MCO 4790.2 MCO 4790.18, TM 4795- OR-1, TM 4750- OD/1, and NAVFAC P -

maintenance timelines.

Selective Interchange Authorization Letters shall be prepared and submitted IAW CmdO 4790.1.   6.E.5.c  The MCMC shall ensure Calibration Services are completed for CESE assets during the production maintenance phase IAW UM 4000- 125, FED LOG Operational 
Test Code (OTC), the applicable TM and Government approved MCMC production schedules.  Test, Meas urement and Diagnostic Equipment (TMDE) are tools that are used 
to perform qualitative/quantitative measure and generally require control, inventory, and maintenance.  
  6.E.5.d  The MCMC shall establish and execute maintenance quality control programs IAW MCO 4790.2, TM 4700- 15-1, UM 4000- 125, and CmdO 4790.1.  
  
6.E.5.e   The MCMC shall perform Condition Code Limited Technical Inspection (LTI) for Navy CESE shipments, redistribution, and disposal IAW NAVFAC P -300 and NAV -
P Disposition Instructions.  The MCMC shall accurately complete and submit DD Form 1348- 1 and DD Form 1149 as required.  
  
6.E.5.f   The MCMC shall establish accurate maintenance records/history file for each Navy CESE PEI IAW NAVFAC P -300 and NAVFAC P -307. The MMC maintenance 
service records will be printed and filed inside an equipment folder/history file. The information wi ll include the United States Navy (USN) serial number assigned to all 
CESE, National Stock Number (NSN), Equipment Code (EC), Description of item, and is not limited, to PMCS records, Corrective Maintenance records, modification control forms, inventory forms, annual condition inspection forms, warranty records, contracted vendor services forms and records. For Navy cranes, the history file shall also include wire rope certification, hook non- destructive test form, crane condition inspection record, 
mainten ance inspection specification record, and the certification of condition inspection 
and test forms.  
  Note: The MCMC shall provide CESE equipment maintenance folder/history file(s) to the NAV- P CESE QARs NLT 1 business day after CESE is presented to the Government 
as Full Mission Capable (FMC).   6.E.5.g  The MCMC shall manage Navy technical and commercial publications by a locally developed database that meets all Government requirements IAW Section C.2 and CmdO 4790.1.  The MCMC shall identify and report Principle End Items for Navy and Commercia l assets that require publications to Director, Navy Prepositioning 
Department, via: Maintenance Management Office (MMO), Attn: NAV -P Department 
Director for Government sourcing. Every Navy publication provided to the MCMC and

 SBMCMC shall be listed on the locally developed database technical manual publication 
listing.  
  
6.E.5.h The MCMC shall perform Equipment Modifications for Navy CESE. Modifications for Navy CESE will be identified by the Government and accomplished IAW applicable modification instructions.  
  6.E.5.i   The MCMC shall perform or obtain from a third- party; Emergent Equipment 
Recovery for Navy CESE and Organic Equipment traveling to or from inspection and repair facilities outside the confines of Marine Corps Support Facility, Blount Island.  
  
6.E.5.j   The MCMC shall provide completed SF -91, accident LTI, and corresponding 
SF-94 when applicable, to the Government when Government -owned assets are involved 
in accidents IAW TM 4700.15/1 and MCO P11240.106. Requirement applies to ME and Non-Tactical Vehicles. SF -91s/SF -94s regarding Navy CESE shall be provided to the 
Government (Attention to Director NAV -P) within one business day of the accident.  
Accident LTI for tactical assets, or GSA leased assets, shall be provided with MCMC investigation (whe re required) within 15 calendar days after accident (unless constrained 
by commercial facility's delivery of estimate).  Accident LTI shall accurately document physical damages incurred. For accidents involving cranes and/or associated rigging gear, refer to section 6.Q.

applicable plans on MCPIC) during the current production period within five business days after completion of the approved production period.

IAW the Maintenance Requirements Package (MRP). Selective intermediate level maintenance may be performed depending on skill set and facility capabilities. The MCMC shall establish, with Government approval, outsourcing criteria for maintenanc e requirements beyond their 
internal capabilities. All maintenance, whether PM or CM, shall be scheduled and completed IAW the Maintenance Model, Master Schedule, Lighterage Maintenance Milestones, and relative maintenance projects. Deviations from schedul e shall be approved via Letter of Non -
Conformance.

considered an element of the WMC. The WMC shall establish a team to maintain the LARC maintenance program and LMF. The LMF and personnel may be used for dual purpose to assist with watercraft monitoring, MPFUB maintenance, and Organic

6.F.1   The MCMC shall establish and maintain a Navy Watercraft Maintenance Center (WMC) IAW 6.A.1.C with dedicated personnel for maintenance and selective repair of Navy Watercraft, including LARC and ABLTS. The MCMC shall perform all organizational level  maintenance 
IAW the Maintenance Requirements Package (MRP). Selective intermediate level maintenance may be performed depending on skill set and facility capabilities. The MCMC shall establish, with Government approval, outsourcing criteria for maintenanc e requirements beyond their 
internal capabilities. All maintenance, whether PM or CM, shall be scheduled and completed IAW the Maintenance Model, Master Schedule, Lighterage Maintenance Milestones, and relative maintenance projects. Deviations from schedul e shall be approved via Letter of Non -
Conformance.  
  
6.F.1.a  The LARC Maintenance Facility (LMF) is an on- island dedicated facility and is 
considered an element of the WMC. The WMC shall establish a team to maintain the LARC maintenance program and LMF. The LMF and personnel may be used for dual purpose to assist with watercraft monitoring, MPFUB maintenance, and Organic 
M6700425R0007

 Watercraft assigned to NAV -P boat crew. Navy Point management/coordination, LARC 
familiarization training or other maintenance and logistics functions as long as LARC 
maintenance, repair or production is not negatively impacted. Deviation requires COR approval.           
  6.F.2   The MCMC shall produce and maintain a Government -approved Navy Master Schedule.  
Navy Master Schedule is a continuous three -year schedule projecting all maintenance phases and 
milestones for all watercraft assets and ABLTS. The MCMC shall deliver a  revised Navy Master 
Schedule (CDRL 60 39) for approval within 20 business days of receiving a new or updated 
MMC schedule with changes that take effect more than nine months from receipt date. The MCMC shall deliver a revised Navy Master Schedule for appro val within ten business days of 
receiving a new or updated MMC schedule with changes that take affect less than nine months from receipt date.  
  6.F.3   The MCMC shall produce, maintain and adhere to a COR approved production schedule tailored for each ship and its associated Navy Watercraft and ABLTS. A Tailored Navy Watercraft Production Schedule  (CDRL 6040)  for each ship is due to NAV -P for approval NLT 
the WDC -III for that respective production period; deviations from Lighterage Maintenance 
Milestones are to be identified and briefed at WDC -III.  Navy Watercraft work shall be 
performed IAW approved project and Lighterage Milestones (see SOW Atta chment C.1- 3 Phase 
IV); the Navy Watercraft Production Schedule shall be accurate IAW the Navy Master Schedule. The MCMC shall provide written notification for changes to the Navy Watercraft Production Schedule baseline within five business days of identif ication.  
  The following are minimal milestone requirements:    
(a) Receive Navy Watercraft and ABLTS at Marine Corps Support Facility - 
Blount Island (MCSF -BI) and transport to MCMC Off Site facility.  
(b) Accomplish Pre -MPF Maintenance Cycle Assessment Trials (Pre- MAT).  
(c) Dry-dock craft.  
(d) Pressure wash Navy Watercraft and ABLTS.  
(e) Accomplish structural hull repairs to Navy Watercraft and ABLTS.  
(f) Perform mechanical and electrical repairs.  
(g) Blast and paint or touch- up coating systems.  
(h) Undock Navy Watercraft and ABLTS (NLT E -73).  
(i) Accomplish Post -MPF Maintenance Cycle Acceptance Trials (Post -MAT) 
waterborne tests NLT End of ship (E) E -45 day.  
(j) Present Navy Watercraft and ABLTS for Government Craft Close Out/Acceptance (NLT E -35 day).  
(k) Transport waterborne Navy Watercraft and ABLTS to MCSF -BI and stage for 
vessel backload (NLT E -24).  
 
 6.F.3.a  The MCMC shall track all Watercraft Maintenance Phase schedules relative to 
the Master Schedule and Milestones ( SOW Attachment  C.1- 3 Phase IV) and inform the 
COR when any task is in jeopardy of not completing on time. This schedule/task 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 206 of 425 
 
 assessment should occur on a regular basis and be presented during the weekly 
production meeting. The MCMC will highlight for the COR’s attention when 50% of the Phase C period of performance has been reached (E -264); and again when 75% of the 
Phase C peri od of performance has been reached (E -231). The MCMC shall present a 
detailed list of all approved work items that will not complete on time and any work items that have yet to be approved or authorized for accomplishment and present to the COR a recommend ed course of action at each of these events. Any items that will not 
meet schedule or cannot be accomplished prior to milestone date are to have a Letter of Non-Conformance submitted to the COR within 5 business days of identification. 
6.F.3.b All authorized work shall be scheduled in a manner which describes the sequence of work and identifies significant task interdependencies required to meet the requirements of the program. Scheduling in a watercraft repair environment is of critical  
importance and shall be accomplished in conjunction with the systems employed to manage material procurement and assembly. NAV -P requires a Functional Area 
Breakdown (FAB) format (refer to SOW Attachment  C.6- 039) to support defined 
reporting criteria to e xternal entities.  
6.F.4   (Phase Delta) The MCMC shall perform all preventative maintenance checks and services (PMCS) for designated Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS during each ship cycle IAW the Maintenance Requirements Package (MRP), Navy Watercraft Production  Schedule and 
Lighterage Maintenance Milestones ( SOW Attachment  C.1- 3 Phase IV).  PMCS includes, but is 
not limited to designated daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi -annual, and annual 
maintenance checks. See SOW Attachment  C.6- 024. The MCMC shall utilize the OMMS -NG 
module for PMCS. Maintenance actions shall be entered accurately IAW maintenance requirements and actual maintenance performed. The Government will not authorize PMCS without OMMS -NG approved work candidates. The JSN/JCN shall be clearly stated on the 
MCMC developed work request/job order routing forms. The MCMC shall close out all open 
PMCS OMMS -NG work candidates within 30 calendar days of repair completion and associated 
Government acceptance.  
  6.F.5   (Phase Delta) The MCMC shall accurately produce and maintain an individual Boat Information Book (BIB) for each Watercraft and ABLTS; the BIB shall be submitted to the Government for review NLT E -4. The BIB shall include at a minimum the following:   (1) Hull 
History (2) Mechanical History (3) Electrical History (4) Collateral Gear Inventory (5) Boat/Ship alterations (6) Configuration Report (7) Deferred Maintenance Report (8) Calibration Report (9) Weight Certificate History (10) Load Test Report (11) Wire Rope and Line Log (12) Corrosion Survey Report ( SOW Attachment  C.6- 025) (13) FSB Completion Log (14) INSURV Message, 
(15) Blast and Paint History and (16) Remarks/ Miscellaneous Section.  
  6.F.6   (Phase Bravo) The MCMC shall produce and order a Bill of Material (BOM) IAW MRP and WDC -II in support of Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS production.  The Bill 
of Material (BOM) is the standard parts to support each and every ship cycle. To include but not limited to POLs, filters, lamps, consumables, Demand Support Items (DSI); i.e. nuts, bolts, screws, safety items, etc. The BOM shall be on order NLT E -236, all BOM material shall be 
stored in its respective layette at NSC and called forw ard to WMC prior to production start. 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 207 of 425 
 
 BOM readiness status shall be provided upon request. One BOM per ship set shall be ordered 
using the OMMS -NG and RSupply modules.  
  
6.F.6.a  All unused BOM materials shall be inventoried at the end of production, results provided to NAV -P along with reason for non -use, then returned to the NSC for future 
re-issue.  
  6.F.6.b  BOM material shall be ordered from the Annual Sustainment Task Order.  
  
6.F.7   The MCMC shall participate in three joint Work Definition Conferences ( CDRL 6041)  
(Refer to SOW Attachment  C.1- 3). WDC -I, to be held NLT Offload + 90 O Day, and is MCMC 
scheduled and led. WDC -I identifies the As -Found condition of the watercraft, LARCs and 
ALBTS as offloaded, provides all surveys as previously defined, provides a MCMC developed Corrective  Mai ntenance recommendation, and highlights any other items or areas of concern 
relative to the identified on the Navy Watercraft and ABLTS offloa ded from the most recent 
MPS and is intended to support Phase Charlie maintenance. The maintenance strategy is based on the Pre -MATS, hull surveys, coating system surveys and configuration validation of 
SYSALTS, FSB and SOUM and includes items from the pre vious/most recent deferred 
maintenance report.  WDC -II scheduled NLT E -360 is Government scheduled and led. WDC -II 
pertains to the watercraft designated for backload to a designated ship. At the WDC -II, the 
Government provides the MCMC an authorized Mainte nance Strategy package based off of the 
information received from the MCMC at WDC -I as well as any new requirements. At WDC -III 
(E-345), the MCMC shall schedule, lead and present their project for Phase Charlie and Phase 
Delta that includes all requirements from WDC -I and WDC -II. 
  
6.F.8   The MCMC shall maintain a Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS technical library on site at the WMC IAW the latest Force Revision and publication updates (NAV -P 
provided). The MCMC is to maintain this library IAW the latest publications and F orce 
Revision. Library should include: Technical Manuals, NAVSEA Standard Items, NSTM, EOSS procedures, Reference and Builder Drawings (can be in online database), FSBs, ECNs, ShipAlts, Deviations, Safety of Use Messages, Emergent Repairs, Equipment Conces sion, Departure from 
Specification, Maintenance Requirement Package (MRP), for all Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS.  Revisions and Updates to the Technical Library shall be completed NLT 20 business days after receipt.    6.F.9   (Phase Delta) The MCMC shall maintain an onboard watercraft hardcopy technical library for each Navy Watercraft, LARCs and ABLTS. The MCMC is to maintain this library IAW the latest Force Revision and publications updates (NAV -P provided). Library to include: 
Technical Manuals and EOSS. This on- board hardcopy allows the operator/customer to perform 
in theater maintenance and repairs to the systems IAW the latest data available. The Library shall be updated by the MCMC prior to commencement of Stage 4 (E -56).  
  6.F.10   The MCMC shall maintain the NAV -P Maintenance Requirements Package (MRP) IAW 
the latest Force Revision (NAV -P-provided). Updates shall occur NLT 20 business days after 
receipt of Force Revision.  Updates shall be made NLT ten business days after i dentification of 
M6700425R0007

 CAT II or CAT III requirement(s).  The NAV -P Maintenance Requirements Package consists of 
four (4) categories: Cat I are routine reoccurring tasks, CAT II are local procedures, CAT III are 
local selective Corrective Maintenance tasks and CAT IV are situati onal requirement tasks. 
Reference Maintenance Requirements Package (See SOW Attachment  C.6-024) and INLS 
Calibration Requirements List (See SOW Attachment  C.6- 027). 
  6.F.11   The MCMC shall provide all records  (CDRL 6042)  of the results of any test, 
inspections, maintenance, or monitoring for Navy Watercraft, including LARCS and ABLTS within one business day of request from NAV -P. Documentation/Information Request. Any time 
engines or generators are operated by MCMC, regar dless of reason, a set of readings are to be 
documented on the current engine operating logs. Entries are to be legible and include the printed name of person conducting the operational test, date, and starting/ending hours from the engine operating panel.  
  6.F.12   The MCMC shall perform operator functional testing for communication systems, tactical and non -tactical, in all watercraft to include LARC(V), IAW Government approved 
production schedules and applicable TMs.  
  6.F.13   Navy Watercraft including LARCs and ABLTS current maintenance status shall be accurately input into NTCSS OMMS -NG. The MCMC shall record newly identified 
maintenance deficiencies and Pre- MAT deficiencies. The MCMC shall utilize the OMMS -NG 
module for Corrective Maintenance. Maintenance actions shall be entered accurately IAW maintenance requirements and actual maintenance performed. The Government will not authorize CM without OMMS -NG approved work candidates. The JSN/JCN shall be clearly 
stated on  the MCMC developed work request/job order routing forms. The MCMC shall close 
out all open CM OMMS -NG work candidates within 30 calendar days of repair completion and 
associated Government acceptance or completion of (Phase C, Phase D).  
  6.F.14   (Phase Bravo) Pre -MATs are the point where the Navy Watercraft, including LARCs 
and ABLTS are placed into full operational mode. This allows all concerned to identify any maintenance or repair issues. The MCMC shall schedule, provide Pre -MMC Assessment Trials 
(Pre-MAT) packages and document joint Pre -MATs for all Navy Watercraft, including LARCs 
and ABLTS ( CDRL 6043)  (see SOW Attachment  C.1- 3 Phase IV). All Pre -MAT deficiencies 
shall be annotated in and have a work candidate created in OMMS -NG. Pre -MATs will be 
performed and completed within 45 calendar days of ship download. Pre -MATs are Government 
led, MCMC assisted and may include Government, military, prime and subcontractor personnel participation. Pre -MATs shall be accurately documented IAW th e Pre- MAT form (SOW 
Attachment  C.6- 028); the MCMC NAV -P will deliver the completed Pre -MAT form to NAV- P 
five (5) business days after completion of the Pre -MAT.  
  Note: OMMS -NG Work Candidates are due within 30 calendar days.  
  6.F.15  (Phase Delta) The MCMC shall perform and document electrical and electronic grooming for each Navy Watercraft and ABLTS every ship cycle IAW the AC and DC Electrical Groom Plans ( SOW Attachment  C.6- 029) and Tailored Navy Watercraft Production Schedule.   
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP

6.F.16   (Phase Delta) The MCMC shall replace all Petroleum, Oils, and Lubricants (POL) to include filters for all Navy Watercraft, LARCS and ABLTS for every ship cycle IAW Applicable TMs and Maintenance Requirements Package and the Navy Watercraft Product ion 
Schedule.  The MMC length of time (~3 years) the PEIs are deployed exceeds all of the service life of the POLs. Therefore, all of the POLs shall be fully changed each time they return to MCSF -BImd.   
  6.F.17  The MCMC shall draw and provide lubricating oil and Jacket Water samples for laboratory analysis on offloaded INLS Watercraft during Phase Bravo.  The MCMC shall provide the Navy Watercraft Oil Analysis Report (CDRL 6044)  IAW NAVSEA Standard 009- 63 
and Caterpillar SOS testing report of Jacket Water within 5 business days of receipt from lab.  
  6.F.18  (Phase Delta) As directed, the MCMC shall perform load testing of hull fittings and “J“ -
bar davits for Navy Watercraft and ABLTS IAW applicable TMs, FSBs and Builder Drawings; load testing shall be conducted IAW the Navy Watercraft Production Sched ule.  Fittings shall 
include all INLS Bridle fittings, travel lift deck fittings, portable J -Bar davits, J -Bar davit bases, 
Warping Tug A -frame, Warping Tug deck winch, ABLTS A and B winch, Bow chute adapter, 
and Jib boom.  The MCMC shall provide Navy Wate rcraft and ABLTS Load Test 
Certification(s) (CDRL 604 5) for periodic and situational load test requirements. 
  6.F.19  (Phase Delta) The MCMC shall perform maintenance and inspection(s) on NAV -P 
identified wire and fiber rope and rigging for Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS IAW NSTM 613. The WMC shall provide the Fibrous and Wire Rope Service Life Data Sheet (CDRL 6046)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 030)

and Caterpillar SOS testing report of Jacket Water within 5 business days of receipt from lab.

bar davits for Navy Watercraft and ABLTS IAW applicable TMs, FSBs and Builder Drawings; load testing shall be conducted IAW the Navy Watercraft Production Sched ule.  Fittings shall 
include all INLS Bridle fittings, travel lift deck fittings, portable J -Bar davits, J -Bar davit bases,

identified wire and fiber rope and rigging for Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS IAW NSTM 613. The WMC shall provide the Fibrous and Wire Rope Service Life Data Sheet (CDRL 6046)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 030) and Written Report NLT E -day. Where cost effective, 
replacement may occur over testing and maintenance. Submit a written report to NAV -P with 
justification for replacement.  
  6.F.20  (Phase Delta) Post -MMC Acceptance Trials (Post -MATs) are the point where the Navy 
Watercraft and ABLTS are placed into full operational mode. This allows all concerned to verify that all maintenance and repairs were effective and identify any outstanding issues. Post -MATs 
are Government led and MCMC assisted. MCMC will accurately document the Post -MAT 
results IAW the Post -MAT Form (CDRL 6043)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 028) and provide to 
NAV- P within five business days after completion of the Post -MAT for each craft. Deficiencies 
noted during Post -MATS where possible will be corrected prior to Government craft acceptance 
(typically E -30). Deficiencies not corrected  will be documented and included in the Watercraft 
End of Shi p Report ( CDRL 6047)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 026) as recommended to be deferred 
maintenance items.  
  The MCMC shall schedule Post -MATs and provide Post -MAT packages for Navy Watercraft, 
including LARCs and ABLTS IAW Master Schedule and Lighterage Milestones NLT E -56 (see 
SOW Attachment  C.1- 3 Phase IV). Post -MMC Trials may include Government, military, prime 
and subcontractor personnel.

 6.F.21  (Phase Charlie) The MCMC shall perform NAV -P approved corrective maintenance to 
all MPF Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS IAW applicable TMs, drawings or 
Maintenance Requirements Package (Categories I, II, III & IV); OPNAVINST 4790.4 Ships  
Maintenance and Material Management AWS D1.1 through D1.6 Structural Welding Code, American Bureau of Shipping (ABS) Rules of Building and Classing Steel Vessels, MIL -STD -
2035, and NAVSEA Standard Items, to include all Tier I, II and III references. Corre ctive 
Maintenance functional areas include but are not limited to: hull, mechanical, electrical, hydraulics, electronic repairs, corrosion control and craft equipage. CM is maintenance that is corrective in nature and repairs resulting from the Pre -MAT or Post -MAT inspections, surveys, 
condition- based assessments, operational tests, time directed repairs or other diagnostic means 
that determine repair actions are warranted to ensure safe and reliable operation of the system and equipment. Known non- emergent  (not time/process sensitive) intermediate and depot level 
Corrective Maintenance actions shall be scheduled for Phase Charlie. O level Corrective Maintenance actions shall be scheduled during Phase Delta.  
  This is an “Over and Above” task which requires prior authorization IAW DFARS 252.217-7028.  In addition to any MCMC developed work request/job order routing forms, the OMMS -
NG module shall be used to document all Corrective Maintenance requests/actions/completions.  Maintenance actions shall be entered accurately IAW maintenance requirements and actual maintenance performed. The Government will not authorize corrective maintenance without OMMS -NG approved work candidates. The JSN/JCN/WCN shall be clearly s tated on the 
MCMC developed work request/job order routing forms. The MCMC shall close out all open Watercraft OMMS -NG work candidates within 30 calendar days of repair completion and 
associated Government acceptance.  
  
6.F.21.a  During performance of maintenance work efforts in any phase, when additional, associated work requirements have been identified by the MCMC or Government that are associated with, but not covered within the existing authorized work item, the newl y 
discovered items on Navy Watercraft shall be communicated to the COR based on a Condition Found Report (CFR). The required work shall be performed IAW the applicable directives for the condition found, and Government direction. This is an “Over and Above ” task which requires prior authorization of the Contracting Officer. The 
MCMC shall submit the Craft Condition Found Report  (CDRL 6048)  (CFR) (SOW 
Attachment C.6 -048) NLT five (5) business days after identification of the condition.  
 
6.F.22  (Phase Delta) The MCMC shall provide weight certificates IAW NAVSUP P -307 for all 
Navy Watercraft and ABLTS with all dry and as -loaded weights NLT E -21. The craft weight 
certificate shall be accurate within 1000 lbs. In addition, the MCMC shall utilize the Navy Watercraft and ABLTS Weight Calculation Form ( SOW Attachment  C.6- 031) as supporting 
documentation. Weight calculation form shall include current sounding levels and actual gallons, and weight of liquid contained within fuel tank(s), ballast tanks, and oily waste tanks.  
  6.F.23  (Phase Delta) The MCMC shall deliver all Navy Watercraft and ABLTS IAW Navy Watercraft production Schedule to the departing ship with an optimal amount of JP -5 fuel as 
indicated:  MPFUB (285 gallons per tank); LCM -8 (520 gallons per tank); INLS WT (400- 450 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 211 of 425 
 
 gallons); CFPMs (400- 450 gallons); BMs (200- 250 gallons); ABLTS (17 gallons); LARCs (65 
gallons per tank); CCS (95 gallons).   
  6.F.24  (Phase Delta) The MCMC shall submit an accurate Navy Watercraft and ABLTS Fuel Status Report ( CDRL 6049)  (See SOW Attachment C.6- 032) NLT E -21 day (see SOW 
Attachment C.1 -3 Phase IV).  
  6.F.25  (Phase Delta) As required, the MCMC shall replace lube oil and hydraulic hoses on all Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS IAW NAVSEA Publication S6430- AE-TED -
010 and the NAVSEA Critical Hose List. See ( SOW Attachment C.6 -033).  
  6.F.26  The MCMC shall provide the NAV -P Director a "Selective Interchange Request Letter" 
(CDRL 60 50) (SOW Attachment  C.6- 034) for Navy Watercraft, LARCs and ABLTS prior to 
performing ANY exchange of parts.  
  6.F.27  (Phase Charlie) The MCMC shall perform corrosion control and coating systems repairs to Navy Watercraft, LARCs and ABLTS IAW NAVFAC 1600471 INLS Paint Schedule and Deck Covering, NAVFAC LARC Drawing 1600901- 4-A, MPFUB DWG 4033 -650-001-D, 
NAVSEA STD Item 009 -32, NSTM 631, NSTM 634 and the Navy Watercraft Production 
Schedule.  Navy Watercraft are to be blasted and painted at a minimum of every six years, upon paint system failures in excess of 25% of panel total area of the watercraft.  Instances wher e 
paint system failures do not exceed 25% but the corrosion is significant enough that structural integrity may be suspect or impacting to operational performance, the MCMC shall submit a CFR for Government review and approval. LARCs, MPFUBs, LCM, and INLS  may be repaired 
utilizing the MCSF -BI Corrosion Repair Facility (CRF) or be repaired utilizing 3rd party 
facilities. The MCMC shall submit the Corrosion Survey Report(s) ( SOW Attachment C.6 -025). 
Refer to  INLS/MPFUB/LARC/LCM Paint Schedule and Deck Drawi ngs ( CDRL 6051)  (SOW 
Attachment C.6 -035) as required.  
  Areas less than 25% of a panel are considered touch -up and touch- up is defined as preservation 
operations on cumulative surface areas less than 25% of the total area of a specific panel (e.g. deck, side shell, underwater hull bottom, bulkhead, tank wall, e tc.) being preserved, with no 
individual spot area greater than 10 square feet (1440 square inches). Touch -up painting can 
occur or be performed during Phase C, Phase D, or while in a COSIS status.   This is an “Over and Above” task which requires prior authorization IAW DFARS 252.217-7028.  In addition to any MCMC developed work request/job order routing forms, OMMS -NG 
module shall be used to document all corrosion control and coating systems repairs requests/actions/completions. The Government will not authorize maintenance without OMMS -
NG approved work candidates. The JSN/JCN shall be clearly stated on the MCMC developed work request/job order routing forms The MCMC shall close out all open corrosion control and coating systems repairs OMMS -NG work candidates within 30 calendar days of repair 
completion and associated Government acceptance. 
  
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP

 6.F.27.a  The MCMC shall provide joint quality checkpoints for coating system repairs 
on Navy Watercraft. Minimum Checkpoints include but not limited to: Cleanliness, Surface profile, Prime Coat Dry Film Thickness (DFT), Topcoat DFT IAW Navy Watercraft Checkpoints for Coatin g System Repairs Completed Paint Report ( CDRL 
6052)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 036). The MCMC shall participate in checkpoints for each 
craft and shall provide the Government with 24- hour notice for all checkpoints. The 
MCMC shall submit the Navy Watercraft Check points for Coating Systems Repairs 
Completed Paint Report IAW NAVSEA Standard 009- 32 NLT two business days after 
completion of final paint inspection/acceptance.  
  6.F.27.b  The RST shall manage watercraft coating system maintenance utilizing the MCSF -BI CRF (Depot Level). Organizational coating system maintenance is managed 
by the respective MCMC, whether WMC or OST.   
6.F.28   (Phase Bravo through Delta) The MCMC shall inventory, order, replace/repair, operationally test and maintain as necessary all outfitting/equipage for Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS IAW applicable reference documents. In the event a cra ft departs 
MCSF -BI for maintenance or if the presence of craft equipage interferes with the scope of work 
for a designated area, all craft equipage shall be removed (disassociated) from craft, inventoried, and properly stowed, including labeling to maintai n set integrity. During Phase Bravo, the 
MCMC shall inspect and operationally test all craft equipage. A list of all items requiring replacement shall be provided to NAV -P complete with replacement cost estimate. When 
authorized, the replacement parts shal l be ordered. When received, items will be labeled and 
stowed with the appropriate craft assigned equipage on hand. During Phase Delta (Stages 1 and 2) perform maintenance, repair, and refurbish as necessary, all craft equipage. During Stage III, all craft  equipage shall be re- associated with the respective craft for final inspection during Post -
MATs. The MCMC shall accurately report and submit the appropriate Pre -MAT and Post -MAT 
Forms  (CDRL 6043)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 028) and Navy Watercraft, LARCs and ABLTS 
Collateral Gear Inventory List ( CDRL 6053)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 037) within five business 
days of completion.  
  6.F.29  The OST shall provide all movement services for Navy Watercraft and ABLTS IAW NAV- P approved Navy Watercraft and ABLTS Movement Schedule and NAV -P provided 
docking plans.  Navy Watercraft and ABLTS movement includes but is not limited to movement on land, movement on water, ship cross -decking and docking/undocking (recovery 
lifting/launching between land/water).  
  
6.F.29.a  The MCMC shall create, maintain and provide updates for the NAV -P approved 
Navy Watercraft and ABLTS Movement Schedule; revisions shall be submitted NLT three business days prior to changes in the movement schedule. Navy Watercraft and ABLTS move ment schedule (CDRL 6054)  identifies all of the watercraft and ABLTS 
movement during the ships cycle.  It will identify dates and locations where craft are currently located and three to six weeks projections necessary to meet schedule requirements. The Navy Watercraft and ABLTS Movement Schedule shall be accurate IAW the Navy Watercraft Production Schedule.  
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 213 of 425 
 
   
6.F.30  The MCMC shall perform and report shipping of Navy Watercraft and ABLTS 
equipment. All shipping directions and timelines will be provided in writing by NAV -P. The 
MCMC shall provide NAV -P copies of the Navy and ABLTS equipment shipping document(s). 
  6.F.31  The MCMC shall perform all work to accomplish known FSBs, Deviations, SYSALTs, Class Advisories, and Safety of Use Messages on Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS. The MCMC shall submit electronic and hardcopy of Notice of Completion (NOC) documentation ( CDRL 6055)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 038) NLT five business days upon task 
completion. New requirements require Contractor analysis and Government direction prior to incurring any costs.  
  6.F.32  The MCMC shall support the prototype and operational testing pursuant to maintenance and associated data collection for maintenance of Navy Watercraft Ashore, INLS, ABLTS, MPFUB, LCM, and LARC in support of major system alterations and special proj ects. 
  6.F.33  The MCMC shall report on and perform emergent repairs or services to Navy Watercraft.  Emergent Repairs are defined as “An immediate repair/service required to protect life or property or environment or time sensitive operation.”  The MCMC shall notify NAV- P within 
one hour of identification of emergent repair or services requirement for Contracting Officer/Government approval.    6.F.34  (Phase Bravo through Delta) The MCMC shall perform "Organizational Level" corrosion control and coating systems maintenance to Navy Watercraft, LARCs and ABLTS IAW NAVFAC 1600471 INLS Paint Schedule and Deck Covering, NAVFAC LARC Drawing 1600901- 4-A, MPFUB DWG 4033 -650-001-D, NAVSEA STD Item 009 -32, NSTM 631, 
NSTM 634 and the Navy Watercraft Production Schedule. Navy Watercraft coating systems spot maintenance shall be performed to prevent premature system wide degradation/ deterioration, which left  unattended, leads to Depot Level maintenance. Spot maintenance is considered to be 
paint system failures equating to less than ~25% of hull panel total area with no individual spot area being greater than 10 square feet. The MCMC shall determine in collab oration with the 
Government the size and scope of area to be painted to present military appearance compared to area of paint system failure.
 Instances where paint system failures do not exceed 25% but the 
corrosion is significant enough that structural integrity may be suspect or impacting to operational performance, the MCMC shall submit a CFR for Government consideration as a Depot Level repair. Spot preservation shall be annotated on the Corrosion Survey Report(s) (SOW Attachment  C.6- 025) and submitted to the  Government for review and approval. Refer to 
the MRP, Cat II SWI for Organizational Level Spot Preservation procedures. This applies to both internal and external hull panels.    
6.F.34.a  The MCMC shall perform continuous and persistent Organizational Level Coating System maintenance (both internal/external) in the following priority:  
(i) Shipset in production/being backloaded  
(ii) Organic equipment  
(iii) Watercraft in maintenance rotation

6.F.34.b  Watercraft and organic assets not designated or scheduled for depot level 
repairs on coating systems shall be maintained in the highest state of readiness to mitigate progressive corrosion

 Instances where paint system failures do not exceed 25% but the

Spot preservation shall be annotated on the Corrosion Survey Report(s) (SOW Attachment  C.6- 025) and submitted to the  Government for review and approval. Refer to 
the MRP, Cat II SWI for Organizational Level Spot Preservation procedures. This applies to both internal and external hull panels.    
6.F.34.a  The MCMC shall perform continuous and persistent Organizational Level Coating System maintenance (both internal/external) in the following priority:  
(i) Shipset in production/being backloaded  
(ii) Organic equipment
