 (iv) Other Navy assets as assigned  
 
Note: The MCMC shall solicit individual asset priority from the NAV -P Deck QAR.  
6.F.34.b  Watercraft and organic assets not designated or scheduled for depot level 
repairs on coating systems shall be maintained in the highest state of readiness to mitigate progressive corrosion, coating system degradation leading to depot level mainte nance and 
an un- kept military appearance. Spot preservation after Depot Level coating system 
repairs may also be necessary in some cases to maintain paint system integrity.  
6.F.34.c  The MCMC WMC and OST shall provide Lighterage Finisher work schedule status information to NAV -P during the weekly production meeting.  
6.F.34.d  Coating system supplies, tools/equipment and paint shall be maintained in sufficient quantity on island and at designated offsite locations to prevent work delays.  
6.F.34.e  Costs associated with replenishment of supplies, tools, equipment and paint shall be detailed in the EAC.  
6.F.34.f  Organizational Level coating system maintenance on parts, pieces and components, both interior and exterior, shall be accomplished based on the coating system survey, craft inspection or as directed by the Government. The 25% surface area criteri a does not apply to: parts, pieces and Components. Coating system maintenance 
shall apply to operability, service life functionality and military appearance as criteria for prioritization and accomplishment.
  
6.F.34.g  Intermediate and Depot Level coating system maintenance, as assigned, shall be accomplished IAW C.6 -025 Intermediate/Depot Level Coating System Spot Preservation 
Criteria which details existing coating system “spot preservation” criteria and proc edures.  
6.F.35  The MCMC shall maintain all Navy Watercraft, LARCs and ABLTS in a satisfactory, clean, and usable condition IAW Section C.9 Port Operations, NSTM 583, INLS SOM and in compliance with USDA Regulations. The Navy Watercraft, including LARCs and ABLTS shall remain clean, dewatered and free from debris and loose material/liquid to pass agricultural inspection.    
 
 
6.F.36   (Phase Bravo or Delta) The MCMC shall perform high pressure washing of all Navy 
Watercraft IAW the Lighterage Milestones and Maintenance Model.  High pressure washing (minimum 3500- 5000 PSI) is required if Navy Watercraft and ABLTS is removed from  
deployed environment, after removal from saltwater to prevent progressive corrosion, prior entering the CRF to remove chlorides or salts and prior to redeployment to remove any dirt or pests from being deployed on the vessel.   
 
 
6.F.37   The MCMC shall create, maintain and execute an NAV -P approved "Destructive 
Weather Plan" (DWP) with actions and milestones for Navy Watercraft, including LARCs.  In the event of a "Hurricane" or any major weather event the MCMC shall make all of t he necessary 
preparations to minimize the possible damage to the Navy Watercraft. The Plan will include the maintenance of all support gear required to execute the DWP.  The DWP will be reviewed/revised on an annual basis NLT 1 May.  The MCMC shall ensure that necessary support gear inventory is accurate and equipment serviceable IAW approved DWP.  
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 215 of 425 
 
   
6.F.38  The MCMC shall establish and maintain a tool control program for Navy Watercraft Maintenance Center, including the LMF, NSC, CSS and RST.  Navy Watercraft, LARCS and ABLTS Tools and Test Equipment (TTE) are required to perform maintenance on the individual systems. All tools are to be checked in and out of the MCMC managed tool rooms and accounted for, properly surveyed and replaced as necessary with NAV -P approval.
 
 
6.F.39  RESERVED  
 6.F.40  RESERVED  
 6.F.41  The MCMC shall comply with Hazardous Material/Hazardous Waste management in accordance with Section 6.T.
 
 
6.F.42   The MCMC shall allow NAV -P personnel and other Government, Sailors, and 
authorized Contractors with specialized training the right to accomplish work at the MCMC’s facility, concurrently with MCMC operations; NAV -P personnel and other Government 
personnel/contractors will not impede MCMC operations without prior notice. On a case -by-case 
basis, NAV- P will identify work that is necessary  to be performed by U.S. Navy or other 
designated Contractor personnel. The MCMC shall provide access within 24 hours of NAV -P 
notification. This work may be performed within the MCMC core production schedule.  All U.S. Navy and civilian Contractor personnel will be approved by NAV -P Director prior to starting 
any work. 
 
 
6.F.43  (Phase Charlie) The MCMC shall provide advance email notification to NAV -P of 
scheduled QA checks required to be performed on Watercraft and ABLTS.  For locations less than 50 mile s from MCSF -BI 24 -hour notice is required. For locations greater than 50 miles 
from MCSF -BI, 72 hours’ notice is required. For locations out of continental U.S. ten business 
days’ notice is required.
 
 
6.F.44  (Phase Bravo thru Delta) The MCMC shall monitor and maintain all assigned Navy Watercraft IAW Watercraft Monitoring Requirements ( CDRL 6056)  (SOW Attachment  C.6-
041), regardless of CONUS location. Navy Watercraft will be designated in a “Monitoring” status if not scheduled for a maintenance action with in 30 calendar days. The Monitoring 
program includes systematic inspections, detections and corrections of discrepancies, including documentation, for Navy Watercraft that is not in use, assigned to fill a current MPS production cycle or is openly stored pending a future maintenance requirement or pending maintenance phase. SOW Attachment  C.6- 041 shall be submitted to NAV -P for review and approval.
 
 
6.F.45   Shall participate with and perform Joint Limited Technical Inspection (JLTI) to support custody transfer/turnover to/from military units directed by the Government. Refer to Navy Watercraft and ABLTS JLTI Custody Transfer Package ( SOW Attachment  C.6- 044). 
 
  
6.F.46  (Phase Bravo & Delta) The MCMC shall perform procedures associated in preparing craft (INLS, MPFUB, LCM8 and ABLTS) for MPS embarkation ISO MPS backload conducted at MCSF -BI IAW Vessel Offload/Backload Letter of Instruction (LOI) provided by BICmd 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 216 of 425 
 
 Operations Department. Procedures shall be performed IAW the applicable TMs and 
Engineering Operating Sequencing System (EOSS). Prior to embarkation events of MPF vessels, the Navy Watercraft and ABLTS shall be configured for embarkation and custody turnover to the NWAMS Contractor ISO Phase Alpha afloat phase. The MCMC shall perform the following preparatory tasks at a minimum: 1) For backloads: Prepare Navy Watercraft and ABLTS for shipboard embarkation (backload) and proper configuration IAW the ship’s l oad plan/drawings. 
Preparation includes the following: A -Frame positioning, lower navigation light mast, install or 
remove lifelines, connect or disconnect shore power at MCSF -BI (Navy Point or Berth 3) 
locations, remove vents, covers, and install blanks based on projected ship’s load plan, meet fuel requirements, deballast, remove anemometer, remove SATNAV antenna, remove bell. 2) For offloads, perform to Government approved schedule for custody transfer of watercraft, LARC and ABLTS from the NWAMS Contrac tor. The MCMC ensure watertight/hull integrity checks 
and make emergent repairs as necessary in preparation for Pre- MATs. 3) For offload and 
backload: Provide and coordinate tug services for Navy Watercraft and ABLTS movement to include at a minimum (Licen sed Tug Captain and Deck Hand) to safely transport craft to or from 
MPS, to include making up tug to craft, utilizing adequate fendering and mooring at either the MPS or shore side berthing location. Additionally, the MCMC shall provide notice to the tug service provider that U.S. Government and military have the right to embark onboard the tug to 
perform Government functions in support of backloads, offloads and cross decking operations. 4) For backloads/offloads: MCMC MPS access is not anticipated but may be required at the Government’s direction and prior notice will be provided. 5) For offload and backload: Provide trained LARC operator and one guide/deckhand to operate LARC craft and drive them onto/off from the ship as required for offload/backload ope rations. 6) Once custody transfer is complete, 
perform outfitting tasks to ensure craft are safe for MCMC access.

 operational checks five to seven workdays prior to embarkation or disembarkation events 
 for the HLTs, MBH, forklifts, etc. that may be utilized during ship embarkation/  
 disembarkation or scheduled portage events. The MCMC shall develop and maintain pre -
 operational check sheet/ODCLs.

authorized by  Government to perform but were not accomplished or finished prior to 
MPS/ROS backload. Included with this worklist shall be an inventory of unused parts/materials that are available for regain into stock for future reuse.

(CDRL 6057) , organized by ship and the watercraft (in the FAB format) assigned to that ship. 
The report should provide the status of percentage of tasks completed and OTD for Phase B,

  6.F.49  The MCMC shall safeguard all assigned lighterage/watercraft at all times to include the following basic measures:  
(a) Protect the vessel from fire, flooding, wind and rain damage.  
(b) Protect the vessel from the activities of maintenance personnel or maintenance actions leading to collateral damage or progressive rework in surrounding areas.  
(c) Protect the vessel from dirt, debris, clutter, water and salt accumulation.  
(d) Protect the vessel from physical security violations and provide safeguards against unauthorized entry, malicious mischief, theft or sabotage.  
(e) Protect the vessel from waterborne damage, while berthed or towed.  
 
Additionally, the MCMC shall provide, maintain and enforce a written security plan that addresses the above bullets, as well as the following NSI 009- 72 items; 3.2.5, 3.2.5.1, 3.2.5.2, 
3.2.5.5, 3.2.5.6, 3.2.8.5, 3.2.9.5, 3.3.2 and Note 5 a -c and Note 6 a -c. This plan is applicable for 
the WMC as well as any other offsite watercraft maintenance locations. Deviations from the plan require Government approval.       6.F.50  The MCMC shall schedule, support, participate in, and document watercraft Operational Tests (OPTEST) for all Navy Watercraft. OPTESTs shall be performed as scheduled and shall include Government, military, prime and any MCMC personnel. OPTESTs shal l be accurately 
documented IAW the provided OPTEST form  (CDRL 6058) ; the MCMC shall deliver the 
completed OPTEST form within five calendar days after completion. OPTESTS include but are 
not limited to Technical Refresh, Major SYSALTS, FSBs, Water Quality S ystem, Mid Life 
Upgrades or other SYSCOM field testing or prototypes.    6.F.51  (Phase Delta) The MCMC shall stage and deliver Navy Watercraft ship sets with their respective Readiness Report for ship backload NLT E+3 IAW the Navy Watercraft spread -load, 
WDC Packages, Planning Considerations, and Lighterage Milestones. MCMC format is acceptable for  the Readiness Report  (CDRL 6059) . The Readiness Report shall report readiness 
on staging, backload preparations, fuel status, weight and any pertinent remarks on craft status that should be turned over to the AMA or included in the Ops Advisory.    6.F.52  The MCMC shall have a system in place at the WMC to manage the hazardous material inventory by authorized use list, shelf -life, and quantities to ensure efficient use of hazardous 
material. Inventory inspections shall be conducted monthly and a report IAW 40 CFR submitted to the Government within 5 business days.
 
 
 6.F.52.a  The MCMC shall manage all waste generated at WMC IAW 40 CFR. Also 
provide to the Government copies of all hazardous waste manifest of waste shipped for 
 disposal.  
 
6.F.53  Upon completion of final craft close -out/Government acceptance (E -7) the MCMC shall

6.F.54  The MCMC shall utilize the Functional Area Breakdown (FAB) format:  Each watercraft will have functional areas that have a direct impact on whether that craft is Full Mission Capable (FMC), Partial Mission Capable (PMC), or Non- Mission Capable (NMC).  
6.F.54.a Functional areas include Craft Equipage, Deck Equipment, Damage Control, 
Coating System(s), Habitability, Auxiliaries, Hull/Structural, Electrical, Communications 
 /Navigation, Machinery Control System, Ship’s Service Diesel Generator (SSDG), 
Propulsion, Main Propulsion Diesel Engine (MPDE).  All craft systems and sub-components fall under one of the functional areas. Not all watercraft have all of these system capab ilities, and where they do not exist on a craft type, those absent delivery 
require ment(s) for that craft type are thus waived.  
6.F.54.b  All weekly/bi -weekly reporting requirements within this document relative to 
USN Watercraft, LARCs and ABLTS will report on craft status, areas/items of concern, and efforts to mitigate in order to render PMC and NMC areas fully FMC. Reports will  
 be formatted to include details specific to each applicable FAB functional area, including 
sub systems and components (if not FMC) on every watercraft, LARC and ABLTS assigned for the current production period. Any FAB functional areas not FMC at the end of Phase D Stage 4 (typically E -30), will require a Letter of Non -Conformance from the 
MCMC detailing their efforts to produce the capability set that meets task order delivery requirements.  
Note: Refer to SOW Attachment  C.6- 039 for Functional Area Breakdown logic.

 6.H   NAVY WATERCRAFT CONTROLS SYSTEM SUPPORT (CSS)   
  6.H.1  (Phase Charlie) The MCMC shall conduct Technical Refresh (TR) Alterations on designated Navy Watercraft.  
  
6.H.1.a  The MCMC shall procure equipment defined in the Navy provided Technical Refresh (TR) Alteration Package ( SOW Attachment  C.6- 046) for each designated 
watercraft IAW the Watercraft Master Schedule when directed by NAV -P.   
  6.H.1.b  The MCMC shall assemble purchased components into TR installation kits by serialized hull number for Navy Watercraft IAW TR Alteration Package. TR kits shall be assembled NLT 30 calendar days prior to the scheduled installation.   6.H.1.c  The MCMC shall issue and deliver completed TR kits to the installation group for designated Navy Watercraft NLT five calendar days prior to installation IAW the Master Schedule. Note: Control System Support (CSS) Shipping Report ( SOW 
Attachment  C.6- 047) shall be used for shipping to Fleet Concentration Areas.  The 
MCMC shall complete Control System Support Issue Receipts and Control System 
Support Delivery Receipts as required.  
  6.H.1.d  The MCMC shall provide an accurate Control System Support Monthly TR Inventory, Kitting, and Delivery Status Report (CDRL 6061)  NLT the fifth business day 
of every month.  
  6.H.1.e  The MCMC shall install and integrate TR alteration kits on designated Navy MPS Navy Watercraft IAW TR Alteration Package, the Navy Watercraft Master Schedule and individual watercraft production schedule. Installation shall be completed in no more  than 50 business days. The MCMC shall ensure successful completion of 
control system integration testing and provide the Notice of Completion ( CDRL 6055)  
(NOC) (SOW Attachment  C.6- 038) to NAV -P NLT five business days after testing is 
complete. The MCMC sh all complete the Craft Condition Found Report ( CDRL 6048)  
(CFR) (SOW Attachment  C.6- 048) as required when actual configuration does not 
comply with the respective drawings.

Sail List prior to getting underway.

 6.I   NAVY EXEMPTIONS, DEVIATIONS AND WAIVERS  
  Many of the specific requirements referenced in contract resource documents are written for operational Navy units/forces and do not universally apply to the MPF program. Detailed below is an accounting of exemptions and standing waivers, along with explan ations, releasing the 
MCPP II MCMC from strict adherence to those requirements. The MCPP II MCMC shall adhere to these exemptions and waivers. Specific applicable exemptions and waivers or tailoring follow:  
  6.I.1  3M Inactive Equipment Maintenance (Lay -up): Navy semi -annual Force Revision 
requirements will be tailored via NAV -P Maintenance Requirements Package (MRP) process for 
Standard Work Items (SWI).  
  6.I.2  Compass swing (Variation/Deviation): Navy semi -annual Force Revision requirements are 
waived.

via 6.F.17.  
  6.I.4   Diesel Engine Inspection Program: Navy semi -annual Force Revision requirements are 
waived.  
  6.I.5   Gauge Calibration Requirements: Modified IAW SYSCOM direction. Calibration for watercraft will be conducted IAW NSWCCD CRL Letter 93/115 dated 01 Dec 2014 or its successor. (See SOW Attachment  C.6- 027).  
 6.J   NAVY SHIPBOARD SUPPORT  
  This Section delineates the surveillance, maintenance, and logistics services to be accomplished by the MCMC during Afloat Phase for Navy equipment per MCO P3000.17 and TM 4790- 14/2. 
This section does not apply to other Navy Contractor services performed a board MPS. Any 
conflicts or confusion should be resolved by the respective Contractors Squadron or Ship Supervisors. If necessary, elevate to the Navy COR.      Additionally, this Section identifies the services that shall be performed to issue and recover Maritime Prepositioning Navy Equipment and Supplies (MPE/S) during Maritime Prepositioning Ships (MPS) Operations.  Work performance shall include places of debarkation throughout the world, during MPS exercises, contingencies, or other types of scheduled and unscheduled downloads, as directed by the Government. Logistics Services (Preventative and Corrective Maintenance, Modifications, Calibration, Maintenance M anagement, Battery Services, Shelf -
Life Management, Inventory Management, Limited Technical Inspection, Equipment 
Configuration, Exercise Support, Shipboard Reports, and surveillance shall conform to the specifications described within the SOW. Shipboard l ogistics services aboard MPS, governed by 
the MCPP II contract shall be performed in accordance with the afloat phase surveillance schedule.   
  6.J.1  The MCMC shall ensure all KSDs supporting the transfer of equipment in support of an exercise, contingency, temporary loan or transfer to another unit/contractor, are uploaded into RSupply, as applicable. Supporting KSDs include the Exercise Support  Letter, 
Outgoing/Incoming Consolidated Asset List with proper signatures, DD Form 1348s, DD Form 1149 (if required), CMRs (if required), or any other documents used to ensure proper chain of custody.    6.J.2  The MCMC shall perform surveillance for assigned Navy MPE/S during MPS afloat phase.  
  6.J.3  The MCMC shall manage the physical inventory of all assigned Navy MPE/S during the afloat phase, maintaining accurate accountability within the appropriate APSR IAW TM 4790-14/2, MCO 3000.17, MCO 4400.201, MCO P4400.151, and UM 4000- 125.

(CDRL 6082)  (SOW Attachment  C.6- 010) of all Navy weapons onboard MPF Ships stating the 
actual physical inventory.  Navy Small Arms Monthly Inventory Report shall be endorsed and validated by the RO. The completed Small Arms Monthly Inventory Report is to be scanned (PDF) and submitt ed to NAV- P NLT the 10th business day of each month.  
  6.J.4.a  The MCMC shall conduct logistics support operations during the offload and backload of MPS. The MCMC shall transport Navy weapons to/from the ship and MCSF -BI armory IAW LOIs, POA&Ms, and Government guidance. A joint weapons’ 
inventory shall be conducted during every custody transfer.  
  6.J.4.b  The MCMC shall maintain positive chain of custody and accountability of all assigned Navy ME during exercise and contingency operations IAW MCO 4400.201, TM 4790- 14/2, MCO 3000.17.  
  
(i) The MCMC shall execute temporary loans of authorized Navy CESE when issuing MPF prepositioning program equipment to the operating forces in support of exercises or contingencies IAW MCO 4400.201, CmdO 4790.1, UM 4000- 125, 
and TM 4790- 14/2.   
  (ii) The MCMC shall make adjustments to Government -provided Consolidated 
Asset Listing (CAL) (Exercise CAL) during the conduct of temp loaning Navy CESE to the operating forces in support of exercises and operations utilizing prepositioned equipment and supplies from MPS IAW TM 4790- 14/2.   
  
6.J.5  The MCMC shall perform Operator Level PMCS on Navy MPE/S assets IAW applicable equipment operator manuals. As applicable per equipment TM, complete Operator (before, during, and after) PMCS during Semi -Annual start -ups (SASU) for square loaded rolli ng stock 
and non- rolling stock (e.g. containers, generators, etc.). PMCS shall be completed every six 
months beginning 60 calendar days after MMC or upon completion of an MPS backload operation. The MCMC shall develop a local form and record PMCS actions r equired IAW the 
equipment technical manual for Navy MPE/S. The PMCS record/form shall be locally generated and contain the following minimal information: EC, Serial Number, deficiencies identified, date, and signature of the individual who completed the PM CS 
  
6.J.6  Battery Maintenance. The MCMC shall test, service, and maintain Navy equipment batteries for square loaded MPE/S IAW TM 9 -6140- 200-13 and the MCMC Battery 
Maintenance Plan.  Battery testing and servicing shall be performed in conjunction with schedu led PMCS and prior to MPS offloads.  The comprehensive battery maintenance plan shall 
be sufficient in scope and effort to minimize battery failure due to storage configuration by ensuring that equipment batteries are in a ready condition.  
   6.J.7  Navy Surveillance/Stock Checks.

 6.J.7.a  Square loaded assets. The MCMC shall preform visual surveillance in all holds 
and compartments containing embarked Navy MPE/S. Visually inspect Navy MPE/S for safety and security problems, fuel level, leaks, flat/low tires, excessive corrosion, damage, pilferage, or loss. The MCMC shall develop a local form to document stock 
check results to include item (EC), description, serial number, location, date, defect found, and signature of inspector. MPE/S, to include weapons and assets in the armory, shall be visually checked every 30 calendar days, starting 30 calendar days after load 
completion.   
  
Note:  The MCMC shall maintain Navy CESE Fuel Levels equal to ¾ tank as required during initial embarkation. Coordinate accounting data with the Navy COR/ACOR.  Generate requests for fuel issue from ship's masters by completing a DD1149. Class of ship will vary in availability of fuel. 
  
6.J.7.b  The MCMC shall replace lubricity fuel filters for Navy Extended Boom Fork Lift (EBFL) IAW Filter Manufacturer Instructions. The lubricity fuel filters shall be replaced every 250 hours or 6 months after last replacement, whichever comes first.  Fuel filters and strainers shall be permanently marked with date changed (MM -DD-YYYY).  Service 
shall be recorded as a separate task under the PMCS Service Request in GCSS -MC.  
Validate lubricity fuel filters that have been replaced by operating forces durin g post -
MPS exercise/contingency JLTI. Report all instances of non- conformance to the 
COR/ACOR.   
  6.J.7.c  Navy Corrective Maintenance.  
  
(i)  The MCMC shall perform Corrective Maintenance on Navy MPE/S during the afloat phase IAW applicable technical manuals, UM 4000 -125, or directives 
(SOUM, MAM, etc.). Corrective maintenance services shall be sufficient in scope and effort to correct all non- mission capable faults in order to restore equipment 
to a Mission Capable condition. MPE/S warranties are to be considered and coordinated with the Navy COR.     (ii)  All non -mission capable equipment shall be repaired. In cases where 
completing maintenance actions are not possible (due to space restrictions, lack of special tools, and required repair parts), place the equipment into an “ABD SHIP” status in GCSS -MC IAW UM 4000- 125 within two business days, and add to the 
Deferred Maintenance Listing (DML).

 6.J.9   Shipboard Reports.  Shipboard reports shall be prepared using standard Microsoft Office 
programs. The MCMC shall provide the following reports to the Navy COR/ACOR IAW the CDRL delivery schedule:   
  6.J.9.a   Monthly Situation Report (SITREP)  (CDRL 6083) .  Monthly SITREPs shall:  
  
(i)  Be submitted by Squadron and assigned MPS.  
(ii) Be submitted NLT the first business day of each month using the standardized format provided in SOW Attachment  C.7- 1.    
(iii) Accurately summarize Logistics Services provided during the afloat phase to 
include but not limited to:  
(a)   Cover Sheet with Squadron Roll up of afloat actions  
(i)  Stock Checks, corrective maintenance, and Inventories. 
(ii)  Stock Discrepancy/Deterioration/Surveillance Status.  
(iii) Semi -Annual PMCS completed. 
     (b)  Equipment Warranty Issues  
     (c)  Applicable directives (SOUM, TI, MAM, etc.)  
     (d)  Organic Calibration Equipment (overdue)  
     (e)  General Comments or issues that affect the MCMC's ability to meet 
the performance of this SOW. Examples include:  
     (i)  MPS facilities or support concerns/issues. 
   (ii)  Refrigeration issues.  
  (iii)  Inability to perform MPE/S services.  
  (iv)  Recommendations for ship alterations to better accommodate   
shipboard requirements.

 (i)  Be submitted upon SASU completion and include the following:

 6.K  NAVY MPS PLANS AND DATA   
  The MCMC has a major role in the command’s ability to achieve mission success in plans and data accuracy. The MCMC is responsible for ensuring Squadron and Ship level plans are adhered to in order to properly configure and stow the stated quantity of equipment and supplies 
aboard specific vessels. Second only to equipment readiness, is the responsibility of the MCMC to provide data in a complete, accurate, and timely manner.  
  The planning facet of the MMC is an 18- Month process that results in Squadron and Ship level 
plans being developed in support of the MPF Program. Currently there several different types of plans utilized in support of a squadron's MPF Maintenance Cycle (MM C). At specified times 
outlined within this section, the MCMC will execute the detailed plans for each of the vessels within the squadrons.   6.K.1  The MCMC shall execute Navy Plans and Data Management.

 6.K.2  The NSC shall appoint a Navy Plans and Data POC who will be responsible for the 
execution and management of all Squadron and ship level detailed plans IAW the MCPP II SOW.  6.K.3  The MCMC shall produce a Unit Deployment Listing (UDL) End of Ship Report (CDRL 6064)  via MCPIC for all equipment and supplies embarked on MPS or as required IAW 
Production/Maintenance PCLs. This data shall be for the Navy at the NSN Level, IAW most current CmdBul 3162 and Ships Detailed Plan posted on Marine Corps Platform Integration Center (MCPIC). The MCMC shall use MCPIC for managing transportation data, which is part 
of the Defense Transportation Regulations (DTR) DoD 4500.9, and Marine Corps P repositioning 
Programs Policy (MCO 3000.17).  The UDL shall be produced IAW Government published milestones contained in Section C.1.  6.K.4  The MCMC shall utilize Government provided information management systems, data bases, and software systems to review, download, manage, store, and modify Maritime Prepositioning Ships planning related data as applicable, utilizing the Marine Corps Platform Integration Center (MCPIC) software system. Required data fields will be populated by the MCMC as detailed in CmdBul 3162 MCPIC Data Summary during the MPS specific production period.    
6.K.4.a  MCPIC is a web -based Information Technology (IT) tool that consists of 
multiple applications that support the MPF planning and execution processes. The MCMC shall utilize this system to receive, review, monitor, and execute all plans and data requ irements that are applicable to the NAV -P, Blount Island Command. 
  
(i) The Government will provide software and systems training for program wide 
use of the MCPIC software and associated applications. MCPIC is the current program of record which collects and provides detailed embarkation data.  
  (ii) In the context of the MCPP II effort:  
 
(a) “Ship Set” is defined as all materiel associated with the Navy and Marine Corps (NAVMC) 2907 (Prepositioning Objective) that are designated for a specific MPS vessel IAW detailed ship -level plans.   
 6.K.5  The MCMC shall adhere to the Government provided Navy Squadron and Ship Detailed Level Plans available on MCPIC IAW MMC execution milestones. Any deviation from these plans shall approved by the Government prior to execution.

  6.M.1 The MCMC shall support all required maintenance actions and embarkation support for 
Navy equipment IAW the current vessel Letter of Instruction (LOI), POA&M and Ship Confirmation Brief.  Including recovery actions for deadlined items as may be requir ed for 
shipboard prepositioned vehicular assets in conjunction with Port Operations and provide qualified personnel for electrical boost starting of vehicles that have been prepositioned aboard ships or that have been staged shore side for backload.    6.M.2  The MCMC shall download, and backload all assigned Navy prepositioned MPE/S, Offload Preparation Party (OPP), Petroleum, Oils, and Lubricants (POL), Armory and Battery Blocks IAW approved LOI, POA&M and Ship Confirmation Brief.   
  6.M.3  The MCMC shall stage all Navy vehicles and equipment in preparation for MPS backload operations IAW the Vehicle Staging List provided by BICmd Port Operations.  The MCMC shall remove all debris and garbage from vehicles prior to being staged for bac kload IAW the USDA 
Compliance Agreement.  This task shall be completed prior to vehicle staging.  Staged vehicles shall not be removed from the staging lot without permission from Port Operations.  Staging shall be completed IAW published MMC Notional POA& M.

  6.M.4  The MCMC shall secure Navy mobile loads IAW MIL -STD -209 and MCRP 4- 11.3.  
Mobile loads will be secured to prime movers to ensure it does not become damaged during ship offload, backload and transportation and is completed IAW the MIL Standard; no da mage shall

The MCMC shall ensure that current advisories, safety of use messages, etc. are adhered to when securing mobile loads.

This task shall be accomplished prior to staging.  Failure to complete this task as required places backload operations at risk due to exceeding MPS deck height restrictions.     6.M.6  The MCMC shall validate, and update Integrated Computerized Deployment System (ICODES) deck diagram after backload in conjunction with the Government.  The MCMC shall validate the ship’s drawings against the actual as loaded vessel; validations and updates shall be completed prior to the ship’s departure and approved by Port Operations.

Navy Consol idated Memorandum Receipt (CMR) via DPAS.

preventive and corrective maintenance actions shall be input into the NTCSS OMMS -NG 
module. Maintenance actions shall be entered accurately IAW maintenance requirements and actual maintenance performed. The MCMC shall close out all open Navy organic GFE OM MS-
NG work candidates within 30 calendar days of repair/maintenance completion and associated Government acceptance.

occurrence. For equipment defect discovery, which include but are not limited to, broken/defective, unserviceable or degraded equipm ent/parts, notify the NAV -P via email and 
pictures within four hours of identification.   6.N.7 Maximum Maintenance Cycle Time (MMCT). Maintenance Cycle Time (MCT) is the period of time covered from the initiation of maintenance actions until repairs and maintenance records are complete.    
6.N.7.a  Maximum Maintenance Cycle Time (MMCT) is the maximum prescribed time an asset is to remain in the maintenance cycle. The prescribed time is established as a decision -making threshold. Every asset type has a unique maintenance cycle time and 
theref ore, maintenance managers shall focus on minimizing maintenance cycle times and 
will only reach the maximum by exception. Maximum Maintenance Cycle Time thresholds only apply to deadlining/degraded services request that are corrective in nature and only exceeded by exception, e.g. economically justifiable, advantageous to mission completion, or availability of resources. The intent is to provide the highest level of operational GSE in support of the MPF program. Extension requests shall be submitted to the Navy COR for approval.    6.N.7.b  Navy GSE shall have a MMCT of 30 days. MMCT extension requests shall be submitted IAW 6.N.7.g.  
  
6.N.7.c  The MMCT shall be entered into the electronic maintenance database to record GSE maintenance history. MMCT Requests shall be submitted IAW 6.N.7.g.    6.N.7.d  The Navy COR is the Government representative to extend or reject MMCT extension requests.  
  6.N.7.e   All requests for MMCT extensions shall be printed and filed in the equipment history file.    6.N.7.f  The MMCT extension requests shall contain, at a minimum, the below information:  
 
(i) USN of the PEI  
(ii) Defect(s) not meeting the MMCT requirement  
(iii) Detailed information explaining why the defect could not be repaired in a timely manner. 
 
  
6.N.7.g  The MCMC shall submit an MMCT extension request to the Government at least five (5) working days prior to the end of the MMCT. Batching or grouping MMCT extension requests within a single work order is not authorized.

accurate data.

Twent Foot Equivalent Units (TEUs) that contain an average of 10,000 total containerized line items. The specialized logistics services performed during a product ion period include but are 
not limited to; inspection, inventory, servicing, corrosion control, Preventive/Corrective Maintenance (as required), configuration of mobile loaded equipment, association of collateral equipment, stock rotation, calibration, mar king and labeling of assets and containers with labels 
and RFID tags.   
6.O.1  The MCMC will be responsible for developing and implementing local procedures (IAW International Standards Organization 9001, CmdO P5000.11 Management Procedures, and BICmd Work Instructions (ISOs)) that delineate those processes used to provide tim ely and 
accurate data.  
 6.O.2  The MCMC shall utilize project management fundamentals and best practices to create production plans and schedules for all MCPP II maintenance requirements (to include Care of Supplies in Storage (COSIS)) and repair activities as directed in by the Ship’s Planning Considerations Letter produced by the Government IAW CmdO 4850.1. These production plans shall be submitted to the Government for review NLT 60 days prior to the commencement of a given production period.  
 
6.O.2.a  The MCMC shall include the following fields in each production plan and the plan shall be broken down by individual PEI/container/objective:  
(i) Projected start date and time  
(ii) Actual start date and time  
(iii) Projected end date and time  
(iv) Actual start date and time  
(v) Container Number (based on ships container plan)

each item that is not complete and ran FMC by the baseline finish date.

6.Q   NAVY WEIGHT HANDLING EQUIPMENT (WHE) PROGRAM  
  6.Q.1  The MCMC shall establish and maintain a Navy WHE program for WHE and rigging gear IAW NAVFAC P -307, NAVFAC P -300, and Navy Consolidated Memorandum Receipt (CMR) 
via DPAS for pre -positioning assets (PO). The NAVFAC P -307 provides the requirements for  
designating WHE personnel, training, maintenance, inspection, test, certification, repair, alteration, operation, and the use of WHE owned by the US Navy.   
  
6.Q.1.a   The MCMC shall nominate, in writing, Weight Handling Program Manager for Navy WHE for Government approval and signature.  The appointment letters  (CDRL 
6068)  shall be submitted to the Navy COR for concurrence within 30 calendar days 
following contract award or change of personnel. The Weight Handling Program Manager shall be appointed by the Commanding Officer (CO) of BICmd. The requirements for this position shall be found in the NAVFAC P -307. 
  6.Q.1.b The MCMC shall nominate, in writing, a Licensing Official for Navy WHE for Government approval and signature. The appointment letters  (CDRL 6068)  shall be 
submitted to the Navy COR for concurrence within 30 calendar days following contract award or change of personnel. The Licensing Official shall be appointed by the Commanding Officer of BICmd. The requirements for this position shall be found in the NAVFAC P -307. 
  6.Q.1.c The MCMC shall nominate, in writing, a primary and secondary Certifying Official(s) for Navy WHE for Government approval and signature.  The appointment letters  (CDRL 6068)  shall be submitted to the Navy COR for concurrence within 30 
calendar days following contract award or change of personnel. The Certifying Official(s) shall be appointed by the Commanding Officer of BICmd. The requirements for this position shall be found in the NAVFAC P -307. 
  6.Q.1.d  The Certifying Official shall appoint, in writing, a primary and secondary Load Test Director(s) and other personnel involved in the certification process for Navy WHE.  Appointment letters (CDRL 6068)  shall be submitted to the Navy COR for concurrence 
within 30 calendar days following contract award or change of personnel.

SECTION C.7 –  SHIPBOARD AND EXERCISE SUPPORT  
 
7.  This Section delineates the activities required of the MCMC during the Afloat Phase of the MPF Program.  The activities include surveillance, maintenance, and logistics services as identified in MCO P3000.17 and TM 4790- 14/2.   Additionally, this Secti on identifies the 
services that shall be performed to issue and recover Maritime Prepositioning Equipment and Supplies (MPE/S) during Maritime Prepositioning Ships (MPS) Operations.  General maintenance management, quality control, supply, Key Management I nfrastructure (KMI), 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 244 of 425 
 
 Transportation, Embarkation Data, Navy, and Organic Property requirements that are associated 
with afloat phase operations, are addressed in other sections within this SOW (Section C.2, Section C.4, Section C.6, and Section C.9).  Work performance shall include places of debarkation throughout the world, during MPS exercises, contingencies, Maintenance Support Team (MST) or other types of scheduled and unscheduled downloads, as directed by the Government.   Logistics Services (Preventative and Corrective Maintenance, Modifications, Calibration, Maintenance Management, Battery Services, Shelf -Life Management, Inventory 
Management, Limited Technical Inspection, Equipment Configuration, Exercise Support, Key Management Infrastructure, Shipboard Reports, and sur veillance shall conform to the 
specifications described within the SOW.  Shipboard logistics services aboard MPS, governed by the MCPP contract shall be performed in accordance with the afloat phase surveillance schedule.   
 7.A.  The MCMC shall perform shipboard logistics (supply and maintenance) services in accordance with standards defined in this section and all applicable Marine Corps Orders and directives, Department of Defense (DoD) guidance and directives, Federal, Sta te and Local laws 
and regulations, industry standards, and other technical documentation to deliver responsive, consistent, and reliable logistics solutions for Maritime Prepositioning Ships (MPS) afloat.     7.B.  The MCMC shall perform surveillance for MPE/S during MPS afloat phase, to include prepositioned Military Equipment (ME), containers,  Civil Engineering Support Equipment (CESE), square loaded Aviation Support Equipment, and installed communication end items.  Afloat phase surveillance will not include Refrigeration End Units, Expeditionary Airfield (EAF), nor Navy LARCs and Lighterage. The MCMC shall report MPE/S maintenance and operational status.   Maintenance reporting and documentation requirement s are contained in 
Section C.2 of the SOW.”  
    
 7.C. The MCMC shall perform Operator Level Preventive Maintenance Checks and Services (PMCS) on MPE/S assets IAW applicable equipment operator manuals.   As applicable per 
equipment TM, complete Operator (before, during, and after) PMCS during Semi -Annual start -
ups (SASU) for square loaded rolling stock and non- rolling stock (e.g.  generators, LVSR 
flatracks, etc.).  For communications systems, perform the daily and weekly PMCS/Scheduled Services in conjunction with scheduled SASU.  PMCS shall be completed every six months beginning 60 calendar days after MMC or upon completion of an MPS backload operation. The MCMC shall develop a local form and record PM CS actions required IAW the equipment 
technical manual for MPE/S.  The PMCS record/form shall be locally generated and contain the following minimal information: TAMCN, Serial Number, deficiencies identified, date, and signature of the individual who compl eted the PMCS and the Supervisor that verified the 
completion of the service.  7.D.  Battery Maintenance. I.A.W TM 9 -6140- 200-13 all batteries must be tested regularly. 
Batteries that register 12.1 volts or lower, indicating a state of charge SOC of 35% or less, should be charged to maintain proper SOC for vehicle roll -on, roll -off conditions. This guidance 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 245 of 425 
 
 specifically deviates from the previously referenced 12.65 volts, as the lower threshold is 
justified by historical experience with equipment preservation during long- term storage aboard 
MPF ships in dehumidified cargo holds.    Battery testing and servicing shall be performed in conjunction with scheduled PMCS and prior to MPS offloads.  The MCMC shall develop a  comprehensive battery maintenance plan to  be sufficient in scope and effort to minimize battery failure due to storage configuration by ensuring that equipment batteries are in a ready condition.   7.D.1 The MCMC shall complete all modifications that affect any Tactical Communications Systems and associated equipment (to include laptop computers) Authority to Operate (ATO) or Authority to Connect (ATC) to Marine Corps Tactical Networks  when possible  during a offload.  
Additionally, modifications affecting ATO or ATC shall be coordinated prior to any exercise / contingency offload for any items placed in a deferred maintenance status.  
 7.D.2.  Marine Corps Transparent Armored Gun Shield (MCTAGS) batteries shall be installed and battery power cables connected in prepositioned tactical motor transport wheeled vehicles (ECV FOV, MTVR FOV, LVSR FOV, and JLTV FOV).   For MCTAGS identified as mobile loaded or containerized systems:  
 
• The MCMC shall ensure effective battery accountability, tracking, and issuing procedures are established and executed between the on -island battery facility and 
shipboard personnel.  
• The MCMC shall ensure an effective battery plan is established aboard each ship to service, maintain, and issue MCTAGS batteries associated with mobile loaded or containerized systems during the afloat phase.  
 
7.D.3.  The MCMC shall maintain the Tactical Hand Held Radios (THHRs) IAW Graywacke Engineering Inc. User/Technical Manual (GEI -24-SAPS) and appropriate equipment TM’s.  
THHRs and their primary batteries shall be maintained in the Stay Alive Power Systems (SAPS) at all times when not in use.    7.D.4.  The MCMC shall perform quarterly recoil exercises on the M777A2 I.A.W. applicable equipment operator manuals and TI 10407C -OR/3 every 90 days after backload. The MCMC 
shall document quarterly recoil exercises by utilizing a service request and M777 Gun Book within GCSS -MC I.A.W. UM 4000.125.  
 7.D.5  The MCMC shall remove and maintain M777A2 hardware and waterproof caps within the ship(s) battery facility I.A.W applicable equipment technical manual.  MCMC shall notify the Operation Department (Shipboard Branch) once the removal of the batteries has been accomplished. 7.F.  Corrective Maintenance.  
 7.F.1.  The MCMC shall perform Corrective Maintenance on MPE/S during the afloat phase IAW applicable technical manuals, UM 4000- 125, or directives (SOUM, MAM, etc.).   
Corrective maintenance services shall be sufficient in scope and effort to correct all non -mission 
capable faults in order to restore equipment to a Mission Capable condition.     7.F.2.  Complete Priority Designators 02 (Deadline) repairs within ten business days from the receipt of required repair parts.  Complete Priority Designators 05 (Operational -Degraded) 
repairs within 15 business days from receipt of the required repair parts.  Com plete maintenance

receipt of the required repair parts.  
 
7.F.3.  The MCMC shall complete all “Urgent” Modification Instructions (MI's) on tactical ground equipment embarked aboard ship IAW the UM 4000- 125 and applicable Urgent MI 
contained on the Marine Corps Stock List (S -L-1/2).  The MCMC shall notify the Government of 
Marine Corps published MIs that has potential application for Navy CESE assets. The Government will provide direction from the Contracting Officer on which modifications to accomplish for Navy CESE assets.  Make ever y reasonable effort to complet e equipment 
"Urgent modifications for equipment embarked aboard MPS vessels within the time compliance period specified in applicable technical manuals.  In cases where completion of "Urgent modifications is not possible, the MCMC shall perform all mainten ance actions possible, obtain 
all necessary modification kits/materials and place the equipment into a deferred maintenance status per Section C.2.  Normal MI will be deferred to the MMC.  7.F.4.   All non- mission capable equipment shall be repaired.  In cases where completing 
maintenance actions are not possible (due to space restrictions, lack of special tools, and required repair parts), IAW UM 4000 -125 within two business days, and add to the Def erred Maintenance 
Listing (DML).  
 
7.F.5. MPE/S warranties are to be completed when applicable.  See Section C.2.

• provide a proposed Resource and Support Plan ( CDRL 7002)  for Contracting Officer 
approval NLT 10 business days after receiving government RFP.  The plan shall include effort to support OPP operations, equipment offloads, arrival and assembly, reconstitution/regeneration, backload operations, and other requirements identified in the RFP.  
• complete Logistics Services for IAW the approved Resource Plan.   
• utilize and update the Government issued Consolidated Asset Listing (CAL) IAW Section C.4.  Government produced CAL shall be used to document the issue of MPE/S to the Marines Air Ground Task Force's (MAGTF) Responsible Officer (RO).  All changes to the CA L shall be coordinated through the TAAT OIC via the assigned  
COTR.   
• perform Mobile Load Association validation during MPS offloads and backload Operations.  
• prepare and issue Temporary Folders, GSSS -MC M777 Gun Book data shall be placed in 
equipment temporary folders  as required per the RFP.  Place equipment records, scheduled for offload, in a suitable container and organized by TAMCN and USMC

ashore, etc.)

o hired personnel not afloat for more than sixty days

• Employee is found in violation of federal or state laws.  
• Employee is found in violation of local laws in a foreign country.

7.K.  Government Furnished Services. The Government will provide certain services and support for contractor employees specifically assigned to work aboard MPS. Services and support include:  
• Unaccompanied baggage authorization. Employees are authorized a 300 pound weight 
allowance for unaccompanied baggage allowance under the following conditions; initial 
assignment to a ship and after successful completion of a contract assignment.  On transfer due to promotion or increases or decreases in authorized personnel per ship due 
to government direction. On a case -by-case basis, as approved by the Government, 
special circumstances will authorized (e.g., extended temporary duty assignments, hardships).  The most economical (i.e. lowest cost) means of shipment, normally U.S. Mail is utilized.
)Food, lodging, internet and email access for official Government or 
contractor proprietary business, and other certain personal services through Inter -Service 
Support Agreement (ISSA) between BICmd and Military Sealift Command (MSC).  
• Fleet post office mailing privileges, subject to geographic availability.   
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 253 of 425 
 
 • Use of ship's store and recreational facilities, subject to hours of operation.  
• Use of crew's laundry (self- service).  
• Contractor personnel may make personal phone calls on a "not- to-interfere" basis via the 
ship's communications equipment, subject to any charges associated for this service.  Use 
will be subject to operational restrictions and shall only be provided at the discretion of the Ship's Master.  
• Privileges at military exchanges, clubs, commissaries, medical facilities, are limited to areas where commercial facilities are not available.  To ensure contract requirements are met, shipboard employees shall be issued a Letter of Authorization (LOA) in the Synchronized Pre- deployment & Operational Tracker (SPOT) database.  The privileges 
will be incorporated onto the employees Common Access Card (CAC). The website for access is https://spot.dmdc.mil
; this website requires an account to generate by name 
LOA's.  The LOA's should be generated during the pre-deployment process for new employees before the CAC is issued.  A SPOT LOA must be generated for those employees who are located OCONUS and do not have the required privileges. Coordinate this effort with the COR to ensure all employees have the appropriate privileges. 
• Medical services aboard ship is limited.  There will be no routine medical care provided aboard ship to the MCMC.  The MCMC shall make their own arrangements for medical services ashore at their own expense.   
• No dental services will be available on MPS.  
• In most cases, each employee will be given a single stateroom with private bathroom and shower.  Some staterooms may be designated as double occupancy and have shared bathroom and shower. 
• Transportation to and from the ships while anchored at sea (arranged by the Ship's Master).  
• Initial training for use of Government required Automated Information Systems (AIS).  
• New equipment training will be provided by the Government, as required.

SECTION C.8 - KEY MANAGEMENT INFRASTRUCTURE  
 This Section provides overarching guidance for the proper management and positive control of Key Management Infrastructure (KMI) assets during prepositioning and ashore operations.  A KMI Account is assigned to BICmd for management of all Communications Security (COMSEC)/Controlled Cryptographic Item (CCI) assets.  The mission of KMI is to ensure the proper distribution, control, security and accountability of COMSEC/CCI materiel used to maintain the cryptographic security of the Department of the Navy Communications.  KMI provides for the security of highly sensitive, classified COMSEC and COMSEC -related 
materiel/equipment.  KMI Support Services as described below are required at all program operating locations.  
 The MCMC shall perform KMI support operations as Local Elements, Issuing or Using in accordance with standards defined in this section and all applicable Marine Corps Orders and directives, Department of Defense guidance and directives and shall ensure the proper management, maintenance and inventory control of KMI assets assigned to them during prepositioning and ashore operations.  KMI support operations at all locations governed by the contract shall be performed in support of the MCPP contract in accordance with the schedule and requirements listed below.

SECTION C.9 - PORT OPERATIONS SUPPORT  
 This Section sets forth the requirements and responsibilities of the MCMC for Port Operations services during offload and backload evolutions of Marine Corps Maritime Prepositioning Ships (MPS) in support of the Marine Corps Maritime Prepositioning Force ( MPF) Maintenance Cycle 
(MMC).  The mission of the MCMC relative to offload/backload operations is to provide support as required by the Government Port Operations Branch during the offload and backload of United States Navy and United States Marine Corps equipment and supplies aboard MPS in a safe, efficient and timely manner.  
 The MCMC shall support all offload/backload operations in accordance with standards defined in this section and all applicable Marine Corps Orders and directives, Department of Defense guidance and directives, Federal, State and Local laws and regulations, industry standards, and other technical documentation.  Operations at all locations governed by the MCPP contract shall be performed in accordance with published ship Planning Objectives and Milestones (POA&Ms) and published Plans and Data tim elines. Products shall conform to the specifications described 
within this section. Quality assurance measurements will be governed by the performance requirements and standards associated with this section and specifications described within Section C.2.  Requirements and clarifications identified herein are those specific to product/service delivery in support of offload/backload operations.  The MCMC shall adhere to these requirements.  
  9.A PORT OPERATIONS AND EMBARKATION SUPPORT  
 9.A.1 The MCMC shall support all required maintenance actions and embarkation support IAW 
the current vessel Letter of Instruction (LOI), POA&M and Ship Confirmation Brief.  Including 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 260 of 425 
 
 recovery actions for deadlined items as may be required for shipboard prepositioned vehicular 
assets in conjunction with Port Operations and provide qualified personnel for electrical boost starting of vehicles that have been prepositioned aboard ships or that have been staged shore side for backload.  
 9.A.2 The MCMC shall download and backload all prepositioned COSIS, Offload Preparation Party (OPP), Petroleum, Oils, and Lubricants (POL), Armory and Battery Blocks IAW approved LOI, POA&M and Ship Confirmation Brief.    9.A.3 The MCMC shall execute all lighterage offload/backload operations IAW the approved LOI, POA&M, Section C.6 Navy and the Ship Confirmation Brief.  
 9.A.4 The MCMC shall stage all vehicles and equipment in preparation for MPS backload operations IAW the Vehicle Staging List provided by BICmd Port Operations.  The MCMC shall remove all debris and garbage from vehicles prior to being staged for backload IAW the USDA 
Compliance Agreement .  This task shall be completed prior to vehicle staging.  Staged vehicles 
shall not be removed from the staging lot without permission from Port Operations.  Staging shall be completed IAW published MMC Notional POA&M ( SOW  Attachment  C.1- 3). 
   9.A.5 The MCMC shall stage all containers in preparation for MPS backload operations IAW the Container Staging List provided by Port Operations.   The MCMC shall wash all containers prior to being staged IAW the USDA Compliance Agreement.  If a container delivered to the staging area has not been washed properly, the container will be sent back to be rewashed and inspected before backload is permitted.  Staging shall be complete IAW published MMC Notional POA&M ( SOW Attachment  C.1- 3). 
 9.A.6 The MCMC shall secure mobile loads IAW MIL -STD -209 and MCRP 4- 11.3.  Mobile 
loads will be secured to prime movers to ensure it does not become damaged during ship offload, backload and transportation and is completed IAW the MIL Standard; no damage shall occur to mobile load prior to the ship’s departure.   Mobile loads will not exceed the maximum transport weight listed in the applicable TMs or Government provided supplemental information.  The MCMC shall ensure that current advisories, safety of use messages, etc. are adhered to when securing mobile loads.  
 9.A.7 The MCMC shall ensure all mobile load and PEI equipment overall height does not exceed the requirements set forth in the Mobile Load Association Plan.  This task shall be accomplished prior to staging.  Failure to complete this task as required places backload operations at risk due to exceeding MPS deck height restrictions.    9.A.8  The MCMC shall validate the ship’s drawings against the actual as loaded vessel; validations and updates shall be completed prior to the ship’s departure and approved by Port Operations.

(2)  Allowable Downtime Standards  
 The Contractor shall manage the overall maintenance effort, so NTVE downtime does not exceed the allowable downtime standards specified herein.  For the purpose of computing downtime, regular working hours is defined as eight hours per day, Mondays through Fridays, except observed Federal holidays.  Downtime shall be measured by individual Shop Repair Order as well as by Allowable Downtime by Individual Alpha Group.  
 Downtime hours per Shop Repair Order shall not exceed the Allowable Individual Downtime by Alpha Code specified in C -10-05.  Downtime hours per individual alpha group shall not exceed 
10% of the total available hours per individual alpha group per month (i nvoice period).  For any 
given alpha group, the total hours of availability per month equals the total number of units in the group times the number of regular working hours per month.  
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 265 of 425 
 
 (3)  Downtime Computation.  Downtime computation shall start immediately when a vehicle is 
reported down; regardless of the Priority identified.  Any time period that NTVE is returned to the Contractor for rework shall be included in the downtime computation.  Any time period NTVE is under the control of the Government shall not be included in the downtime computation.  If the Government elects to have work performed by other than the Contractor, any downtime associated with such performance will not be included in the downtime computation.  When computing downtime, fractions of hours shall be rounded to the nearest hour (less than 30 minutes = 0, 30 minutes or more = one hour).  
 Examples of downtime computation for individual Service Repair Order:  
Scenario 1:  
Background:  
N9812345 is a Low Speed Vehicle with an Alpha Code Z (LSV) and is a Priority 
3 asset.  
 
Situation:  
The Asset is reported down at 10:00 hours on April 1, 2016, and the Work Order was 
completed at 09:00 hours on April 3, 2016.  The asset is returned without any Downtime infraction.  
 
Scenario 2:  
Background:  
N9812456 is a Low -Speed Vehicle with an Alpha Code Z (LSV) and is a Priority 
3 asset.  
 
Situation:  
The Asset is reported down at 10:00 hours on April 1, 2016, and the Work Order was 
completed at 12:00 on April 4, 2016. The asset is returned without meeting the allowable downtime by Individual Alpha Code for an individual service repair order. (Work Orde r 
exceeded the 24 maximum allowable hours for individual Alpha Code Z (LSV)).  
 Examples of downtime per Individual Alpha Group:  
1) Available Hours = number pieces of equipment per Individual Alpha Code * eight 
hours per day * number of working days in the month.   
Example:  
50 pieces of equipment identified within Alpha Code Z * eight hours per day * 21 
working days in April 2016 = 8,400 Available Hours. 
 2) Downtime Hours = 10 pieces of equipment identified within Alpha Code Z had 
400 total hours of downtime. 
 Percentage of Alpha Code Z Downtime:  
400tal hours of downtime / 8,400 Available Hours = 4.8 % of Downtime

 (4)  Temporary Suspensions of Downtime Standards  
 
When the Contractor experiences a delay in material receipt that will cause the individual unit downtime of a particular unit to exceed the allowable time, the Contractor shall submit a Temporary Suspension of Downtime Accumulation (CDRL 10003) to the KO.  Written requests shall include a copy of a purchase order showing the material order date and expected delivery date, and information demonstrating the lack of required material is beyond the Contractor's control.  Downtime suspensions will not be approved if the material in question should have been stocked in adequate quantities to meet expected demand was not ordered to allow for adequate vendor lead times, or if the shipping method used to transport the material causes/contributes to the delay.  
 If granted, suspension of downtime accumulation will only cover the period from the date/time the Contractor's written request was provided to the KO to the date/time the material is received.  The Contractor shall notify the KO immediately upon receipt of  the material and provide written 
documentation showing date and time of receipt.  
 
(5)  Excessive Repairs  
 The one -time repair limits set forth in Appendix G of MCO 11240.106_ do apply.  Before the 
Contractor performs all repairs that exceed the one- time limits they will obtain approval (CDRL 
10004) from KO.  
 
(6)  Fleet Size  
 Fleet size shall be based upon the actual quantity of equipment in the fleet at a given time and is not a cumulative total of the equipment maintained throughout the contract.  The number of NTVE may vary as much as 5% without a contract modification.    The contract inventory will be adjusted semiannually based on actual inventory.  Each modification shall reset the 5% variance allowance.  
 Each item of equipment pending removal from the fleet, and its designated replacement, shall be counted as a single unit for inventory purposes while the replacement unit is prepared for service (e.g., equipment installation, marking, and record establishm ent).  Repairs to keep equipment 
safe and reliable while pending disposition shall be required.  
 
(7)  Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS)  
 The Contractor shall provide and implement a commercially available Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS), to be licensed to the Government, which manages all service operations and shall provide to the government a monthly preventive maintenance 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP

 (PM) work schedule.  The Contractor shall ensure appropriate licenses are provided which will 
provide three licenses for Government use and adequate appropriate licenses for Contractor data management and entry.  Required data types for Service Provider Information is:  
 • Work Order Data:  Work order data includes all necessary information for the 
documentation of all completed work orders, including, but not limited to, service orders, preventive maintenance, and work issued as work request.  
 • Asset Data: Asset data includes the specific details necessary for proper identification and 
tracking of assets.  Asset data shall be updated for all completed work orders where an asset is repaired, replaced, installed, or otherwise affected.  When repl acing existing assets, the 
Contractor shall change the status of the current asset which will remove it from the maintenance plan and add the new asset.  
 (8)  Fleet Focus.  The Contract shall obtain access to government system, Fleet Focus.  This will be the system of records for all GSA, NTVE maintenance records (inventory provided in C -10-
02, 03, 04).   (iv)  Records and Reports.  The Contractor shall maintain NTVE, AWP, MHE, and WHE records and reports in accordance with MCO 11240.106_, and NAVFAC P -307 and.  Such 
records include the following:  
 
(1).  Individual vehicle/equipment history jackets     (2).  Accident damage repair estimates  
(3).  Vehicle repair status  
(4).  Annual safety and reliability inspection checklists  
(5). Enter/maintain monthly mileage report from activity in Fleet Focus.  
 The Contractor is responsible for validation, accountability and accuracy of NTVE, AWP, MHE, and WHE documents and Fleet Focus data fields.  All physical and digital records are subject to USMC Audit’s and evaluations.  The Contractor shall ensure reports and data are accurate, on time and completed in Fleet Focus on a daily basis.  
(v)  Reserved  
 
(vi) Workmanship and Material Standards  
 The Contractor shall be responsible for maintaining all NTVE (to include NTVE, WHE, MHE and support equipment), identified in this technical spec to a standard that prevents deterioration beyond that which results from normal wear and tear and corrects def iciencies in a timely 
manner to assure full life expectancy of the NTVE (to include NTVE, WHE, MHE and, and support equipment).  Best commercial practices shall be applied in the performance of work.  All work shall be completed per approved equipment manufacturers and accepted industry standards and shall comply with safety codes, applicable activity, local, state, and federal regulations, and other technical requirements identified within this technical spec.  
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 268 of 425 
 
  
Workmanship for maintenance and repair shall include all work necessary to safely return NTVE (to include NTVE, WHE, MHE and support equipment) to service, including corrosion control and operational checks.  Workmanship includes repair of coating systems damaged during maintenance or repair.  Upon completion of work, the Contractor shall ensure all NTVE (to include NTVE, WHE, MHE and support equipment) are free of missing components or defects which would affect the safety, appearance or operation of the N TVE (to include NTVE, WHE, 
MHE and support equipment) or would prevent any electrical, mechanical, or structural system from functioning in accordance with design intent.  Repairs shall be made in accordance with the manufacturers’ specifications and guide lines. The quality of repairs shall meet the applicable 
standards and shall prevent any malfunction reoccurrences caused by poor workmanship or other contractor inadequacies.  The quality of the repaired areas shall be fully compatible with adjacent surfac es or equipment.  Except where otherwise specified, replacements shall match existing 
components and interior and exterior paint/coating in dimensions, finish, color, design, and functionality and shall have an appearance like the original finished appeara nce with only minor 
unobjectionable deterioration resulting from normal use.  The Contractor shall not allow debris to spread unnecessarily into adjacent areas nor accumulate in the work area.  All such debris, excess material, and parts shall be cleaned up and removed at the completion of the job and at the end of each day work is in progress.  Upon completion of work, any stains and other unsightly marks shall be removed.  All equipment, including parts washers, comply and are managed in accordance with all federal, state and local requirements.  
 The Contractor shall secure and organize all tools, equipment and materials (used and un- used); 
to prevent loss and ensure a clean and safe working environment in the immediate working area 
as well as all adjacent areas   The Contractor shall adequately se cure all material stored on cranes 
and other vehicles and equipment to prevent injury from overhead falling objects and debris.  
 
(vii) Reserved  
 10.A.3 Recurring Work  
 The Contractor shall develop and implement a comprehensive industrialized transportation program consisting of NTVE, MHE, and WHE management, operations, maintenance, repair, and alteration to ensure efficient, safe and reliable NTVE, MHE, and WHE services  such that all 
NTVE, MHE, and WHE requirements are continually met.  The Contractor shall comply with federal, state, local regulations, applicable instructions, publications, manuals, directives, manufacturer’s specifications, and recommendations.  GSA Fleet Inventory is provided in C -10-02. 
 NTVE Fleet Inventory is provided in C -10-03. 
 Material Handling Equipment (MHE) Fleet Inventory is provided in C -10-04

Weight Handling Equipment (WHE) Inventory is provided in C -10-##. 
 
10.A.3.a Operations  
 The Contractor shall operate NTVE to ensure services are performed.  
 Obtain necessary permits for the movement of overweight/oversized loads on public highways.  Necessary permits must be obtained prior to scheduled performance.  
 Mark and operate vehicles used for transporting ordinance, hazardous materials and other dangerous articles in accordance with NAVSEA OP -2239, Federal Motor Carrier Safety 
Regulations, U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Highway Administration and Bureau of Motor Carrier Safety and other applicable rules and regulations  
 Provide qualified operators for WHE and MHE services.  
 Provide qualified rigging support.  Provide proper and certified equipment for all work requested.  
 Complete and retain Operator Daily Checklist and PM records on vehicles and equipment as specified by the MCO 11240.106_ and NAVFAC P -307.  Retained records shall be used to take 
corrective actions in accordance with MCO 11240.106_ and NAVFAC P -307. 
 
All crane lifts shall be documented and reported to the Government to include a description of and the total number of jobs.  Vehicles used for transporting ammunition, explosives, or other dangerous/hazardous material shall be marked and operated per applicable laws and regulations.  
 
(i) WHE/MHE/NTVE Services  
 The Contractor shall provide WHE/MHE/NTVE services when dispatched to ensure the mission of the Government is accomplished safe and timely manner.  Appropriate equipment and qualified operators and support personnel are provided 30 minutes prior to the scheduled time.  The Contractor shall provide pier side gantry crane support per the MMC offload/backload schedule and during ammunition operations.  Ammunitions Operators occur after workhours (after 1900 on Friday(s) continuing nonstop (24 hrs) until Ammuni tion Operators are complete).  
 Operating personnel shall have experience in operating cranes of the size and capability used to perform the work and the necessary safety precautions required per the NAVFAC P -307. 
 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 270 of 425 
 
 The Contractor shall comply with crane weight restrictions and weightlifting capacity of piers at 
MCSF -BI.  Crane operators shall be trained and licensed to operate crane per the NAVFAC P -
307 and MCO 11240.106_. 
 
Crane riggers/signalman shall be trained and qualified to signal a crane per the NAVFAC P -307.  
The size of the crane team will vary to suit the job as determined by the rigger supervisor. The Contractor shall provide additional riggers as needed to ensure  safe crane operation (e.g., 
ordnance work, high work lifting personnel, multiple elevation lifts). The Contractor may receive assistance from qualified shipboard personnel: However, the Contractor cannot be guaranteed such assistance and is responsible for completing jobs with its’ own workforce.  
For all crane jobs the Contractor shall be on site in sufficient time to inspect the equipment and rigger gear, position the crane, and make all preparation such that the Contractor is ready at the time requested by the customer to perform the first lift.  
The Contractor shall meet safety and operation standards for crane services and shall have a certificate of load test and condition inspection on the crane at all times and that the crane and rigger gear are in compliance with the NAVFAC P -307. 
The Contractor shall fully cooperate with all Government incident/accident investigations and shall provide all requested information for the Crane Accident Review Board (CARB) following crane accidents and incidents.  After a weight handling incident or c rane accident, the operator 
shall be prohibited from operating a crane until after the weight handling incident has been reviewed.  The Government will make the determination when an operator can continue to operator cranes under this Contractor.  
The Contractor shall provide and maintain a sufficient inventory of slings, straps, and lifting devices.  All rigging equipment shall comply with the NAVFAC P -307 requirements.  Maintain 
a serialized record of slings and lifting devices.  Test all new slin gs and lifting devices per the 
NAVFAC P -307.  Destroy all unsafe slings and lifting devices. 
Cranes will be washed monthly.  All wash water from crane washing and other large equipment shall drain into the sewer system.  Discharge of wash water to the storm drain is prohibited. As a minimum, the Contractor shall maintain two full- time licensed crane operators and two full -
time qualified riggers to ensure team size requirements are maintained per NAVFAC P -307.  The 
workload/requirements specified are not limited by the minimum num ber of crane crews.  The 
Contractor must perform all workload specified including exigencies.  
Necessary permits for the movement of overweight/oversized loads on public highways shall be obtained. Vehicles used for transporting ammunition, explosives, or other dangerous/hazardous material shall be marked and operated per NAVSEA OP -2239 and the rules and regulations prescribed by 
the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations, U.S. Department of Transportation, Federal Highway Administration, Bureau of Motor Carrier Safety.

The contractor shall use GFE and provide personnel to dispense Government procured fuel in NTVE/MHE.

The contractor shall use GFE and provide personnel to dispense Government procured fuel in NTVE/MHE.  
The Contractor shall fuel NTVEs/MHE as needed.  Fuel delivery services are performed safely in an efficient, accurate, and timely manner.  Fuel is safely delivered within plus or minus 15 minutes of scheduled time.

 The Contractor shall oversee the entire fuel transfer operation and coordinate and oversee the 
opening and closing of the valves on the fuel tanker truck.   
The Contractor shall take all steps and precautions necessary for product receipt and issue from storage facilities per UFC 460 -01/03, DoN regulations, and installation instructions applicable to 
fuel storage operations.  To this end, the contractor shall develop and submit to the contracting officer for approval within 14 calendar days of award, standard operating procedures that detail how the contractor intends to ensure that fueling operations are conducted in a safe and environmentally sound manner and in accordance with all Federal and state laws.  
Provide unscheduled gasoline and diesel fuel delivery services as requested by the COR or KO.  
Maintain accurate fuel record of fuel delivery in accordance with DLA P -2. 
Ensure fuel accountability of at least 98% accuracy.  
Ensure that all fuel meters are certified and calibrated as required.  
10.A.3.b Support Services  
The Contractor shall provide NTVE/MHE support services to ensure that the service requirements are met.  
 
(i)  Dispatching Services  
 
The Contractor shall provide dispatching services for NTVE/MHE to ensure vehicles are dispatched in a timely manner.  
Provide dispatching services during Government regular working hours to support customer requirements.  
Provide service within 15 minutes plus or minus of scheduled time.  
Dispatch of NTVE/MHE are processed through Fleet Focus.  
Dispatch Process and Procedures are reviewed and updated periodically.  The Contractor shall 
implement any future versions of Dispatch Process and Procedures at no additional cost to the Government.  
Permissible Operating Distance (POD) requests shall be approved by the Government.  
Vehicles and equipment dispatched are inspected at check -out and check -in, and discrepancies 
are noted by the operator on in Fleet Focus. The Contractor shall ensure compliance with GSA Fleet Guide and Accident Reporting. The Contractor shall ensure operators complete SF -91 if involved in an accident. 
The Contractor shall ensure dispatchers are cognizant of the Government's fuel conservation program, and trips are consolidated whenever possible. The Dispatcher's Log in Fleet Focus shall be used to record all vehicles dispatched off station and for all Class C pool vehicles.  
 
(ii)  GSA Leased Vehicles  
The Contractor shall coordinate, and schedule required maintenance and repair of leased vehicles to ensure maintenance is kept up to date and repairs are performed in an expeditiously.  
GSA Fleet Inventory is provided in C -10-02. 
 
(1)  Support for GSA Leased Vehicles  
The Contractor shall coordinate, and schedule required maintenance and repair to ensure maintenance is kept up to date and repairs are performed expeditiously. 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP

 The Contractor shall coordinate preventive maintenance, repairs, recalls and warranty to ensure 
work is performed at the intervals specified by GSA, Fleet Manager, and approved service providers.  
Prepare and submit Shop Repair Orders in Fleet Focus and GSA Fleet.Gov for all work performed on vehicles and equipment and maintain copies in each history file. Each GSA vehicle requires an annual service.  Additionally, approximately 10% require unscheduled maintenance or repair.  
 
(2) Received, Replaced and Reassigned GSA Vehicles and Equipment  
Provide Support Services for newly Received, Replaced and Reassigned GSA Vehicles and Equipment to ensure satisfactory operation.  
The work includes GSA Vehicles and Equipment for Government use.  
The contractor shall pick up and drop off newly received, reassigned and replaced GSA vehicles and equipment at B -351 Organic Maintenance. The contractor shall also perform other in- service 
and turn- in duties to include vehicle preparation and administrati ve functions. 
For Received Vehicles and Equipment:  
Establish new History Jacket, and initial new service Shop Repair Order (SRO); perform an initial safety inspection; if problems identified, prepare a Quality Deficiency Report.  
Spare Keys shall be locked and secured within the designated key lockbox.  
The contractor shall coordinate the installation and removal of specialized accessories including but not limited to, special lights, radios, racks, shelves, tow -hitches, and other customer provided 
accessories.  
Pick up new vehicles from the dealership or auction house and complete in -service preparation 
within two working days of notification of arrival.  
Ensure the presence of technical and digital parts manuals and ensure that accident reporting kits 
are in each GSA vehicle and coordinate all warranty requirements with the manufacturer or leaser.  
For Reassigned Vehicles and Equipment:  
Ensure that the vehicle is up to date with Preventative Maintenance requirements. Establish History Jacket as required.  
 
10.A.3.c Maintenance of Non- Tactical Vehicles and Equipment (NTVE)  
The Contractor shall maintain, repair and alter Government owned vehicles and equipment to ensure they are in fully operational condition.  
Selective exchange of parts between vehicles or equipment must be authorized  (CDRL 10005)  
by the KO.  
Ensure adequate parts are available to expedite maintenance repairs in order to meet allowable downtime standards.   
Properly mark vehicles and equipment as required so that vehicle identification markings are clear and visible at all times in accordance with MCO 11240.106_ and P -307. 
Maintain vehicle and equipment history files (hardcopy) for life of all units serviced under this contract.  
Prepare and submit Shop Repair Orders for all work performed on all vehicles and equipment and maintain copies in each history file. Vehicles are maintained in accordance with OEM recommendations.

New Vehicles
