service all fluid levels and tire pressures; and apply appropriate vehicle markings as required. Spare Keys shall be locked and secured in designated key lockbox.  
Reassigned Used Vehicles  
Service includes (if needed or within 30 days of scheduled SM) an oil change, tune -up, lube, 
reliability inspection, and repair of any deficiencies noted during the required inspections. Spare Keys shall be locked and secured in designated key lockbox.

The Contractor shall perform Maintenance (Preventative, Interim), Inspection, Testing, and Certification of all WHE, MHE and AWP to ensure equipment is maintained in a safe and fully 
operational and certified condition at all times.  
The Contractor shall Inspect, test, and certify all WHE, MHE, AWP, and OHE at least annually and after an adjustment, repair, replacement, or alternation/modification of a load bearing or load controlling part in accordance with MCO 11240.106_, NAVFAC P -307, NAVSEA OP -5 and the 
equipment manufacturer's instructions and manuals. The Contractor shall provide an updated monthly PM/Certification and Testing schedule  (CDRL 
10007) to include 100% of the identified WHE, MHE, OHE.  
The Contractor shall submit a Maintenance Inspection Specification and Record for WHE to the government certifying official for approval.  Conduct condition inspection for WHE in accordance with MCO 11240.106_and P -307. 
The Contractor shall perform preventative maintenance, interim maintenance and repairs found necessary as a result of any inspection/test, or preventive maintenance deficiency.  
The Contractor shall support all related audits, evaluations, safety and condition inspections, and investigations from authorized Government audits and pre -audits. The Contractor shall perform 
tasks such as, but not limited to, rigging, operator, test dir ector and inspector duties; ensuring all 
equipment and program requirements are in full compliance; providing report responses  CDRL 
10008)  within 20 days of report receipt in proper format and address all findings and 
observations with sound recommendations for correcting all discrepancies.  
Corrosion control inspection, repairs and prevention measures shall be performed as part of Preventive Maintenance.

(3) Preventive Maintenance Inspections of NTVE  
The Contractor shall perform preventive maintenance (PM) inspections, including safety and reliability inspections for automotive vehicles and equipment, to ensure safe and proper operation.  
The Contractor shall submit a PM Schedule  (CDRL 10012) . 
PM shall be performed up to 14 calendar days prior to the due date if the equipment is in the shop for any other reason. 
Unless otherwise specified PM inspections and any related repair work shall be completed within three working days after NTVE is received, except where the criteria for Priority 1 or 2 applies. Corrosion control inspection, repairs and prevention measures shall be performed as part of Preventive Maintenance.  
PM’s intervals for vehicles shall be performed at least annually during the safety/reliability inspection.  However, additional PM’s may be performed if the vehicle exceeds more than 6,000 miles or 200 hours from the safety/reliability inspection or as spe cified by the manufacturer or as 
specified by the manufacturer for city driving.  If the mileage/hour meter or annual safety/reliability inspection are within 60 calendar days of each other the more stringent type of service shall be performed.  The Contra ctor shall affix a PM “Inspection Due” sticker with the 
current month plus one year on the inside windshield of the vehicle upon completion. Perform required inspection testing and certification for applicable equipment requiring certification in accordance with OEM, MCO 11240.106_ and NFPA. The Contractor shall wash all NTVE when annual PM is performed.  
All work shall be in accordance with the MCO 11240.106_ , NFPA and OEM standards.

 The MCMC shall comply with Safety and Occupational Health requirements  IAW Marine Corps 
Order 5100.29, 29 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) 1910, and all applicable National Fire Protection Association codes/standards.  The MCMC shall provide a Site Safety and Health Officer (SSHO) whose primary duty and responsibility is to prepare and enforce the MCMC’s safety program on this contract.  The SSHO or an equally qualified Designated Representative/alternate shall be on -site at all times when work is being performed to implement 
and administer th e Contractor's safety program and government -accepted Accident Prevention 
Plan.   The SSHO must have a working knowledge of current CFR’s, NFPA, and ANSI standards.  Must have completed the OSHA 30 Hour Construction or equivalent, General Industry safety c lasses, and maintain competency through 24 hours of formal safety and health 
related coursework every four years.  The SSHO shall be certified in First Aid/CPR/AED.  The SSHO shall have, at minimum, a four -year degree in Occupational Safety and Health and 5- 7 
years of satisfactory experience in preparing and enforcing safety programs on contracts of similar size and complexity.    The SSHO shall also have incident and accident investigation knowledge as well as experience using root cause methodology.  The SSHO shall not be the

and health program referenced in the APP in the applicable APP element and made site- specific.

responsible for informing their subcontractors of the safety provisions under the terms of the contract and the penalties for noncompliance, coordinating the work to prevent one craft from interfering with or creating hazardous working conditions for other crafts, and inspecting subcontractor operations to ensure that accident prevention responsibilities are being carried out.   The APP shall be signed by an officer of the firm (MCMC senior person), the individual preparing the APP, and the designated Site Safety and Health Officer (SSHO)).  Continuously review and amend the APP, as necessary, throughout the life of the contract. Changes to the accepted APP shall be made with the knowledge and concurrence of the Contracting Officer, SSHO and Quality Control Manager. Incorporate unusual or high- hazard

10.B.3  Hot Work  
 
10.B.3.a Permit and Personnel Requirements.  Obtain a signed permit (CDRL 10023) 
prior to performing "Hot Work" (i.e. welding or cutting) or operating other flame-producing/spark producing devices, from the IDIV Safety Office.  A permit is required from the Explosives Safety Office for work in and around where explosives are processed, stored, or handled. CONTRACTORS ARE REQUIRED TO MEET ALL CRITERIA BEFORE A PERMIT IS ISSUED. Provide at least two 20 lb. (9 kg)extinguishers with a minimal ra ting of 4A:20BC for normal "Hot Work". The 
extinguishers must be current inspection tagged, and contain an approved safety pin and tamper resistant seal. It is also mandatory to have a designated FIRE WATCH for any "Hot Work" done at this activity. The Fir e Watch shall be trained in accordance with 
NFPA 51B and remain on-site for a minimum of one hour after completion of the task or as specified on the hot work permit. 
 When starting work in the facility, require personnel to familiarize themselves with the location of the nearest fire alarm boxes and place in memory the emergency phone numbers.  REPORT ANY FIRE NO MATTER HOW SMALL, TO THE RESPONSIBLE INSTALLATION SAFETY OFFICE IMMEDIATELY AFTER CALLING THE EMERGENCY NUMBER.  
 10.B.3.b No Hot Work Permit Required Designated Areas.  The MCMC may, in coordination with and under the authorization of the Installation, establish No Hot Work Permit Required Designated Areas.  These areas shall be maintained in compliance with NFPA 51B standards.  Standard signs shall be posted to identify No Hot Work Permit Required Designated Areas.

10.B.5 Voluntary Protection Program (VPP) 
 The MCMC shall provide representation and employee participation in the Installation's OSHA VPP.  Participation includes, but is not limited to, attendance at meetings, providing data, and providing recommendations to improve processes or correct non-compl iance issues and will be 
IAW OSHA VPP standards, the elements of VPP and the VPP evaluation checklist.  Installation Safety will request data when required by the annual VPP Application.  
10.B.6 Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) 
 The MCMC shall comply with established OSHA PPE requirements and shall provide PPE that is in a serviceable condition to their employees IAW 29 CFR 1910 and MCO 5100.29.  Appropriate garment shall be based on the worker hazards and tasks, complexity of the work environment or background, and vehicular traffic and speed.  The MCMC shall assess the workplace to determine if hazards are present, or are likely to be present, which necessitate the use of personal protective equipment (PPE).   If such hazards are present, or likely to be present, the MCMC shall:  
1. Select, and have each affected employee use, the types of PPE that will protect the affected employee from the hazards identified in the hazard assessment;  
2. Communicate selection decisions to each affected employee; and,  
3. Select PPE that properly fits each affected employee.  
 The MCMC shall verify that the required workplace hazard assessment has been performed through a written certification that identifies the workplace evaluated; the person certifying that the evaluation has been performed; the date(s) of the hazard assessm ent; and, which identifies 
the document as a certification of hazard assessment.

10.B.9  Smoking Policy 
 Smoking is prohibited within and outside of all buildings on the installation, except in designated smoking areas.  Discarding tobacco materials other than into designated tobacco receptacles is considered littering.  The Contracting Officer will identify designated smoking areas.  The MCMC shall enforce the smoking and non-smoking areas with their employees.  
10.B.10  Accident and Damage Reporting  
 The Contractor shall notify (CDRL 10030)the Contracting Officer as soon as practical, but no more than four hours after any accident meeting the definition of Recordable Injuries or Illnesses or High Visibility Accidents, property damage, or any Weight Handling Equipment (WHE) accident.  Notific ation shall also be provided for any mishap occurring in any of the following 
high hazard areas: electrical (to include Arc Flash, electrical shock, etc.); uncontrolled release of hazardous energy (includes electrical and non -electrical); weight or load ha ndling equipment 
(LHE) or rigging; and fall- from -height (any level other than same surface).  These mishaps shall 
be investigated in depth to identify all causes and to recommend hazard control measures.    Within notification include Contractor name; contract title; type of contract; name of activity, installation, or location where accident occurred; date and time of accident; names of personnel injured; extent of property damage, if any; extent of injury, if known, and brief description of accident (to include type of equipment used, PPE used, etc.).  Preserve the conditions and evidence on the accident site until the Government investigation team arrives on- site and 
Government investigation is conducted.  The Contractor shall conduct an accident investigation for recordable injuries and illnesses, for accidents requiring Medical Treatment, property damage accidents, and near misses as defined in MCO 5100.29, to establish the root cause(s) of the accident.

 For any weight handling equipment accident to include when MHE configured in a manner to lift 
suspended loads (including rigging gear accidents) the Contractor shall conduct an accident investigation to establish the root cause(s) of the accident and compl y with additional 
requirements and procedures for accidents in accordance with NAVFAC P -307, Section 12.  The 
Contractor shall submit a WHE Accident Report.  No crane operations are allowed to proceed until cause is determined and corrective actions have b een implemented to the satisfaction of the 
Contracting Officer.  
 For a near miss involving crane or rigging operations, the Contractor shall report verbally to the Contracting Officer as soon as management becomes aware but not later than 4 hours of such event and comply with additional requirements and procedures for n ear-misses in accordance 
with NAVFAC P -307, Section 12.  A near miss occurs when an accident was avoided by mere 
chance or when intervention prevented an ongoing sequence of events that would have resulted in an accident (e.g. unplanned encroachment, improper crane set -up, improperly rigged load, 
etc.).  The Contractor shall submit a Crane and Rigging Gear Near Miss Report. 
 10.B.10.a Accident Reporting and Notification Criteria  
 The following criteria and definitions apply to the accident reporting requirements specified above:  
 Recordable Injuries or Illnesses.  Any work -related injury or illness that results in:  
1. Death, regardless of the time between the injury and death, or the length of the illness;  
2. Days away from work (any time lost after day of injury/illness onset);  
3. Restricted work;  
4. Transfer to another job;  
5. Medical treatment beyond first aid;  
6. Loss of consciousness; or  
7. A significant injury or illness diagnosed by a physician or other licensed health care professional, even if it did not result in (1) through (6) above.  
 High Visibility Accident. Any mishap which may generate publicity or high visibility.  
 Medical Treatment. Treatment administered by a physician or by registered professional personnel under the standing orders of a physician. Medical treatment does not include first aid treatment even through provided by a physician or registered personnel.   WHE Accident.  A WHE accident occurs when any one or more of the eight elements in the operating envelope fails to perform correctly during operation, including operation during maintenance or testing resulting in personnel injury or death; material or equipment damage; dropped load; derailment; two -blocking; overload; or collision, 
including unplanned contact between the load, crane, or other objects. A dropped load, derailment, two -blocking, overload and collision are considered accidents even though no 
material damage or injury occurs. A component failure (e.g., motor burnout, gear tooth 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 284 of 425 
 
 failure, bearing failure) is not considered an accident solely due to material or equipment 
damage unless the component failure results in damage to other components (e.g., dropped boom, dropped load, roll over, etc.  The MCMC shall provide a mishap statistics report that shall categorize all mishap information from the previous year by type of mishap and shall identify property losses and MCMC man -days lost and will be submitted by close of business (COB annually on 
January 15
th. 
 10.B.10.b The MCMC shall provide a quarterly mishap statistics report that shall categorize mishap information by type of incident, (i.e. First Aid, OSHA reportable, and lost time).  
 
10.B.11  Radiation Safety Requirements  
 The MCMC shall comply with radiation safety standards IAW CmdO P5104.3, 29 CFR 1910, and MCO 5100.29. 
 10.B.11.a The MCMC shall provide notification (CDRL  10031) to the Radiation Safety 
Manager regarding any radiation accident/incident IAW CmdO 5104.3, 29 CFR 1910, MCO 5100.29. Notification will be verbally within 30 minutes of incident and followed up with a formal written (email, memo, letter) notification within 3 business days of incident.   10.B.11.b The MCMC shall ensure adequate radiation training for designated personnel IAW CmdO P5104.3, 29 CFR 1910, and MCO 5100.29.  10.B.11.c The MCMC shall properly manage a radioactive material/waste storage area IAW EPA 40 CFR, The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) 10 CFR Part 20, MCO 4450.12, 29 CFR 1910, and MCO 5100.29.  10.B.11.d The MCMC shall complete semi-annual radioactive material inventories and submit (CDRL 10032) to Installation Safety Office IAW CmdO P5104.3, 29 CFR 1910, MCO 5100.29, EPA 40 sub Chap F CFR, the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) 10 CFR Part 20, MCO 4450.12 by June 30
th and January 30th every year.  
 10.B.11.e The MCMC shall provide notification (CDRL 10033) to the Radiation Safety Manager within 3 business days of any radiation producing components that have been received aboard MCSF -BI or left. The notification shall list the type of radiation source, 
the location of the component (or where it was headed), and any documentation that accompanies the components (i.e. wipe test), RAM movement forms, and inventories.

10.B.13  Safety Inspections  
 The Contractor shall conduct inspections of its work areas, sites, and work crews every day work is being performed to ensure that all Contractor operations are being conducted safely.  These inspections shall ensure:    
• The site is safe and free of job -site hazards   
• Proper PPE is being utilized and worn.   
• Safe work practices and processes are being followed.  
• Workers are familiar with the hazards covered in the respective AHA for that work 
activity.  
• All equipment and tools are in good condition and being used safely.  
 
 The Government reserves the right to inspect and monitor Contractor operations for safety compliance.  In general, the Government approach will be to conduct Performance Assessment on the quality and effectiveness of the Contractor’s safety program.   The Government reserves the right to stop any work activity when it deems danger is imminent.  Contractor personnel shall work in a safe manner and comply with all applicable safety regulations.  The Contractor shall be subject to safety inspections of its work sites by the Government.  Contractor safety records shall be available to the KO upon request.  The Contractor shall submit a copy of the Contractor Safety Inspection and Monitoring Report upon request (CDRL  10036).  The Contractor Safety Inspection and Monitoring Report shall 
include a narrative of significant events; a tabulated summary and results of the safety inspection

10.C ENVIRONMENTAL

This includes Executive Orders (EO), Department of Defense (DoD), Department of Navy (DoN) and Marine Corps (USMC) Policies, Instructions and Directives.  
10.C.2  Environmental Manager 
 The Contractor shall provide (CDRL  10040) an Environmental Manager and designated alternate 
whose primary duty and responsibility is to ensure Contractor operations adhere to the goals and policies of the Environmental Management System, and other specified Sustainability requirements affecting th is contract.  The Environmental Manager shall develop, implement, and 
monitor environmental strategies, policies and programs that promote sustainable development and examine the contract activities to establish wh ere improvements can be made and ensure 
compliance with environmental legislation and energy policy.     The Environmental Manager shall have a minimum two years’ experience with environmental procedures similar to those of this contract; familiarity with Environmental Management Systems (EMSs); and knowledge of environmental regulations and policy (including energy and water reduction requirements and renewable energy requirements) that are applicable to operations similar to those of this contract and shall have experience in hazardous waste management and hazardous substance spill response.  The Environmental Manager shall be trained prior to the start of this contract and possess the following:  
1. Site supervisor for spill cleanup in accordance with OSHA regulation and 29 CFR 1910  
2. Initial DOT

Hearing Conservation Program (HCP) 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 287 of 425 
 
  
The MCMC shall administer a continuing, effective hearing conservation program IAW 29 CFR 1910.95 and MCO 5100.29 where contractor personnel’s workplace noise exposure equals or exceeds OSHA’s occupational noise Action Level (i.e., 8-hour time-weighted average sound level (TWA) of 85 decibels measured on the A scale).  The MCMC is responsible for determining MCMC personnel’s occupational noise exposure and ensuring all required personnel are enrolled in the HCP.  
 10.B.18. Hazard Communication (HAZCOM) Program  The MCMC shall develop, implement, and maintain a written HAZCOM program IAW 29 CFR 1910.1200 and MCO 5100.29.  The program should include, but is not limited to, label and warning requirements, Safety Data Sheet requirements, and employee information and training.    
 
10.C ENVIRONMENTAL  
 10.C.1  The MCMC shall comply with all environmental requirements in accordance with all local, state and federal environmental statutes, regulations and standards. This includes Executive Orders (EO), Department of Defense (DoD), Department of Navy (DoN) and Marine Corps (USMC) Policies, Instructions and Directives.  
10.C.2  Environmental Manager 
 The Contractor shall provide (CDRL  10040) an Environmental Manager and designated alternate 
whose primary duty and responsibility is to ensure Contractor operations adhere to the goals and policies of the Environmental Management System, and other specified Sustainability requirements affecting th is contract.  The Environmental Manager shall develop, implement, and 
monitor environmental strategies, policies and programs that promote sustainable development and examine the contract activities to establish wh ere improvements can be made and ensure 
compliance with environmental legislation and energy policy.     The Environmental Manager shall have a minimum two years’ experience with environmental procedures similar to those of this contract; familiarity with Environmental Management Systems (EMSs); and knowledge of environmental regulations and policy (including energy and water reduction requirements and renewable energy requirements) that are applicable to operations similar to those of this contract and shall have experience in hazardous waste management and hazardous substance spill response.  The Environmental Manager shall be trained prior to the start of this contract and possess the following:  
1. Site supervisor for spill cleanup in accordance with OSHA regulation and 29 CFR 1910  
2. Initial DOT Ground Shipper for Hazardous Material/Hazardous Waste training that meets 49 CFR 177.800-816 and triennial refresher.  
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 288 of 425 
 
 3. Initial 40 CFR 262.17(a)(7) and 40 CFR 265.19 training for a Large Quantity Generator 
of Hazardous Waste and annual refresher.  
4. 40 hours HAZWOPER and annual 8-hour refresher training   
5. A minimum of 16 -hour course in hazardous material transportation.  
6. Within 6 months following the start of the contract, the Environmental Manager shall complete annual training in SPCC, Storm Water Pollution Prevention, site specific training for HW, 40 CFR 265.16 training for a Large Quantity Generator of Hazardous Waste, and EPCRA. 
 The Environmental Manager shall not be the same person as the Project Manager, Quality Manager, SSHO, Transportation Manager, WHE Operation and Maintenance Manager, or site manager.  
 
10.C.3 Air Resources  
 Equipment operation, activities, or processes shall be in accordance with 40 CFR 64, state air emission and performance laws & standards and all air permits.  
 10.C.3.a  IAW EPA Title V Air Permit the MCMC shall (CDRL  10041) input and 
maintain operating data for all painting and abrasive blast operations.  This data will be kept in a Government provided Microsoft Access Air database and will include at a minimum date of operation, painting hours, amount of paint used/applied, type of paint used/applied, type of application (i.e., spray, can, brush), type and amount of solvent used, blast hours, type of blast media, amount of blast media used, and visual emission observations.   The database will be updated NLT the fifth business day of every month.  Database training will be provided as needed to the MCMC contractor by Installation Division Environmental Section as needed.  If training is needed MCMC shall make a request in writing immediately and provide a time for the required training.   10.C.3.b The MCMC shall request through the Environmental Compliance Workflow approval for any new or existing paint or paint related product they wish to use that is not currently listed in the Microsoft Access Air Database. The new paint or paint related  
product request (CDRL  10042) should include the name of the product, product 
information (size, type, etc.) and its Safety Data Sheet (SDS) number.  The request should be at least 5 business days prior to the required usage date.   10.C.3.c The MCMC shall (XDRL  10043) provide an accurate inventory for all MCMC 
assigned Green House Gas (stationary and mobile) sources NLT 30 calendar days after the Government’s request for annual reporting.  The tracking and inventory of the Green House Gases shall be IAW the Clean Air Act (CAA), MCSF -BI Air Emission Integrity 
and Green House Gas Inventory.   10.C.3.d The MCMC shall utilize the Microsoft Access Ozone Depleting Substance Government database located on the shared site and input accurate Ozone Depleting 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 289 of 425 
 
 Substance inventory data and update (CDRL 10044) it NLT the 5th business day of each 
new quarter. 
 
10.C.4 Stormwater  
 10.C.4.a  The MCMC shall obtain Government approval prior to any water discharges using the Water Discharge Form/Workflow.  Water discharges include storm water, waste water, large amounts of potable water, and contained/confined water.  10.C.4.b Stormwater Pollution Prevention  The MCMC shall comply with the installation’s Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan (SWPP), requirements of 40 CFR 122.26, along with NPDES, and St John’s River Water Management District (SJRWMD) Master Stormwater Permit.  
 10.C.4.b.1 Inspection Reports Submit “Inspection Reports” (CDRL  10045) to the Contracting Officer in 
accordance with section 10.C.4.b.  
10.C.5  Hazardous Material Management  
 10.C.5.a  The MCMC shall comply with all Federal, state, local and DoD hazardous materials requirements, IAW 40 CFR Parts 260 -370, 49 CFR Parts 105-178, 29 CFR 
1910, MCO 5100.29B, MCO 5090.2, DoD MIL- STD -101C, and the most recent U.S. 
DOT Emergency Response Guidebook.  Do not bring hazardous material onto Government property that does not directly relate to requirements for the performance of this contract at that specific Government property location.  Submit (CDRL  10046) a 
SDS and estimated quantities to be used for each hazardous material to the Installation Division Environmental manager prior to bringing the material on the installation.  Use hazardous materials in a manner that minimizes the amount of hazardous waste generated.  Containers of hazardous materials shall have National Fire Protection Association labels or their equivalent.  Certify that hazardous materials removed from the installation or offsite locations are hazardous materials and do not meet the definition of hazardous waste, in accord ance with 40 CFR 261. 
 10.C.5.b  The MCMC shall provide a written Exposure Control Plan (CDRL 10047) for hazardous work environments to all work centers within 30 calendar  days of contract 
award and 30 calendar  days of a change to the plan. The plan shall include the 
mechanisms for providing knowledge to the workforce and follow-up exposure criteria. Mechanisms are defined as email, signs, training, safety meetings, etc.  
 10.C.5.c  The MCMC shall provide all required training to all personnel that handle hazardous materials IAW 40 CFR Parts 260-370, 49 CFR Parts 105-178, 29 CFR 1910, MCO 5100.29B, MCO5090.2, DoD MIL- STD -101, and MCSF-BI Comprehensive 
Environmental Training and Education Plan (CETEP). 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 290 of 425 
 
  
10.C.5.d  The MCMC shall maintain all hazardous materials in the Marine Corps approved system Hazardous Material Management System (HMMS) IAW MCSF -BI 
Hazardous Material Management Plan (HMMP).  The MCMC shall conduct inspections as required by HMMP and provide (CDRL 10048) the respective HAZMAT Inspection Form to the Government NLT the 5th business day following the inspection.      10.C.5.e  The MCMC shall comply with Emergency Planning and Community Right- to-
Know Act Reporting (EPCRA) requirements IAW MCSF-BI Integrated Contingency Plan and MCO 5090.2. 
 
10.C.6  Hazardous Waste Control and Management  
 Do not dispose of, nor bring hazardous waste on Government property.  Do not discharge any waste to a sanitary sewer, storm drain, or to the surface water or conduct waste treatment or disposal on Government property without written approval of the Contrac ting Officer.  
 10.C.6.a  Hazardous Waste Management  Identify, label, handle, transport and storage of hazardous waste or debris in accordance with federal, state, and local regulations, including DoD, DoN, USMC and installation Policies, Instructions, Directives and plans.  Manage hazardous waste in accordance with the approved installation Hazardous Waste Management Plan.  Store hazardous wastes in approved containers in accordance with 49 CFR 173 and 49 CFR 178.  Hazardous waste generated within the confines of Government facilities is identified as being generated by the Government.  Prior to removal of any hazardous waste from Government property, hazardous waste manifests shall be signed by personnel from the Installation Division Environmental Office.  Do not bring hazardous waste onto Government property.    10.C.6.b  Facility Hazardous Waste Generator Status.  MCSF -BI is designated as a Large 
Quantity Generator.  The MCMC shall meet the regulatory requirements of this generator designation for any work conducted within the boundaries of the installation.  Comply with provisions of federal, state, and local regulatory requirements applicable to this generator status regarding training and storage, handling, and disposal of derived wastes.  10.C.6.c  Waste Storage/Satellite Accumulation/90 Calendar  Day Storage Areas.  
Accumulate hazardous waste at satellite accumulation areas and in compliance with 40 CFR 262 and other applicable federal, state or local regulations.  Individual waste streams shall be limited to less than 208 liter/ 55 gallons of acc umulation (or 0.95 liter 1 
quart for acutely hazardous wastes).  
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 291 of 425 
 
 10.C.6.d  Universal Waste Management.  Manage the following categories of universal 
waste in accordance with federal, state, and local requirements and installation instructions: 
(i) Batteries as described in 40 CFR 273.2 
(ii) Lamps as described in 40 CFR 273.5 
(iii) Mercury -containing equipment as described in 40 CFR 273.4 
(iv) Pesticides as described in 40 CFR 273.3 
 10.C.6.e  Electronics End-of- Life Management.  Recycle or dispose of electronics waste, 
including, but not limited to, used electronic devise such as computers, monitors, hard-copy devices, televisions, mobile devices, in accordance with 40 CFR 260-262, st ate, and 
local requirements and installation instructions.  
 10.C.6.f  Biomedical Waste.  The MCMC shall manage all biomedical waste generation, handling, and storage IAW MCSF- BI's Biomedical Waste Management Plan.  The 
MCMC is the permitee for the installation’s Biomedical Waste disposal permit.  The MCMC shall hav e the permit transferred to them upon commencement of the contract 
and maintain the permit in a compliant status.  
 10.C.6.g  The MCMC shall identify waste generated and complete all waste disposal turn-in-documents IAW MCSF- BI's Hazardous Waste Management Plan. Turn in 
documentation to include Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for Off- Spec or HMMS waste profile 
number for used items.  
 
10.C.7  Pest Management  
 The MCMC shall comply with Pest Management IAW MCSF -BI Integrated Pest Management 
Plan.   
10.C.7.a  The Contractor shall not apply any pesticide products per ESOH-W-8.1.1089 Obtaining Animal and Pest Control Services.  Pesticide application shall be according to need and not by schedule. Application of pesticides in any area inside or outside t he 
premises shall not occur unless visual inspections or monitoring devices indicate the presence of pests in that specific area.  
 
10.C.8  Petroleum, Oil, Lubricant (POL) Storage and Fueling 
 POL products include flammable or combustible liquids, such as gasoline, diesel, lubricating oil, used oil, hydraulic oil, mineral oil, and cooking oil. Store POL products, fuel equipment, motor vehicles and used oil in a manner that affords the maximum protection against spills into the 
environment. Manage and store POL products and used oil in accordance with 40 CFR 112, and 
other federal, s tate, regional, and local laws and regulations. Use secondary containments, dikes, 
curbs, and other barriers, to prevent POL products and used oil from spilling and entering the ground, storm or sewer drains, stormwater ditches or canals, or navigable waters of the United States.  Storage of fuel on the installation or offsite locations shall be in accordance with EPA, state, and local laws and regulations and paragraph POL storage including fuel tanks. 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 292 of 425 
 
  
10.C.8.a Used Oil Management.  Manage used oil generated on site in accordance with 
40 CFR 279, state and local regulation and applicable MCSF-BI environmental plans. 
 
10.C.8.b POL Storage Including Fuel Tanks.   
 (i)  Provide secondary containment and overfill protection for POL storage tanks. Use drip pans during POL transfer operations; adequate absorbent material shall be onsite to clean up any spills and prevent releases to the environment. Cover tanks and drip pans during inclement weather. Provide procedures and equipment to prevent overfilling of tanks.  If tanks and containers with an aggregate aboveground capacity greater than 1,320 gallons will be used on the installation or offsite locations (only containers with a capacity of 55 gallons or greater are counted), provide and implement a SPCC plan meeting the requirements of 40 CFR 112.  Submit the SPCC plan to the Contracting Officer for approval.  (ii)  Monitor any rainwater that accumulates in open containment dikes or berms; coordinate with Installation Division Environmental office for removal. Inspect the accumulated rainwater and contract the Environmental Office to schedule the water to be drained or pumped out. 
 
10.C.8.c The MCMC shall take operational control of the storage tanks assigned to the MCMC under the MCSF- BI's AST -UST Plan and IAW MCSF -BI Integrated 
Contingency Plan.  The MCMC shall perform storage tank inspections and provide the necessary maintenance.  The MCMC shall submit Tank Maintenance Records (CDRL 
10049) monthly, NLT the 30
th day of each month following maintenance.  
 
10.C.9 Employee Training 
 10.C.9.a The MCMC shall develop and maintain a training program to enhance annual environmental awareness training for all employees in additional to training required by all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and executive orders, and with base-wide instructions, standards, and permit requirements.  Environmental awareness training shall include topics such as hazardous material and regulated/non- regulated 
waste management, recycling, solid waste disposal, stormwater BMPs, general housekeeping procedures, fueling procedures, spill prevention and response, natural resources, and cultural resources.   All site personnel shall receive annual and refresher training and training records are maintained on site for five years.  Employees identified as the Environmental Manager, spill response team member, or as a qualified individual for a specific program must obtain the necessary training certifications required to fulfill the position assigned.  Training records must be maintained and available for inspection

 Provide a Position Description for each employee, by subcontractor, evaluating the 
employee’s association with hazardous and regulated wastes.  This Position Description will include training requirements as defined in 40 CFR 265 for a Large Quantity Generator facility.  
 10.C.9.b  The MCMC shall provide appropriate spill response training for personnel who routinely handle POLs or hazardous material within 30 calendar  days of a new hire or 
transfer.  
 10.C.9.c  The MCMC shall complete and report (CDRL  10050) attendance of all 
employees Environmental Training and Education using the Government provided Comprehensive Environmental Training and Education Plan (CETEP) Microsoft Access Database.  Once the Employee has completed the training, the Training records wi ll be 
entered into the database NLT the 5th business day of the following month.

 10.C.10.a  Pickup solid wastes, and place in covered containers that are regularly emptied.  Do not prepare or cook food on the installation.  Prevent contamination of the site or other areas when handling and disposing of wastes.  Employ segregation measures so that no hazardous or toxic wastes will become co-mingled with non-hazardous solid waste.  
 10.C.10.b  The MCMC shall comply with Solid Waste Management IAW MCSF- BI 
Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan including the recycling of all waste stream outlined in the plan.  
10.C.11 Green Procurement  
 The MCMC shall comply with the Green Procurement Process for MCMC acquired consumables, materials and equipment IAW federal laws, regulation and EOs as well as DoD and DoN policies.  This shall be done IAW MCSF-BI Green Procurement Plan and a report of Green Procurement actions (CDRL  10051) will be provided to the Government NLT the 5th of 
each month.  
10.C.12  Pollution Prevention 
 The MCMC shall comply with pollution prevention federal laws, regulation, EOs, DoD and DoN policies. This shall be accomplished IAW MCSF-BI Pollution Prevention Management Plan, which shall include participating in the Navy’s Pollution Prevention Program per Executive Order 13423 and OPNAV 5090.1. The MCMC will also follow Pollution Prevention Program per MCO 5090.2 which requires Pollution

Prevent contamination of the site or other areas when handling and disposing of wastes.  The MCMC shall comply with the Green Procurement Process for MCMC acquired consumables, materials and equipment IAW federal laws, regulation and EOs as well as DoD and DoN policies.  This shall be done IAW MCSF-BI Green Procurement Plan and a report of Green Procurement actions (CDRL  10051) will be provided to the Government NLT the 5th of

This shall be accomplished IAW MCSF-BI Pollution Prevention Management Plan, which shall include participating in the Navy’s Pollution Prevention Program per Executive Order 13423 and OPNAV 5090.1. The MCMC will also follow Pollution Prevention Program per MCO 5090.2 which requires Pollution Prevention surveys, and including but not limited to, attendance in kickoff meetings, planning meetings, responding to requests for data collection, verification of data provided for collection, and providing recommendations to improve processes or decrease the amount of waste generated.

DRAFT  RFP

10.C.13  Protection of Natural Resources 
 
10.C.13.a The MCMC shall minimize interference with, disturbance to, and damage to fish, wildlife, and plants, including their habitats.  Prior to the commencement of activities, consult with the IDIV Environmental Office, regarding rare species or sensiti ve 
habitats that need to be protected.    10.C.13.b The MCMC shall comply with the MCSF- BI Natural Resources Management 
program as outlined in the MCSF- BI's Integrated Natural Resource Management Plan 
(INRMP).  
 10.C.13.c The MCMC shall conduct Manatee watch as required IAW MCSF- BI's 
INRMP and provide an after Action Log (CDRL 10052) NLT the 5th of the following month.  Manatee watch is required with 30 minutes notification each time there are ship movements in the slipway except during designated winter months as determined by IDIV Environmental Office.  The Government will provide a Government provided boat and all MCMC employees will be required to have National Association of State Boating Law Administrators class or equivalent. 
 
10.C.14  Control and Management of Polychlorinated Biphenyls (PCBs) 
 The MCMC shall manage PCB contaminated waste in accordance with 40 CFR 761.  
10.C.15  Military Munitions  
 The MCMC shall comply MCO 5090.2 when dealing with Military Munitions Waste.  In the event military munitions, as defined in 40 CFR 260, are discovered, immediately stop work in that area and immediately notify MCSF -BI or BICmd Safety Office.  
 
10.C.16 Conformance with the Environmental Management System (EMS) 
 10.C.16.a The MCMC shall participate in the Installation's ISO 14001 site certification and EMS Programs IAW MCSF-BI Environmental Management System Manual.  10.C.16.b Work under this contract shall be consistent with the policy and objectives identified in the installation’s EMS.  Perform work in a manner that conforms to objectives and targets of the environmental programs and operational controls identified by the EMS.  Support Government personnel when environmental compliance and EMS audits are conducted by escorting auditors at the shop/building, answering questions, and providing proof of records being maintained.  Provide monitoring and measurement infor mation as necessary to address environmental performance relative to 
environmental, and energy goals.  In the event an EMS nonconformance or environmental noncompliance associated with the contracted services, task, or actions occurs, take corrective and p reventative actions.  In addition, employees shall be aware of 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 295 of 425 
 
 their roles and responsibilities under the installation EMS and of how these EMS roles 
and responsibilities affect work performed under the contract.  10.C.16.c The MCMC shall coordinate with the installation’s EMS coordinator to identify training needs associated with environmental aspects and the EMS, and arrange training or take other action to meet these needs.  Make EMS Awareness training completion  certificates available to Government auditors during EMS audits and include 
the certificate in the Employee Training Records.  See paragraph 10.C.9 Employee Training. 
 
10.C.17  Recycling  
 10.C.17.a  The MCMC shall recycle to the maximum extent possible; office paper, computer paper, mixed paper, corrugated cardboard, wood, scrap metals, POL products, e-waste, batteries, antifreeze, and any additional items identified by the government.  10.C.17.b The MCMC shall recycle all spent fluids IAW MCSF- BI Solid Waste 
Management Plan.  All spent fluids shall be recycled to the maximum extent possible.  All spent fluids handling and transportation shall be closely coordinated with the Government and shall be in full compliance with DoD, Navy and Marine Corps, Federal, State, and local environmental regulations.  10.C.17.c The MCMC shall keep their respective building’s trash collection areas clean 
at all times to deter the harboring of pest including areas in and around all roll- off 
containers, dumpsters and compactors.  10.C.17.d The MCMC shall coordinate all recycling activates with the government.  10.C.17.e The MCMC shall designate personnel responsible for the orderliness of recyclable material collection areas.  
 
10.C.18  Releases/Spills of Oil and Hazardous Substances  
 10.C.18.a  Response and Notifications.  The MCMC shall exercise due diligence to prevent, contain, and respond to spills of hazardous material, hazardous substances, hazardous waste, regulated gas, POLs, and other substances regulated in accordance with 40 CFR 300.  In the event of a spill, take prompt, effective action to stop, contain, curtail, or otherwise limit the amount, duration, and severity of the spill/release.  In the event of any release of oil and hazardous substances, chemicals, or gases; imme diately notify  
(CDRL 10053), the Installation Command Duty, and the IDIV Environmental Office and follow the procedures in the MCSF-BI Integrated Contingency Plan.  Spill response shall be in accordance with 40 CFR 300 and applicable state and local regulations, and IAW MCSF -BI Inte grated Contingency Plan.  The MCMC boat crew shall be the initial 
response team for any spills in the slipway and will be responsible for deploying the containment boom across the slipway to prevent any spill within the slipway f rom 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 296 of 425 
 
 migrating to the river.  The boat crew shall all be OSHA 40- hour HAZWOPER trained, 
maintain a boater education certificate “boaters license”, and shall be familiar with the 
Facility Response Plan.  The boat crew shall attend initial FRP training and annual  
refresher training and/or exercises.  The boat crew supervisor shall function as an alternate Incident Commander and as such should have successfully complete National Incident Management System training courses 100c, 200c, and ICS 300.   10.C.18.b  Clean Up.  The MCMC shall clean up hazardous and non- hazardous waste 
spills.  Reimburse the Government for costs incurred including sample analysis materials, clothing, equipment, and labor if the Government will initiate its own spill cleanup procedures, for MCMC –  responsible spills, when: Spill cleanup procedures have not 
begun within one hour of spill discovery/occurrence; or, in the Government’s judgment, spill cleanup is inadequate and the spill remains a threat to human health or the envir onment. 
 10.C.18.c  The MCMC shall maintain spill kits IAW MCSF -BI Integrated Contingency 
Plan.  The MCMC shall complete the Spill Kit Inventory Form (CDRL10054) and provide it to the government monthly.  
10.C.19 Regulated Garbage Inspection and Control  
  10.C.19.a   The MCMC shall inspect all garbage which includes waste material derived 
in whole or in part from fruits, vegetables, meats, or other plant or animal material  and other refuse of any character whatsoever that has been associated with such mat erial under 7 CFR 
330.400 and 9 CFR 94.5.  The regulated garbage shall be stored in a red, 96 -gallon, identifiable 
containers labeled “Regulated Garbage” for foreign garbage. The Installation Division 
Environmental USDA team will implement training programs for relevant personnel before they are permitted to handle or supervise the handling of regulated garbage annually or as needed along with the compliance agreement and training records.  The MCMC supervisor is responsible for inspections and possess know ledge of the ships layout. MCMC shall provide 
IDIV Environmental with records of regulated garbage pickups.  All regulated garbage shall be kept completely separate from trash.    
 
SECTION C.11 –  COMMUNICATION  SUPPORT SYSTEMS  
  This Section applies to land- based preposition sites established or will be established to 
additional OCONUS locations and sets forth the requirements and responsibilities of the MCMC for providing voice, video, and data services in support of MCSF -BI, BICmd and tenant 
organizations associated with the facility and the Marine Corps Prepositioning mission.  The Marine Corps operates a Governm ent Owned, Government Operated -  MCMC Supported 
(GOGO- CS) Enterprise Network.  It is setup within a regionalized strategy comprised of four 
Regional Network Operations Support Centers (RNOSCs) and eight sub regions supported by Marine Air Ground Task Force  (MAGTF) IT Centers (MITSCs) designed to provide garrison 
Marine Expeditionary Forces and Marine Corps Supporting Establishments within respective 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 297 of 425 
 
 areas of responsibility.  Under this construct, the MCMC shall provide touch labor in support of 
the MITSCs.  MITSC East is the region for this contract.  Overall NetOps/Cyber Network Operations (Cyber NetOps) are directed by the Marine Corps Cyber -Securit y Operations Group 
(MCCOG).  
 The MCMC shall install, configure, administer, operate, and maintain all video, and data services and equipment under the direction of the S -6, MITSC East and Higher Headquarters (HHQ).  
Under the MCEN, the MCMC shall be responsible for Managed services such as Data Center Support, Tech Refresh, Asset Management, Staging and Wa rehousing, and Software Release & 
Deploy.  The MCMC shall be responsible for Sustainment operations such as Network Support, IT Service Management, Data Center.  Equipment the MCMC shall install, configure, administer, operate, and maintain is listed below .  This list may not be all inclusive and will 
change as technology changes.  Network devices such as routers, switches (90), wireless Access Points (50), Passive Radio Frequency Identification (pRFID) readers (50) that are currently used at the facility. End User devices currently consist of Dell, HP, Panasonic, GETAC, DT, Lenovo and Microsoft workstations (1100). Video Teleconference equipment is primarily Cisco Tandberg systems (10). Servers  running virtual software  (75 physical/virtual servers) and Miscellaneous IT equipment includes but is not limited to leased Multifunction Devices (50), 
Raspberry Pi devices, standalone printers and plotters (20).  
 The Government has identified GFE as identified in SOW Attachment  C.1- 2 to include IT 
equipment.  The MCMC must receive Government approval prior to utilizing associated MCMC provided peripherals; Bluetooth and or wireless technologies are not permitted without prior Government approval.  Any unauthorized devices may be  confiscated without reimbursement.  
The Government has maintenance contracts in place which provide consumable supplies for 
Multi- Function Devices (MFD) and standalone printers (except paper ). 
 
Primary software and systems that the Contactor shall install, configure, administer, operate and maintain are listed below.  This list may not be all inclusive and will change as technology changes.  Microsoft Office products, Microsoft Server products (S harePoint, Project, SQL, etc.), 
Adobe Acrobat Professional, AutoCAD products, Citrix, iCODES, Oracle Database Enterprise, Sybase SQL Anywhere, Citrix, Stencil CAD Basic, and various graphic programs.  The MCMC is required to manage software licenses as def ined in Section 11.B.3.  
 
The MCMC shall ensure video, and data service delivery in accordance with standards defined in this section and all applicable Marine Corps Orders and directives, Department of Defense guidance and directives, Federal, State and Local laws and regulations,  industry standards, and 
other technical documentation.  Services at MCPP locations governed by the contract shall be performed in accordance with standards set forth and approved delivery schedules.  Products shall conform to the specifications described within this section.  Quality assurance

assets that are accounted for within GCSS -MC IAW UM 4000- 125 and CmdO 4790.2.   The

MCMC shall ensure all Task Notes are entered for each task created on the SR. The MCMC shall document all GCSS -MC actions performed in the Remedy IT Service Management Suite 
and reference the GCSS -MC Service Request.  
 11.C.2  The MCMC shall perform preventive maintenance annually on video and data service delivery equipment IAW Command Order (CmdO) 2620.1 and manufacturer’s technical manuals.  Hardware preventive maintenance procedures and requirements are contained in CmdO 2620.1.  The MCMC shall comply with the authorization procedures and maintenance windows as defined in CmdO 2620.1.  Service interruptions for maintenance procedures shall be scheduled and approved prior to execution.  
 11.C.3  The MCMC shall initiate troubleshooting and root cause analysis of video, and data 
service delivery hardware failures.  Failures occurring during working hours shall be recorded within 10 minutes of notification.  Failures occurring during non- work ing hours shall be 
recorded within 10 minutes of commencement of the next business day.  Distribution and server architecture monitoring shall be conducted continually during working hours.  Failures affecting distribution or server architecture shall be r eported to the S -6 immediately upon discovery (no 
later than five minutes).  
  
 11.C.3.a  The MCMC shall receive written authorization from the Government prior to 
procurement of components or services to execute repairs not covered by current Government maintenance procedures.

Software maintenance includes, but is not limited to, upgrades, Information Assurance Vulnerability Assessment (IAVA) implementation, Security Technical Implementation Guide (STIG) implementation, patches, firmware upgrades, and Operating System (OS) modifications and changes.  Telephone service delivery equipment (i.e. switch, server, etc.) are specifically included in Cyber Security monitoring, scanning, and software m aintenance operations.

Failures occurring during working hours are recorded within 10 minutes.  Failures occurring during non- working hours are recorded within 10 minute s of commencement 
of next business day.  Distribution and server architecture monitoring shall be conducted continually during working hours.  Failures affecting distribution or server architecture shall be reported to the S -6 immediately (no later than five minutes) upon discovery.  The MCMC shall

normally associated with "Domain Administration" in the IT industry.   11.E.1  The MCMC shall submit updates for the Blount Island Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) for IT services.  The Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) for IT is to ensure survivability of, and access to, mission critical data and technology in the eve nt of a disaster.  
Updates shall be submitted to the Cyber Security Manager for approval prior to implementation.  The COOP will be reviewed/updated at a minimal of once a calendar year.  
 11.E.2 The MCMC shall revise and maintain an IT services Backup Plan and perform back- up 
and restoration of all required data.  Monthly back up data shall be stored in a secured off -site 
facility as identified by the government.  Data that requires back- up is identified in the current 
revision of the COOP for IT services.  The MCMC shall ensure that Data is backed up on a daily, weekly and monthly basis.  Daily back up data shall be successfully restored within 30 minutes of restoration request.  Weekly bac k up data shall be successfully restored within 24 
hours of restoration request.  Monthly back up data shall be successfully restored within 48 hours of restoration request.

  MCPP MANAGEMENT SUPPORT SYSTEMS  
  This Section sets forth the requirements and responsibilities of the MCMC for providing voice, video, and data services in support of MCSF -BI, BICmd and tenant organizations associated 
with the facility and the Marine Corps Prepositioning mission. The Mari ne Corps operates a 
Government Owned, Government Operated -  MCMC Supported (GOGO -CS) Enterprise 
Network. It is setup within a regionalized strategy comprised of four Regional Network Operations Support Centers (RNOSCs) and eight sub regions supported by Ma rine Air Ground 
Task Force (MAGTF) IT Centers (MITSCs) designed to provide garrison Marine Expeditionary Forces and Marine Corps Supporting Establishments within respective areas of responsibility with four regional Network Battalions responsible for the I T transport equipment and services. 
Under this construct, the MCMC shall provide touch labor in support of the BICmd S6 and Regional Network Battalion. Overall NetOps/Cyber Network Operations (Cyber NetOps) are directed by the Marine Corps Cyber -Security O perations Group (MCCOG).  
  The MCMC shall install, configure, administer, operate, and maintain all voice, video, and data services and equipment under the direction of the S -6, Network Battalion and Higher 
Headquarters (HHQ). Under the MCEN, the MCMC shall be responsible for Manage d services 
such as Data Center Support, Tech Refresh, Asset Management, Staging and Warehousing, and Software Release & Deploy. The MCMC shall be responsible for sustainment operations such as Desktop Support, Network Support and IT Service Management. Equipment the MCMC shall install, configure, administer, operate, and maintain is listed below. This list may not be all inclusive and will change as technology changes. Network devices such as routers and switches (quantity to be location and mission depende nt), Wireless Access Points (quantity to be location 
and mission dependent), Passive Radio Frequency Identification (pRFID) readers (quantity to be location and mission dependent) will be utilized at the facility. End User devices currently consist of Dell , HP, Panasonic, DT Research, and Microsoft workstations (quantity to be location 
and mission dependent). Server types are IBM, and Dell running virtual software. Miscellaneous

(a) The Contractor shall comply with the higher -level quality standard(s) listed below.  
 The Contractor shall comply with the "American National Standards Institute/ International Organization for 
Standardization, ANSI/ISO, currently 9001:2015”, and CmdO 5000.11, which is hereby incorporated into this contract 
by reference.  
 The contractor shall be certified within nine months of contract award to the provisions of ANSI/ISO, 9001:2015 by a Government approved third party certification organization.  Additionally, the contractor shall employ quality control 
measures as described in ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 -2003, ANSI/ASQ Z1.9- 2003 and the Lean Six Sigma principles, concepts, 
and practices recognized as standards by the American Society of Quality.  
  
(b) The Contractor shall include applicable requirements of the higher -level quality standard(s) listed in paragraph 
(a) of this clause and the requirement to flow down such standards, as applicable, to lower -tier subcontracts, in -- 
     
(1) Any subcontract for critical and complex items (see 46.203(b) and (c)); or  
     
(2) When the technical requirements of a subcontract require -- 
     
(i) Control of such things as design, work operations, in- process control, testing, and inspection; or  
     
(ii) Attention to such factors as organization, planning, work instructions, documentation control, and advanced metrology.  
 (End of clause)  
 
 
 
 
252.211- 7003  ITEM UNIQUE IDENTIFICATION AND VALUATION (JAN 2023)  
 (a) Definitions. As used in this clause - 
 Automatic identification device means a device, such as a reader or interrogator, used to retrieve data encoded on 
machine- readable media.  
 
Concatenated unique item identifier means -- 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 309 of 425 
 
  
(1) For items that are serialized within the enterprise identifier, the linking together of the unique identifier data elements in order of the issuing agency code, enterprise identifier, and unique serial number within the enterprise 
identifier; or  
 
(2) For items that are serialized within the original part, lot, or batch number, the linking together of the unique identifier data elements in order of the issuing agency code; enterprise identifier; original part, lot, or batch number; and serial number  within the original part, lot, or batch number.  
 
Data Matrix means a two -dimensional matrix symbology, which is made up of square or, in some cases, round 
modules arranged within a perimeter finder pattern and uses the Error Checking and Correction 200 (ECC200) 
specification found within International Standards Organization (ISO)/International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) 16022.  
 Data qualifier means a specified character (or string of characters) that immediately precedes a data field that defines 
the general category or intended use of the data that follows.  
 
DoD recognized unique identification equivalent means a unique identification method that is in commercial use and has been recognized by DoD.  All DoD recognized unique identification equivalents are listed at https://www.acq.osd.mil/asda/dpc/ce/ds/unique -id.html
. 
 DoD item unique identification means a system of marking items delivered to DoD with unique item identifiers that have machine- readable data elements to distinguish an item from all other like and unlike items.  For items that are 
serialized within the enterprise identifier, the unique item identifier shall include the data elements of the enterprise identifier and a unique serial number.  For items that are serialized within the part, lot, or batch number within the enterprise identifier, the unique item identifier  shall include the data elements of the enterprise identifier; the original 
part, lot, or batch number; and the serial number.  
 Enterprise means the entity (e.g., a manufacturer or vendor) responsible for assigning unique item identifiers to items.  
 Enterprise identifier means a code that is uniquely assigned to an enterprise by an issuing agency.  
 Government's unit acquisition cost means -- 
 (1) For fixed -price type line, subline, or exhibit line items, the unit price identified in the contract at the time of 
delivery;  
 (2) For cost -type or undefinitized line, subline, or exhibit line items, the Contractor's estimated fully burdened unit 
cost to the Government at the time of delivery; and  
 (3) For items produced under a time -and-materials contract, the Contractor's estimated fully burdened unit cost to 
the Government at the time of delivery.  
 Issuing agency means an organization responsible for assigning a globally unique identifier to an enterprise, as indicated in the Register of Issuing Agency Codes for ISO/IEC 15459, located at http://www.aimglobal.org/?Reg_Authority15459
. 
 Issuing agency code means a code that designates the registration (or controlling) authority for the enterprise 
identifier.  
 
Item means a single hardware article or a single unit formed by a grouping of subassemblies, components, or constituent parts.  
 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 310 of 425 
 
 Lot or batch number means an identifying number assigned by the enterprise to a designated group of items, usually 
referred to as either a lot or a batch, all of which were manufactured under identical conditions.  
 
Machine- readable means an automatic identification technology media, such as bar codes, contact memory buttons, 
radio frequency identification, or optical memory cards.  
 
Original part number means a combination of numbers or letters assigned by the enterprise at item creation to a class of items with the same form, fit, function, and interface.  
 
Parent item means the item assembly, intermediate component, or subassembly that has an embedded item with a 
unique item identifier or DoD recognized unique identification equivalent.  
 Serial number within the enterprise identifier means a combination of numbers, letters, or symbols assigned by the enterprise to an item that provides for the differentiation of that item from any other like and unlike item and is never used again within t he enterprise. 
 Serial number within the part, lot, or batch number means a combination of numbers or letters assigned by the enterprise to an item that provides for the differentiation of that item from any other like item within a part, lot, or batch number assignment.  
 
Serialization within the enterprise identifier means each item produced is assigned a serial number that is unique 
among all the tangible items produced by the enterprise and is never used again. The enterprise is responsible for ensuring unique serializat ion within the enterprise identifier.  
 Serialization within the part, lot, or batch number means each item of a particular part, lot, or batch number is assigned a unique serial number within that part, lot, or batch number assignment. The enterprise is responsible for 
ensuring unique serializa tion within the part, lot, or batch number within the enterprise identifier.  
 
Type designation means a combination of letters and numerals assigned by the Government to a major end item, assembly or subassembly, as appropriate, to provide a convenient means of differentiating between items having the same basic name and to indicate modifications and changes thereto.  
 
Unique item identifier means a set of data elements marked on items that is globally unique and unambiguous. The 
term includes a concatenated unique item identifier or a DoD recognized unique identification equivalent.  
 Unique item identifier type means a designator to indicate which method of uniquely identifying a part has been used. The current list of accepted unique item identifier types is maintained at 
https://www.acq.osd.mil/asda/dpc/ce/ds/unique -id.html
.

(3) The unique item identifier component data elements shall be marked on an item using two dimensional data matrix symbology that complies with ISO/IEC International Standard 16022, Information  
technology--International symbology specification--Data matrix; ECC200 data matrix specification.  
     
(4) Data syntax and semantics of unique item identifiers. The Contractor shall ensure that -- 
     
(i) The data elements (except issuing agency code) of the unique item identifier are encoded within the data matrix symbol that is marked on the item using one of the following three types of data qualifiers, as determined by the 
Contractor:  
 
(A) Application Identifiers (AIs) (Format Indicator 05 of ISO/IEC International Standard 15434), in accordance with 
ISO/IEC International Standard 15418, Information Technology-- EAN/UCC Application Identifiers and Fact Data 
Identifiers and Maintenance and ANSI MH 10.8.2 Data Identifier and Application Identifier Standard.  
     
(B) Data Identifiers (DIs) (Format Indicator 06 of ISO/IEC International Standard 15434), in accordance with 
ISO/IEC International Standard 15418, Information Technology-- EAN/UCC Application Identifiers and Fact Data 
Identifiers and Maintenance and ANSI MH  10.8.2 Data Identifier and Application Identifier Standard.  
     
(C) Text Element Identifiers (TEIs) (Format Indicator 12 of ISO/IEC International Standard 15434), in accordance with the Air Transport Association Common Support Data Dictionary; and  
     
(ii) The encoded data elements of the unique item identifier conform to the transfer structure, syntax, and coding of 
messages and data formats specified for Format Indicators 05, 06, and 12 in ISO/IEC International Standard 15434, 
Information Technology -Transfer Syntax for High Capacity Automatic Data Capture Media.  
     
(5) Unique item identifier.  
     
(i) The Contractor shall --

(A) Determine whether to --

(B) Place the data elements of the unique item identifier (enterprise identifier; serial number; DoD recognized 
unique identification equivalent; and for serialization within the part, lot, or batch number only: Original part, lot, or 
batch number) on item s requiring marking by paragraph (c)(1) of this clause, based on the criteria provided in MIL -
STD -130, Identification Marking of U.S. Military Property, latest version;  
     
(C) Label shipments, storage containers and packages that contain uniquely identified items in accordance with the 
requirements of MIL -STD -129, Military Marking for Shipment and Storage, latest version; and  
     
(D) Verify that the marks on items and labels on shipments, storage containers, and packages are machine readable 
and conform to the applicable standards. The contractor shall use an automatic identification technology device for 
this verification that has  been programmed to the requirements of Appendix A, MIL -STD -130, latest version.  
     
(ii) The issuing agency code -- 
     
(A) Shall not be placed on the item; and  
     
(B) Shall be derived from the data qualifier for the enterprise identifier.  
 
(d) For each item that requires item unique identification under paragraph (c)(1)(i), (ii), or (iv) of this clause or when 
item unique identification is provided under paragraph (c)(1)(v), in addition to the information provided as part of the Material Inspection and Receiving Report specified elsewhere in this contract, the Contractor shall report at the time of delivery, as part of the Material Inspection and Receiving Report, the following information:  
 
(1) Unique item identifier.  
 
(2) Unique item identifier type.  
 
(3) Issuing agency code (if concatenated unique item identifier is used).  
 
(4) Enterprise identifier (if concatenated unique item identifier is used).  
 
(5) Original part number (if there is serialization within the original part number).  
 (6) Lot or batch number (if there is serialization within the lot or batch number).  
 (7) Current part number (optional and only if not the same as the original part number).  
 (8) Current part number effective date (optional and only if current part number is used).  
 
(9) Serial number (if concatenated unique item identifier is used).  
 (10) Government's unit acquisition cost.  
 (11) Unit of measure.

D.1 GENERAL PACKAGING REQUIREMENTS  
1. All supplies and materials furnished under this IDIQ contract shall be preserved, packaged, packed, and 
marked in accordance with the requirements of:  
o MIL-STD -129 (Military Marking for Shipment and Storage) for shipments to DoD facilities.  
o MIL-STD -2073- 1 (Standard Practice for Military Packaging) for items requiring military -grade 
protection.  
o Commercial packaging standards if specified in the task or delivery order.  
o Command Order  4035.1  
2. Packaging shall ensure that items remain serviceable while in transit, storage, and handling, and shall prevent deterioration due to environmental factors.  
D.2 PACKAGING SPECIFICATIONS  
1. Packaging requirements for each task or delivery order shall be defined in the order’s statement of work 
(SOW).  
2. Unless otherwise specified, packaging shall adhere to Level B or C standards based on the item's fragility 
and shipping method.  
3. Items shall be packed to prevent damage due to shock, vibration, moisture, static electricity, and rough handling.  
4. Containers shall be stackable and modular, where possible, to optimize storage and transportation.  
D.3 MARKING REQUIREMENTS  
Each package shall be clearly marked with the following:  
1. Required Markings on Each Package:  
o Contract Number: [Insert Contract Number]  
o Task/Delivery Order Number: [Insert TO/DO Number]  
o National Stock Number (NSN) or Manufacturer Part Number  
o Item Description and Quantity  
o Manufacturer’s Name and CAGE Code  
o Lot/Serial Number (if applicable)  
o Shelf -life or Expiration Date (if applicable)  
2. Military Shipping Label (MSL) Requirements (if applicable):  
o Required for shipments to DoD facilities per MIL -STD -129. 
o Label shall include linear and 2D barcodes for automated tracking.  
o RFID tagging shall be applied when required by the task/delivery order.  
3. Hazardous Materials Marking (if applicable):  
o Shipments containing hazardous materials must comply with:  
 49 CFR (DOT regulations) for ground transport.  
 IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations for air transport.  
 United Nations (UN) Hazardous Materials Classification System.  
o Proper hazard class labels, placards, and handling instructions shall be affixed as required.  
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 315 of 425 
 
 D.4 UNIQUE IDENTIFICATION (UID) MARKING  
1. Each UID -eligible item shall be marked per MIL -STD -130 with:  
o Data Matrix 2D barcode.  
o Unique Item Identifier (UII).  
o Contract Number, Lot Number, and Serial Number.  
2. UID marking requirements shall be specified in each task/delivery order where applicable.  
D.5 RFID TAGGING REQUIREMENTS  
1. Passive RFID tags shall be applied in accordance with DoD logistics guidance.  
2. Passive RFID tags shall be placed on the exterior of the shipping container or vehicle and comply with 
DoD Wide -Area Workflow (WAWF) electronic tracking requirements.  
D.6 EXPORT CONTROLLED ITEMS (if applicable)  
1. If items are subject to International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) or Export Administration Regulations (EAR), packages shall be marked:  
o “EXPORT CONTROLLED – ITAR RESTRICTED”  
o “AUTHORIZED U.S. GOVERNMENT PERSONNEL ONLY”  
2. The contractor shall ensure proper recordkeeping and shipment tracking for export -controlled items.  
D.7 DELIVERY, INSPECTION, AND ACCEPTANCE  
1. Items shall be packaged for easy access to allow government inspection upon receipt.  
2. Each shipment shall contain a packing list inside the primary shipping container, including:  
o Contract Number and Delivery/Task Order Number.  
o Itemized list of shipped products with quantities and serial numbers (if applicable).  
o Any required safety data sheets (SDS) for hazardous materials.  
3. The Government reserves the right to reject improperly marked or inadequately packaged items at the 
contractor’s expense.

a Government approved third party certification organization.  Additionally, the contractor shall employ quality control 
measures as described in ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 -2003,  ANSI/ASQ Z1.9 -2003 and the Lean Six Sigma principles, concepts, 
and practices recognized as standards by the American Society of Quality.  
 
(b) The Contractor shall include applicable requirements of the higher -level quality standard(s) listed in paragraph 
(a) of this clause and the requirement to flow down such standards, as applicable, to lower -tier subcontracts, in -- 
     
(1) Any subcontract for critical and complex items (see 46.203(b) and (c)); or  
     
(2) When the technical requirements of a subcontract require -- 
     
(i) Control of such things as design, work operations, in- process control, testing, and inspection; or  
     
(ii) Attention to such factors as organization, planning, work instructions, documentation control, and advanced 
metrology.  
 
(End of clause)  
   
E-1 INSPECTION/MONITORING OF PERFORMANCE  
 
(a) Inspection and monitoring of services performed under this contract will be conducted to verify 
accomplishment of work in compliance with the Statement of Work and all applicable publications, adherence to established MCMC processes, to determine timel iness of actions taken, and to ascertain quality of the efforts.  The 
goal of contract performance and the inspections is to attain 100% of required supplies and equipment, maintain them 
at 100% fully mission capable status and to ensure 100% data accuracy  is achieved at the levels described in the SOW.  
Technical standards for maintenance preservation and inspection will be those contained in the SOW and equipment 
technical manuals.  
 (b) In addition to inspection and monitoring by the Government, inspection and monitoring of services shall be 
performed by the MCMC in accordance with his approved quality control plan.  The MCMC's Quality Control Plan 
must align with the BICmd Risk and Performa nce Manual (CmdO P5000.11) to comply with ISO Joint Certification 
requirements.  Documentation associated with MCMC Quality Control  shall be available to Government Quality 
Assurance (QA) personnel upon request.  The MCMC shall use the Government provided Quality Management 
Support System, to document all MCMC inspection and monitoring of services as identified in the approved quality 
control plan.  
 
(c) Inspection and monitoring of services under this contract may be accomplished by any of the following 
Government personnel:  
 
 (1) The Contracting Officer and/or assigned Contracting Officer's Representative (COR).  
 
 (2) Quality Assurance Representatives (QAR) personnel responsible for the  inspection and monitoring of 
equipment, supplies, or processes.  
 
 (3) Personnel who inspect equipment as an adjunct to their primary task of equipment employment.  
 
M6700425R0007  
DRAFT  RFP 
 
Page 319 of 425 
 
  (4) Cognizant personnel at Marine Corps Logistics Command and Blount Island Command charged with the 
responsibility for the MPS Program, MCPP -N and MCPP -PHIL.  
 (d) Government personnel will inspect and monitor through:  
 
 (1) Examination and employment of equipment and supplies,  
  (2) Reviewing records of maintenance actions taken,  
 
 (3) Reviewing written reports from the MCMC  
 
 (4) Reviewing the maintenance, supply or other Government approved records, and  
  (5) Monitoring established MCMC processes.  
 
(e) The extent of Government Verification inspection will be determined by the Government.  Such inspections will 
normally not exceed the definitive quality assurance requirements established in the SOW, specifications, and/or 
contract; however, the Government reserves the right to perform any amount of inspection (including 100% inspection), when it determines such inspection is warranted.  
 
(f) At the option of the Government, Government inspection may be performed concurrently with the MCMC, or 
independently on samples selected by the Government representative.  When independent Government inspection is 
performed, the MCMC shall ensure that adequate test equipment is available to perform the inspection.  
 
(g) Inspection or monitoring by the Government may be accomplished at any time convenient to the Government.  
 
(h) All MCMC generated quality records shall be available for review by Government personnel via QMSS at all 
times.  
 
(i) Discrepancies noted by any of the above personnel will be brought to the attention of the MCMC.  All official 
notification of discrepancies shall be in writing from either the Contracting Officer, COR, or personnel duly assigned 
by the Contracting Offi cer.  Any official notice of unsatisfactory performance shall be in writing from the Contracting 
Officer.  
 (j) In addition to the foregoing inspections, inspection of the methods of performing the services under this contract 
may be performed by the Master or Officers of a prepositioning ship to assure conformance with applicable maritime 
laws, rules, and regul ations relating to the performance of such activities on board ships and to ensure the general 
safety and sea- worthiness of the ship.  Any shipboard MCMC activity or condition found by the Master of a ship to 
be in violation of, or not in consonance with, any of the foregoing may be brought to the attention of the COR by the Master of the vessel or one of her Officers on his behalf.  If the activity or condition is not discontinued or corrected as directed by the Master within such time period and in such m anner as shall be acceptable to the Master, the Master 
may issue a written notice to the COR with a copy to the MCMC; such notice may be the basis of removal from the vessel as  expeditiously as possible of the employee(s) responsible for the failure to comply with the Master's 
instructions.  
 
(k) Differences in interpretation of technical issues or the inspection reports will be brought to the attention of the 
COR for resolution.  Issues not resolved at this level will be forwarded to the Contracting Officer for final 
determination.  
 
(l) The Government reserves the right to participate in MCMC ISO 9001:2015 internal audits or perform 
independent ISO 9001:2015 external audits.
